items.description: This Order provides for the winding-up of the Greater Manchester Residuary Body in pursuance of a scheme submitted by that Body to the Secretary of State under section 67 of the Local Government Act 1985.

items.title: The Social Security (Claims and Payments and Payments on account, Overpayments and Recovery) Amendment Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations amend the Social Security (Claims and Payments) Regulations 1987 (the Claims and Payments Regulations), the Social Security (Payments on account, Overpayments and Recovery) Regulations 1988 (the Payments on account, Overpayments and Recovery Regulations) and the Social Security (Common Provisions) Regulations 1988 (the Common Provisions Regulations).

items.title: The National Health Service (General Medical and Pharmaceutical Services) Amendment Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations further amend the National Health Service (General Medical and Pharmaceutical Services) Regulations 1974 (“the principal Regulations”) which govern the arrangements to be made by Family Practitioner Committees for the provision for their locality of general medical services and pharmaceutical services.

items.title: The Prevention of Terrorism (Temporary Provisions) Act 1989 (Commencement No. 1) Order 1989
items.description: Schedule 3 to the Prevention of Terrorism (Temporary Provisions) Act 1989 provides for the supervision of the exercise of powers of detention and examination under the Act. (These powers came into force on 22nd March 1989, by virtue of section 27 of the Act.) This Order brings the Schedule into force on 1st September 1989 in relation to cases of detention or further examination arising on or after that date.

items.title: The Social Security (Attendance Allowance)Amendment Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations amend the Social Security (Attendance Allowance) (No. 2) Regulations 1975 (“the Attendance Allowance Regulations”). Regulation 2 of these Regulations amends regulation 5D of the Attendance Allowance Regulations to take account of the change in wording made to section 35(4A) of the Social Security Act 1975 by Schedule 8 to the Social Security Act 1989. The effect of the amended regulation is to provide, as before, that where a fresh claim is made within six months of the expiry of an Attendance Allowance Board certificate, an allowance may be made for the intervening period.

items.title: The Annual Close Time (Lossie Salmon Fishery District) Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The Annual Close Time (Lossie Salmon Fishery District) Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Central Regional Council (The Stirlingshire and Falkirk Water Order 1957 Amendment) Water Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The Central Regional Council (The Stirlingshire and Falkirk Water Order 1957 Amendment) Water Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Scottish Land Court (Fees) Order 1989
items.description: This Order prescribes the fees payable in respect of applications to the Scottish Land Court following its designation as a Scottish Court for the purposes of section 2 of the Courts of Law Fees (Scotland) Act 1895.

items.title: The Scottish Land Court Order 1989
items.description: This Order specifies the Scottish Land Court as a Scottish Court for the purposes of section 2 of the Courts of Law Fees (Scotland) Act 1895. In effect this transfers the power to set fees in the Scottish Land Court from the Land Court members to the Secretary of State, with the consent of the Treasury.

items.title: The Scottish Land Court Amendment Rules 1989
items.description: These Rules revoke Appendix 1 of the Scottish Land Court Rules 1979 which previously set out the fees payable in respect of proceedings before the Scottish Land Court. Fees are now prescribed by the Scottish Land Court (Fees) Order 1989 (SI 1989/1365) following designation of the Land Court as a Scottish Court for the purposes of section 2 of the Courts of Law Fees (Scotland) Act 1895. The Rules provide for references to the Table of Court Fees in the Scottish Land Court Rules 1979 to be construed as references to the Fees now prescribed in the Scottish Land Court (Fees) Order 1989.

items.title: The Excise Duties (Deferred Payment) (Amendment) Regulations 1989
items.description: Under the Cider and Perry Regulations 1989 (S.I. 1989/1355), cider and perry produced in the United Kingdom may be deposited in an excise warehouse for subsequent delivery for home use.

items.title: The Electricity Act 1989 (Commencement No.1) Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The Electricity Act 1989 (Commencement No.1) Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Home Purchase Assistance (Price-limits) Order 1989
items.description: Assistance for first-time purchasers of house property, under section 446 of the Housing Act 1985 in England and Wales and under section 223 of the Housing (Scotland) Act 1987 in Scotland, may take the form of a bonus of up to £110 on savings and a loan of £600 additional to that which the lending institution would otherwise have made and free of any obligation to repay principal or interest for up to five years. Assistance is only available where the purchase price of the property is within the limit prescribed by the Secretary of State. This Order prescribes limits for different areas which are higher than those prescribed by the Home Purchase Assistance (Price-limits) Order 1988, which is revoked.

items.title: The Electricity Supply (Scientists) Superannuation Scheme (Winding Up) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations provide for the winding up of the Electricity Supply (Scientists) Superannuation Scheme which provided pension benefits for scientists employed by Electricity Boards.

items.title: The Community Charges (Cross-Border Information) Regulations 1989
items.description: Community charges registration officers in Scotland, and those in England and Wales, are under a duty to compile and maintain community charges registers under Part II of the Abolition of Domestic Rates Etc. (Scotland) Act 1987 and Part I of the Local Government Finance Act 1988 respectively.

items.title: The Motor Cars (Driving Instruction) (Amendment) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Motor Cars (Driving Instruction) Regulations 1977.

items.title: The Recovery Vehicles (Prescribed Purposes) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations revoke and reenact the provisions of the Recovery Vehicles (Prescribed Purposes) Regulations 1987. In addition they also prescribe four purposes for which recovery vehicles may be used, in addition to those specified in paragraph 8(2) of Part I of Schedule 3 to the 1971 Act, for the purposes of that provision.

items.title: The Recovery Vehicles (Prescribed Purposes) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1989
items.description: These Regulations revoke and reenact the provisions of the Recovery Vehicles (Prescribed Purposes) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1987. In addition they also prescribe four purposes for which recovery vehicles may be used, in addition to those specified in paragraph 8(2) of Part I of Schedule 3 to the 1972 Act, for the purposes of that provision.

items.title: The Rivers (Prevention of Pollution) Act 1951 (Continuation of Byelaws) Order 1989
items.description: Section 114 of the Water Act 1989 enables the National Rivers Authority to make byelaws for preventing pollution of controlled waters, including byelaws concerning the keeping or use on any controlled waters of vessels provided with sanitary appliances.

items.title: The Water and Sewerage (Works) (Advance Payments) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations make provision for advance payment on account of compensation payable by a water undertaker, a sewage undertaker or the National Rivers Authority when it exercises powers conferred by paragraphs 4 and 5 of Schedule 19 to the Water Act 1989 (powers to carry out work other than street works and powers in relation to stopcocks).

items.title: The Local Government Act 1988 (Defined Activities) (Competition) (Wales) (Variation) Regulations 1989
items.description: Regulation 3(3) of the Local Government Act 1988 (Defined Activities) (Competition) (Wales) Regulations 1988 requires county councils who wish to carry out refuse collection work to compete for such work. Regulation 3(4) of the Regulations requires district councils who wish to carry out school and welfare catering work to compete for such work. It was intended that these provisions should apply only to work arising in Wales. These Variation Regulations vary the original Regulations so as to expressly provide for this.

items.title: The Water (Local Statutory Provisions) (Consequential Amendments) Order 1989
items.description: This Order is consequential upon the Water Act 1989. It substitutes for references in local statutory provisions to water authorities, references to the relevant water undertaker or sewerage undertaker, as the case may be, where the provision in question relates to functions, property, rights or liabilities which are transferred to the undertaker by or under the 1989 Act (article 3(1)). Where functions, property, rights or liabilities are transferred to the National Rivers Authority, references in local statutory provisions to water authorities are replaced by references to that Authority (article 3(2)).

items.title: The Water Reorganisation (Pensions etc.) (Amendment) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations amend the Water Reorganisation (Pensions etc.) Regulations 1989.

items.title: The Water Reorganisation (Pensions etc.) (Designated Persons) (Amendment) Order 1989
items.description: This Order amends the Water Reorganisation (Pensions etc.) (Designated Persons) Order 1989 by substituting for the reference in article 2(2)(a) to British Water International Limited a reference to Water International Limited. British Water International Limited changed its name to Water International Limited on 22nd April 1988.

items.title: The Water Supply and Sewerage Services (Customer Service Standards) (Amendment) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations amend the Water Supply and Sewerage Services (Customer Service Standards) Regulations 1989.

items.title: The Water Supply (Water Quality) (Amendment) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations amend the Water Supply (Water Quality) Regulations 1989.

items.title: The (A6) London–Inverness Trunk Road (Quorn–Mountsorrel Bypass and Slip Roads) Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The (A6) London–Inverness Trunk Road (Quorn–Mountsorrel Bypass and Slip Roads) Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The (A6) London–Inverness Trunk Road (Quorn–Mountsorrel Bypass De-Trunking) Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The (A6) London–Inverness Trunk Road (Quorn–Mountsorrel Bypass De-Trunking) Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Black Country Development Corporation (Vesting of Land) (Borough of Walsall) Order 1989
items.description: This Order vests in the Black Country Development Corporation certain land which is at present vested in the council of the borough of Walsall (or in another body mentioned in section 141(1) of the Local Government, Planning and Land Act 1980), and related easements and similar rights. The land is shown coloured black on the map forming part of this note.

items.title: The Diseases of Animals (Protein Processing) (Amendment) Order 1989
items.description: This Order amends the Diseases of Animals (Protein Processing) Order 1981 (“the 1981 Order”). It enables a veterinary inspector by notice to prohibit, except under licence, the removal of any processed animal protein off premises used for processing animal protein where the test of a sample carried out under the 1981 Order shows that the sample does not conform with the required bacteriological standard as defined in the 1981 Order (article 2).

items.title: The Black Country Development Corporation (Vesting of Land) (General) Order 1989
items.description: This Order vests in the Black Country Development Corporation certain land which is at present vested in the councils of the boroughs of Sandwell and Wolverhampton, the British Waterways Board, the Severn-Trent Water Authority, the West Midlands Residuary Body (or in another body mentioned in Section 141(1) of the Local Government, Planning and Land Act 1980), and related easements and similar rights.

items.title: The London Docklands Development Corporation (Vesting of Land) (Port of London Authority and London Borough of Newham) Order 1989
items.description: This Order vests in the London Docklands Development Corporation certain land which is at present vested in the Port of London Authority, the council of the London borough of Newham (or in another body mentioned in section 141(1) of the Local Government, Planning and Land Act 1980), and related easements and similar rights. The land is shown coloured black on the map forming part of this note.

items.title: The London Docklands Development Corporation (Vesting of Land) (Thames Water Authority) Order 1989
items.description: This Order vests in the London Docklands Development Corporation certain land which is at present vested in the Thames Water Authority (or in another body mentioned in section 141(1) of the Local Government, Planning and Land Act 1980), and related easements and similar rights. The land is shown coloured black on the map forming part of this note.

items.title: The Merseyside Development Corporation (Vesting of Land) (Transport Land) Order 1989
items.description: This Order vests in the Merseyside Development Corporation certain land which is at present vested in the British Railways Board, the Merseyside Passenger Transport Executive, Merseyside Transport Limited (or in another body mentioned in section 141(1) of the Local Government, Planning and Land Act 1980), and related easements and similar rights. The land is shown coloured black on the map forming part of this note.

items.title: The Sheffield Development Corporation (Vesting of Land) (British Railways Board) Order 1989
items.description: This Order vests in the Sheffield Development Corporation certain land which is at present vested in the British Railways Board (or in another body mentioned in section 141(1) of the Local Government, Planning and Land Act 1980), and related easements and similar rights. The land is shown coloured black on the map forming part of this note.

items.title: The Black Country Development Corporation (Vesting of Land) (British Railways Board) Order 1989
items.description: This Order vests in the Black Country Development Corporation certain land which is at present vested in the British Railways Board (or in another body mentioned in section 141(1) of the Local Government, Planning and Land Act 1980), and related easements and similar rights. The land is shown coloured black on the map forming part of this note.

items.title: The Black Country Development Corporation (Vesting of Land) (Central Electricity Generating Board) Order 1989
items.description: This Order vests in the Black Country Development Corporation certain land which is at present vested in the Central Electricity Generating Board (or in another body mentioned in section 141(1) of the Local Government, Planning and Land Act 1980), and related easements and similar rights.

items.title: The Sandwell Borough Council (Holloway Bank Canal Bridge) Scheme 1988 Confirmation Instrument 1989
items.description: This version of the The Sandwell Borough Council (Holloway Bank Canal Bridge) Scheme 1988 Confirmation Instrument 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The West of Maidenhead–Oxford Trunk Road (Crowmarsh Hill Realignment) Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The West of Maidenhead–Oxford Trunk Road (Crowmarsh Hill Realignment) Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Local Government Reorganisation (Property, etc.) (West Midlands) Order 1989
items.description: This Order transfers property, rights and liabilities formerly vested in the West Midlands County Council to the councils of districts in that county. The transfers effected are in addition to those affected by S.I. 1987/1077 and S. I. 1988/783.

items.title: The Agricultural Holdings (Units of Production) Order 1989
items.description: This Order prescribes units of production for the assessment of the productive capacity of agricultural land and sets out the amount which is to be regarded as the net annual income from each such unit for the year 12th September 1989 to 11th September 1990 inclusive. In so far as the units of production prescribed in the Order are relevant to the assessment they are to be used, in conjunction with the net annual income ascribed to them, in calculating the net annual income of the unit of land when assessing whether it is a commercial unit of agricultural land for the puposes of the succession provisions in Part IV of the Agricultural Holdings Act 1986, that is to say a unit of agricultural land which is capable, when farmed under competent management, of producing a net annual income not less than the aggregate of the average annual earnings of two full-time, male agricultural workers aged 20 or over, (see paragraph 3(1) of Schedule 6 to the Act). Therefore the assessment will not necessarily be based on the system of farming carried out on the holding or on historical data from the holding.

items.title: The Fire Precautions (Sub-surface Railway Stations) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations provide for fire precautions to be taken in certain underground and low level railway stations used by members of the public. The railway stations to which the Regulations apply are described in regulation 3 by reference to the station having a railway platform which is wholly or mainly enclosed in a tunnel or building at a level below ground level.

items.title: The Dartford–Thurrock Crossing Tolls Order 1989
items.description: This Order, which comes into force on 1st September 1989, fixes the amounts of the tolls to be paid to use the Dartford–Thurrock Crossing, presently consisting of the Dartford Tunnels.

items.title: The Leeds Development Corporation (Vesting of Land) (British Railways Board) Order 1989
items.description: This Order vests in the Leeds Development Corporation certain land which is at present vested in the British Railways Board (or in another body mentioned in section 141(1) of the Local Government, Planning and Land Act 1980), and related easements and similar rights. The land is shown coloured black on the map forming part of this note.

items.title: The Leeds Development Corporation (Vesting of Land) (General) Order 1989
items.description: This Order vests in the Leeds Development Corporation certain land which is at present vested in the council of the city of Leeds, the British Waterways Board, the West Yorkshire Residuary Body (or in another body mentioned in section 141(1) of the Local Government, Planning and Land Act 1980), and related easements and similar rights. The land is shown coloured black on the map forming part of this note.

items.title: The Tyne and Wear Development Corporation (Vesting of Land) (Port of Tyne Authority and British Railways Board) Order 1989
items.description: This Order vests in the Tyne and Wear Development Corporation certain land which is at present vested in the Port of Tyne Authority and the British Railways Board (or in another body mentioned in section 141(1) of the Local Government, Planning and Land Act 1980), and related easements and similar rights.

items.title: The Tyne and Wear Development Corporation (Vesting of Land) (Various Local Authorities) Order 1989
items.description: This Order vests in the Tyne and Wear Development Corporation certain land which is at present vested in the councils of the city of Newcastle upon Tyne, the boroughs of North Tyneside, South Tyneside and Sunderland (or in another body mentioned in section 141(1) of the Local Government, Planning and Land Act 1980), and related easements and similar rights. The land is shown coloured black on the map forming part of this note.

items.title: The Port of Tyne (Pilotage) Harbour Revision Order 1989
items.description: By virtue of section 7(5) of the Pilotage Act 1987 a direction given by a harbour authority for the purpose of making pilotage compulsory for ships navigating in a specified area shall not apply to any area which is outside the authority’s harbour unless the limits of jurisdiction of the authority for pilotage purposes have been extended to cover that area.

items.title: The Babies' Dummies (Safety) (Revocation) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations revoke the Babies' Dummies (Safety) Regulations 1978 and the Babies' Dummies (Safety) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1978 which imposed requirements relating to the safety of babies' dummies. The requirements were concerned with the materials and construction of dummies, their resistance to damage and their packaging and the instructions to be given with them for their safe use. The Regulations which are revoked required compliance with clauses 4–7 of the British Standard Specification for babies' dummies: BS 5239/1975.

items.title: The Severn-Trent Water Authority (Bradmer Hill and Budby Boreholes) Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The Severn-Trent Water Authority (Bradmer Hill and Budby Boreholes) Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Cod (Specified Sea Areas) (Prohibition of Fishing) Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The Cod (Specified Sea Areas) (Prohibition of Fishing) Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Rent Officers (Additional Functions) (Amendment) Order 1989
items.description: This Order amends the Rent Officers (Additional Functions) Order 1989 which provided in certain cases for the rent officer to make determinations, or re-determinations, relating to a tenancy or licence of a dwelling with reference to the time the application for the determination was made. Article 2(2) and (3) amends paragraphs 1 and 2 of Schedule 1 so that the relevant time under those paragraphs (or those paragraphs as applied for re-determinations by Schedule 4) will be the time the tenancy or licence ended if that was before the application for the determination was made. The amendment made by article 2(4) stops the provision of meals being treated as the provision of services for the purposes of determinations and re-determinations under paragraph 3 of Schedule 1 (or that paragraph as applied by Schedule 4). Article 2(5) amends paragraph 1 of Schedule 2 (tenancy or licence excluded because of recent earlier determination) to cover the case where a tenancy or licence ended before the earlier determination was made. Article 2(1) and (6) alters the size criteria (for an occupier who is at least 16, but under 18, years old) in Schedule 3 which is relevant for determinations and re-determinations under paragraph 2 of Schedule 1 (or that paragraph as applied by Schedule 4).

items.title: The London-Brighton Trunk Road (A23 Dale Hill Slip Roads) (No 2) Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The London-Brighton Trunk Road (A23 Dale Hill Slip Roads) (No 2) Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The London — Brighton Trunk Road (A23 Hickstead Slip Roads) (No 2) Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The London — Brighton Trunk Road (A23 Hickstead Slip Roads) (No 2) Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The London–Brighton Trunk Road (A23 Warninglid Flyover–South of Bolney Slip Roads) Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The London–Brighton Trunk Road (A23 Warninglid Flyover–South of Bolney Slip Roads) Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The London-Brighton Trunk Road (Bolney De-Trunking) Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The London-Brighton Trunk Road (Bolney De-Trunking) Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Levens Bridge to Carlisle Trunk Road (A595 Egremont Bypass) (Detrunking) Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The Levens Bridge to Carlisle Trunk Road (A595 Egremont Bypass) (Detrunking) Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Levens Bridge to Carlisle Trunk Road (A595 Egremont Bypass) Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The Levens Bridge to Carlisle Trunk Road (A595 Egremont Bypass) Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Authorised Unit Trust Scheme (Investment and Borrowing Powers) (Amendment) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations amend the Authorised Unit Trust Scheme (Investment and Borrowing Powers) Regulations 1988 (“the Principal Regulations”) in various respects. They amend the definitions of “approved market” and “approved securities” so as to deal more clearly with the position of the Official List of a member State and with the markets upon which shares admitted to the Official List are dealt in or traded. They make new provision as to circumstances in which the manager of a scheme must pay sums into the property of a scheme following an acquisition or disposal of units in another scheme. They make provision as to the ability of an authorised securities scheme or a money market fund to continue to hold investments convertible into other investments. They also make provision as to the ability of an authorised securities scheme which is not a Government and other public securities fund to invest in Government and other public securities. They add the markets specified in regulation 7(1) to the list of markets specified in Part I of Schedule 1 to the Principal Regulations, the markets specified in regulation 7(2) to the list of markets specified in Part II of that Schedule and the market specified in paragraph 7(3) to the list of markets specified in Schedule 2 to those Regulations.

items.title: The Air Navigation (Restriction of Flying) (Gruinard Island) (Revocation) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations revoke the restrictions on flying in specified airspace around Gruinard Island, Ross and Cromarty.

items.title: The Levens Bridge–Carlisle Trunk Road (A596 Wigton Bypass) (Detrunking) Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The Levens Bridge–Carlisle Trunk Road (A596 Wigton Bypass) (Detrunking) Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Diseases of Animals (Approved Disinfectants) (Amendment) Order 1989
items.description: This Order amends the Diseases of Animals (Approved Disinfectants) Order 1978 by substituting new schedules for Schedules 1 and 2 to that Order (article 2(a) and (b)). Schedule 1, which lists approved disinfectants, includes newly approved disinfectants, and Schedule 2 lists disinfectants which are now omitted from Schedule 1 but which may nevertheless continue to be used as approved disinfectants until 30th June 1989 (article 2(c)).

items.title: The Levens Bridge–Carlisle Trunk Road (A596 Wigton Bypass) Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The Levens Bridge–Carlisle Trunk Road (A596 Wigton Bypass) Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Rent Officers (Additional Functions) (Scotland) Amendment Order 1989
items.description: (This Note is not part of the Order)

items.title: The Inverclyde Enterprise Zones Designation Order 1989
items.description: This Order designates as enterprise zones the areas named in the Inverclyde Enterprise Zones Scheme as Site No. 1 (Kelburn (Reclamation)), Site No. 2 (Kelburn), Site No. 3 (Gourock Ropeworks), Site No. 4 (Ardgowan Street), Site No. 5 (James Watt Dock/Garvel Island), Site No. 6 (Cartsburn), Site No. 7 (Baker Street), Site No. 8 (East India and Victoria Harbours), Site No. 9 (Larkfield Platform), Site No. 10 (Spango) and Site No. 11 (Faulds), which Scheme was adopted by Inverclyde District Council on 27th October 1988.

items.title: The Education (Bursaries for Teacher Training) (Amendment) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations amend the Education (Bursaries for Teacher Training) Regulations 1988 to empower the Secretary of State to pay training bursaries in respect of courses of initial teacher training which begin on or after 1st September 1989 and of which the principal subject matter is chemistry. The Regulations also substitute for references to the Education (Teachers) Regulations 1982 references to the Education (Teachers) Regulations 1989, which come into force on 1st September 1989.

items.title: The Education (Further and Higher Education Funding Schemes) (Publication and Information) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations (which apply to local education authorities in England only) make provision for the publication of local education authorities' schemes for the planning and funding of locally funded further and higher education, and for the publication of information about them.

items.title: The Education (School Teachers' Pay and Conditions) (Amendment) Order 1989
items.description: This Order amends the School Teachers' Pay and Conditions Document 1989 to take account of the provisions of the Education (Teachers) Regulations 1989 and to give effect to recommendations of the Interim Advisory Committee on School Teachers' Pay and Conditions relating to licensed teachers and to the retrospective award of qualified teacher status which could not be implemented until those Regulations came into force. Both the Order and the Regulations come into force on 1st September 1989.

items.title: The Nurses, Midwives and Health Visitors (Parts of the Register) Amendment (No. 2) Order 1989
items.description: This Order further amends the Nurses, Midwives and Health Visitors (Parts of the Register) Order 1983 which specifies the various parts of the register of qualified nurses, midwives and health visitors. Article 2 of this Order amends Schedule 1 to that Order by adding to the register a further four parts to accommodate four additional categories of nurses, namely those who are qualified following a course of preparation in, respectively, adult nursing, mental health nursing, mental handicap nursing or children’s nursing.

items.title: The Nurses, Midwives and Health Visitors (Registered Fever Nurses Amendment Rules and Training Amendment Rules) Approval Order 1989
items.description: The Rules approved by this Order, and made by the United Kingdom Central Council for Nursing, Midwifery and Health Visiting, further amend the Rules approved by the Nurses, Midwives and Health Visitors Rules Approval Order 1983 (“the principal Rules”).

items.title: The Reporting of Injuries, Diseases and Dangerous Occurrences (Amendment) Regulations 1989
items.description: This version of the The Reporting of Injuries, Diseases and Dangerous Occurrences (Amendment) Regulations 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Education (Mandatory Awards) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations, which come into force on the 1st September 1989, supersede the Education (Mandatory Awards) Regulations 1988 which are revoked (Regulation 6(1).)

items.title: The Industrial Training Levy (Road Transport) Order 1989
items.description: This Order, which comes into force on 1st August 1990, gives effect to proposals of the Road Transport Industry Training Board which were submitted to and approved by the Training Commission, and thereafter submitted by the Training Commission to the Secretary of State. The proposals are for the imposition of a levy on employers in the road transport industry for the purpose of raising money towards meeting the expenses of the Board.

items.title: The Assured Tenancies and Agricultural Occupancies (Forms) (Amendment) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations correct an error in the Assured Tenancies and Agricultural Occupancies (Forms) Regulations 1988. The effect of the correction is to prescribe form no. 5 in the Schedule to those Regulations for a notice under section 13(2) of the Housing Act 1988 proposing a new rent for an assured agricultural occupancy (as well as for an assured tenancy).

items.title: The Misuse of Drugs (Amendment) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations amend the Misuse of Drugs Regulations 1985 by adding, to Schedules 3 and 4 respectively, Buprenorphine and Pemoline which become subject to control under the Misuse of Drugs Act 1971 by virtue of the Misuse of Drugs Act 1971 (Modification) Order 1989 (S.I. 1989/1340).

items.title: The Companies Act 1985 (Modifications for Statutory Water Companies) Regulations 1989
items.description: Paragraph 71(2) of Schedule 25 to the Water Act 1989 provides that Part XIII of the Companies Act 1985 (arrangements and reconstructions) shall have effect in relation to statutory water companies that are not limited companies with prescribed modifications. These Regulations specify the modifications which have the effect of applying only sections 425 and 426 of Part XIII.

items.title: The Local Government Superannuation (Water) Regulations 1989
items.description: (This Note does not form part of the Regulations)

items.title: The Colyton Grammar School (Designation of Staff) Order 1989
items.description: Section 75 of the Education Reform Act 1988 provides that the contract of employment of any person who is employed by the local education authority to work solely at a school immediately before the school acquires grant-maintained status, shall have effect from the date of acquisition as if made between the employee and the governing body of the grant-maintained school.

items.title: The Water Authorities (Successor Companies) Order 1989
items.description: The Water Act 1989 provides for the appointment of successor companies wholly owned by the Crown to take over the functions of the water authorities established in England and Wales by the Water Act 1973 (c. 37) so far as they relate to the supply of water and the provision of sewerage services. The 1989 Act further provides that their regulatory functions shall vest in a new National Rivers Authority created by the Act.

items.title: The Education (Adult Education Centres Funding Schemes) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations permit local education authorities to bring adult education centres maintained or assisted by them within the scope of their further and higher education funding schemes so long as they are treated by the local education authority as separate institutions for the purposes of financing (whether or not they occupy premises of their own) and have their own principal or administrative head.

items.title: The Education (Grant-maintained Schools) (Publication of Proposals) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations contain provisions about the publication of proposals relating to grant-maintained schools.

items.title: The Town and Country Planning (Use Classes) (Scotland) Order 1989
items.description: This Order revokes and replaces the Town and Country Planning (Use Classes) (Scotland) Order 1973 as amended by the Town and Country Planning (Use Classes) (Scotland) (Amendment) Order 1983.

items.title: The Education (Proposed Further and Higher Education Institutions) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations make provision for the government of proposed institutions of further or higher education intended to be established and maintained by a local education authority (“LEA”).

items.title: The A23 Trunk Road (Brighton Road, Croydon) (Prescribed Routes) Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The A23 Trunk Road (Brighton Road, Croydon) (Prescribed Routes) Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Social Security (Contribution Conditions for Unemployment and Sickness Benefit) Transitional Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations make savings provisions in connection with the commencement on 2nd October 1988 of section 6 of the Social Security Act 1988 which relates to contribution conditions for unemployment and sickness benefit. Where a person has a day of unemployment or incapacity for work and that day falls within a period of interruption of employment which began before 1st January 1978, the contribution conditions for unemployment benefit and sickness benefit in his case are those which applied prior to the changes introduced by section 6.

items.title: The Car Tax (Amendment) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations which come into force on 8th September 1989 amend the Car Tax Regulations 1985. By this amendment a car tax registered trader is required to preserve for three years a certificate issued by a lessor which states that the conditions for the relief from car tax for a vehicle supplied for leasing to a handicapped person have been met.

items.title: The Standard and Collective Community Charges (Scotland) Amendment (No.2) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations revoke regulation 4 of the Standard and Collective Community Charges (Scotland) Regulations 1988 ( “the 1988 Regulations”).

items.title: The Abolition of Domestic Rates (Domestic and Part Residential Subjects) (No.2) (Scotland) Regulations 1989
items.description: Under Part I of the Abolition of Domestic Rates Etc (Scotland) Act 1987 (“the Act”), as amended by the Local Government Finance Act 1988, rates ceased to be leviable in respect of domestic subjects with effect from 1st April 1989. Domestic subjects are defined in section 2(3) of the Act, as amended.

items.title: The Road Vehicles (Construction and Use) (Amendment) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations amend the Road Vehicles (Construction and Use) Regulations 1986 in four respects.

items.title: The Town and Country Planning (General Development) (Scotland) Amendment Order 1989
items.description: This Order amends the Town and Country Planning (General Development) (Scotland) Order 1981 in two ways.

items.title: The Aerodromes (Designation) (Facilities for Consultation) Order 1989
items.description: Under section 35 of the Civil Aviation Act 1982 the person having the management of any aerodrome to which the section applies shall provide facilities for consultation on matters concerning the management or administration of the aerodrome for users of the aerodrome, for any appropriate local authority and for any other organisation representing local interests.

items.title: The Gas Cooking Appliances (Safety) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations prohibit the supply of gas cooking appliances which do not comply either with the requirements specified in the Regulations or with the safety requirements of European Standard EN 30 or British Standard Specification 5386: Part 3: 1980 (domestic cooking appliances burning gas) or Part 4: 1983 (built-in domestic cooking appliances). Offering for supply, agreeing to supply and exposing and possessing for supply such appliances are also prohibited (regulation 3).

items.title: The Civil Legal Aid (Scotland) (Fees) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations regulate fees and outlays allowable to solicitors and fees allowable to counsel (subject to any taxation) from the Scottish Legal Aid Fund in respect of civil legal aid, when such fees and outlays are not recoverable by virtue of an award of expenses, or of any agreement as to expenses (regulation 3).

items.title: The Criminal Legal Aid (Scotland) (Fees) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations prescribe the fees and outlays of solicitors, and the fees of counsel, which an auditor of court may allow in taxing an account of fees and outlays claimed from the Scottish Legal Aid Fund in respect of legal aid in criminal proceedings in any court in Scotland. The Regulations apply only to proceedings concluded on or after 31st August 1989 (regulation 3).

items.title: The Advice and Assistance (Scotland) Amendment (No.2) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations amend the provisions of the Advice and Assistance (Scotland) Regulations 1987 (“the principal Regulations”).

items.title: The Legal Advice and Assistance (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations amend the Legal Advice and Assistance (Scotland) Regulations 1973.

items.title: The Legal Aid (Scotland) (Fees in Criminal Proceedings) Amendment (No.2) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations amend the Legal Aid (Scotland) (Fees in Criminal Proceedings) Regulations 1984 so as to increase the fees allowable to solicitors for legal aid in criminal proceedings given under the Legal Aid (Scotland) Act 1967. The increased fees will apply to proceedings concluded on or after 31st August 1989.

items.title: The Legal Aid (Scotland) (Fees in Civil Proceedings) Amendment (No.2) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Legal Aid (Scotland) (Fees in Civil Proceedings) Regulations 1984 so as to increase the fees allowable to solicitors for legal aid in civil proceedings. The overall increase is around 9% and will apply to work done on or after 31st August 1989.

items.title: The Central Regional Council (Denny and Dunipace Water Supply Confirmation Act 1888) Repeal Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The Central Regional Council (Denny and Dunipace Water Supply Confirmation Act 1888) Repeal Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Annual Close Time (River Dee (Aberdeenshire) Salmon Fishery District) Order 1989
items.description: In relation to the annual close time of the River Dee (Aberdeenshire) salmon fishery district (27th August to 10th February) this Order prescribes the period from 27th August to 31st October as the period during which fishing for salmon by rod and line is permitted in that part of the district comprising the River Carron, the River Cowie (other than the part from the river’s source to the downstream side of the Swanley Road Bridge) and part of Stonehaven Bay. For the remaining part of the district the period during which such rod and line fishing is permitted in the annual close time continues to be from 27th August to 30th September, by virtue of the provisions referred to in section 6(2)(b) of the Salmon Act 1986.

items.title: The Secretaries of State (Government Oil Pipe-line and Petroleum Licences) Order 1989
items.description: This Order transfers property, rights and liabilities in consequence of certain transfers of Ministerial functions.

items.title: The River Dee (Aberdeenshire) Salmon Fishery District (Baits and Lures) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations specify natural prawns and shrimps and any part of them for the purposes of the definition of “rod and line” in section 24(1) of the Salmon and Freshwater Fisheries (Protection) (Scotland) Act 1951. They apply throughout the River Dee (Aberdeenshire) salmon fishery district for the whole of each year. The effect is to prohibit the use of natural prawns and shrimps (or any part of them) as bait when fishing by rod and line for salmon and sea trout.

items.title: The Northern Ireland (Emergency Provisions) Act 1978 (Amendment) Order 1989
items.description: This Order adds to the list of scheduled offences in Schedule 4 to the Northern Ireland (Emergency Provisions) Act 1978 (which are not to be treated as scheduled offences if the Attorney General for Northern Ireland so certifies in any particular case) the offence of blackmail. The Order comes into force on 24th August 1989.

items.title: The Anglian Water Authority (Rushbrooke Borehole) Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The Anglian Water Authority (Rushbrooke Borehole) Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Education (School Government) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations apply (except where the context otherwise requires) in relation to all county, voluntary and maintained special schools. The Regulations make provision as to the meetings and proceedings of the governing bodies of such schools. Provision is also made as to the delegation of functions by governing bodies, and the establishment of committees by them (Part III); as to the meetings and proceedings of selection panels (constituted in relation to the appointment of head teachers and deputy head teachers at county, controlled, maintained special and special agreement schools) (Part IV); and as to the circumstances in which a local education authority, where they would otherwise have been required to consult the governing body of a school before taking a particular step, may proceed without such consultation (Part V). The Regulations revoke the Education (School Government) Regulations 1987 (“the 1987 Regulations”) (regulation 2), and re-enact them with additions and amendments. Major amendments and new provisions are noted below. Except where the context otherwise requires, any reference to a “governing body” is a reference to the governing body of a county, voluntary or maintained special school; “governing body” includes a temporary governing body; and “school” includes a new school as defined in paragraph 1 of Schedule 2 to the Education (No. 2) Act 1986 (regulation 3).

items.title: The Levens Bridge–Carlisle Trunk Road (A595 Hensingham Bypass) (Detrunking) Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The Levens Bridge–Carlisle Trunk Road (A595 Hensingham Bypass) (Detrunking) Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Levens Bridge – Carlisle Trunk Road (A595 Hensingham Bypass) Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The Levens Bridge – Carlisle Trunk Road (A595 Hensingham Bypass) Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Bermuda Constitution (Amendment) Order 1989
items.description: This Order amends the Constitution of Bermuda to enable the Governor to delegate, under certain conditions, responsibility to Ministers for defence, including the armed forces, in addition to the matters responsibility for which he was previously empowered to delegate, that is to say external affairs, internal security and the police.

items.title: The Consular Fees Order 1989
items.description: This Order revokes and replaces the Consular Fees Order 1987 and subsequent amending Orders. It increases some consular fees by amounts that are intended to reflect, as far as possible, the cost of providing the services. The average increase is approximately 20%. It rationalises and clarifies the description of the services for which fees are charged. In particular, the introduction of the new United Kingdom passport in the European Community common format and the cessation of the issue of standard passports which include the particulars of a spouse, have necessitated a revision of the fees for Passports, Visas and Kindred Matters in Part II. The fees in respect of Shipping, Seamen and Kindred Matters in Part IX have been rationalised and reduced in number.

items.title: The Finance Act 1989 (Savings-Related Share Option Schemes) (Appointed Day) Order 1989
items.description: (This Note is not part of the Order)

items.title: The Savings Certificates (Yearly Plan) (Amendment) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations amend the Savings Certificates (Yearly Plan) Regulations 1984.

items.title: The London–Edinburgh–Thurso Trunk Road (Bramham Moor to Grange Moor and Slip Roads) Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The London–Edinburgh–Thurso Trunk Road (Bramham Moor to Grange Moor and Slip Roads) Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The A2 and the A205 Trunk Roads (Rochester Way and Westhorne Avenue Detrunking)Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The A2 and the A205 Trunk Roads (Rochester Way and Westhorne Avenue Detrunking)Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Hong Kong (Legislative Powers) Order 1989
items.description: This Order confers on the legislature of Hong Kong the power to repeal or amend enactments insofar as they are part of the law of Hong Kong, and to make laws having extra-territorial operation, in order to enable legislation to be enacted to give effect to international agreements which apply to Hong Kong.

items.title: The Water Authorities (Transfer of Functions) (Appointed Day) Order 1989
items.description: This Order appoints 1st September 1989 as the transfer date for purposes of section 4 of the Water Act 1989.

items.title: The Water Reorganisation (Holding Companies of Successor Companies) Order 1989
The Water Reorganisation (Successor Companies) (Transfer of Loans) Order 1989
The Financial Services (Authorised Unit Trust Scheme) (Certificate of Compliance) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations confer power on the Securities and Investments Board, additional to that exercisable by it under section 78(8) of the Financial Services Act 1986, to issue a certificate to the effect that an authorised unit trust scheme complies with the conditions necessary for it to enjoy the rights conferred by Council Directive No. 85/611/EEC co-ordinating the laws, regulations and administrative provisions relating to undertakings for collective investment in transferable securities.

items.title: The Agricultural Levy Reliefs (Frozen Beef and Veal) Order 1989
items.description: This Order, which applies throughout the United Kingdom, requires the Minister of Agriculture, Fisheries and Food to allocate to persons established within the United Kingdom its share of a quota for the levy-free import of frozen beef and veal under the provisions of Council Regulation (EEC) No. 4076/88 (OJ No. L359, 28.12.88, p. 5).

items.title: The Education (Teachers) (Amendment) (No. 2) Regulations 1989
items.description: These amending Regulations make 4 corrections to the Education (Teachers) Regulations 1989. Paragraph 2 of Schedule 1 is amended so that it does not re-impose a bar on the employment of a teacher imposed before 8th April 1982 but subsequently withdrawn. Paragraph 5(3)(a)(ii) of Schedule 4 is amended by the addition to the statement required under paragraph 5(3)(a) of a requirement to state that the schools at which the teacher may be employed are maintained by the same local education authority. A drafting amendment has been made to paragraph 1(1) of Schedule 5. The final sentence of paragraph 2(3) of Schedule 5 has been deleted as inconsistent with the requirement for written notification of qualified teacher status under paragraph 1(1)(b) of that Schedule.

items.title: The Harwich Dock Company Harbour Empowerment Order 1988
items.description: This Order authorises the Harwich Dock Company Limited to construct and maintain new works at Harwich Harbour; and to carry out subsidiary works.

items.title: The Essex (District Boundaries) Order 1989
items.description: This Order transfers an area in the county of Essex from the district of Epping Forest to the district of Harlow. Consequential changes to parishes and local government electoral areas are made.

items.title: The Landlord and Tenant Act 1954, Part II (Notices) (Amendment) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations replace forms 1, 3, 13 and 15 (notices terminating business tenancies to which Part II of the Landlord and Tenant Act 1954 applies) in Schedule 2 to the Landlord and Tenant Act 1954, Part II (Notices) Regulations 1983. The notices now expressly require a tenant to notify the landlord whether he is willing to give up possession of the property let to him as well as whether he is unwilling to do that. The Regulations also update a reference to a booklet in the forms prescribed by the 1983 Regulations.

items.title: The A1079 Trunk Road (Market Weighton Bypass) Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The A1079 Trunk Road (Market Weighton Bypass) Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The St. Helena Constitution Order 1989
items.description: This Order validates the continuance in being and proceedings of the St. Helena Executive and Legislative Councils and Council Committees notwithstanding the revocation of the St. Helena (Constitution) Order 1966, under which they were constituted, and its replacement by the St. Helena Constitution Order 1988. It also requires the dissolution of the Legislative Council no later than 6th March 1989.

items.title: The A1079 Trunk Road (York Road to Weighton Hill) (Detrunking) Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The A1079 Trunk Road (York Road to Weighton Hill) (Detrunking) Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Diseases of Animals (Approved Disinfectants) (Amendment) (No. 2) Order 1989
items.description: This Order further amends the Diseases of Animals (Approved Disinfectants) Order 1978 by adding to the list of approved disinfectants in that Order those newly approved disinfectants which are mentioned in Schedule 1 to this Order (article 2).

items.title: The A35 Trunk Road (Yellowham Hill, Troy Town Improvement) Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The A35 Trunk Road (Yellowham Hill, Troy Town Improvement) Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Water Act 1989 (Commencement No. 2 and Transitional Provisions) Order 1989
items.description: Article 2 of this Order brings into force in England and Wales on 1st September 1989 part of section 98 and sections 99 and 100 of the Water Act 1989 (relating to statutory water companies) and some of the entries in Part II of Schedule 27 (repeals).

items.title: The Merger Reference (Blue Circle Industries plc and Myson Group plc) Order 1989
items.description: Blue Circle Industries plc, its subsidiaries and its associates are prohibited by this Order from acquiring any shares or interests in shares in Myson Group plc beyond those already held, from exercising 15% or more of voting rights and from making agreements with others to secure control of Myson.

items.title: The Merger Reference (Yale & Valor plc and Myson Group plc) Order 1989
items.description: Yale & Valor plc, its subsidiaries and its associates are prohibited by this Order from acquiring any shares or interests in shares in Myson Group plc if together they would have 15% or more of any class of voting shares, from exercising 15% or more of voting rights and from making agreements with others to secure control of Myson.

items.title: The Naval, Military and Air Forces etc. (Disablement and Death) Service Pensions Amendment Order 1989
items.description: This Order further amends the Naval, Military and Air Forces etc. (Disablement and Death) Service Pensions Order 1983 (“the principal Order”) which makes provision for pensions and other awards in respect of disablement or death due to service in the naval, military and air forces during the 1914 World War and after 2nd September 1939.

items.title: The Water Act 1989 (Commencement No. 3) (Scotland) Order 1989
items.description: This Order brings into force on 1st September 1989 the provisions of the Water Act 1989 extending to Scotland which are not brought into force directly by section 194(2) or (3) of the Act other than—

items.title: The Submarine Pipe-lines (Designated Owners) (No. 19) Order 1989
items.description: This Order designates the companies listed in Part I of the Schedule as owners of the pipe-lines described in Part II of the Schedule. The pipe-lines lie above and between the Beryl and Ness oilfields south-east of the Shetland Islands.

items.title: The Submarine Pipe-lines (Designated Owners) (No. 20) Order 1989
items.description: This Order designates Texaco North Sea UK Company as owner of the pipe-line described in the Schedule, which lies above and between a number of offshore oilfields situated east of the Highland Region of Scotland.

items.title: The Submarine Pipe-lines (Designated Owners) (No. 21) Order 1989
items.description: This Order designates owners of pipe-lines lying above and between the North and South Cormorant oil fields, situated north-east of the Shetland Islands. The companies listed in Part I of Schedule 2 are designated as owners of those parts of the pipe-lines (including associated apparatus and works) which are installed on or beneath the Cormorant A Platform, and the companies listed in Part II of Schedule 2 are designated as owners of the remainder of the pipe-lines.

items.title: The Submarine Pipe-lines (Designated Owners) (No. 22) Order 1989
items.description: This Order designates owners of two pipe-lines lying between offshore installations above the Clyde and Fulmar oilfields, situated east of the coast of Fife. The companies listed in Part I of the Schedule are designated as owners of the apparatus and works and the riser which in each case form part of each of the pipe-lines and which are installed on or beneath the platform, and the companies listed in Part II of the Schedule are designated as owners of that part of the remainder of each of the pipe-lines which lie in controlled waters, that is the territorial sea and areas designated under the Continental Shelf Act 1964 (c. 29).

items.title: The Submarine Pipe-lines (Designated Owners) (No. 23) Order 1989
items.description: This Order designates the companies listed in Part I of the Schedule as owners of the pipe-lines described in Part II of the Schedule which lie between installations situated east of the coast of Lincolnshire and north east of the coast of Norfolk.

items.title: The Submarine Pipe-lines (Designated Owners) (No. 24) Order 1989
items.description: This Order designates the companies listed in Part I of the Schedule as owners of the pipe-line described in Part II of the Schedule. The pipe-line lies between installations above the Hutton and the North West Hutton oilfields, situated north east of the Shetland Islands.

items.title: The Submarine Pipe-lines (Designated Owners) (No. 25) Order 1989
items.description: This Order designates the companies listed in Part I of the Schedule as owners of the pipe-line described in Part II of the Schedule, which lies between the Piper oilfield situated east of the Highland Region of Scotland and the MCP-01 Platform also situated east of the Highland Region of Scotland.

items.title: The Copyright (International Conventions) (Amendment) Order 1989
items.description: This Order further amends the Copyright (International Conventions) Order 1979 to take account of the accessions of Liberia and the United States of America to the Berne Copyright Convention.

items.title: The Guarantee Payments (Exemption) (No. 25) Order 1989
items.description: This Order, which comes into force on 29th September 1989, excludes from the operation of section 12 of the Employment Protection (Consolidation) Act 1978 employees to whom the Working Rule Agreement of the Building and Allied Trades Joint Industrial Council made between the parties described in Schedule 1 to this Order on 14th December 1988 relates. It supersedes the Guarantee Payments (Exemption) (No. 20) Order 1980 which is revoked by this Order. The present Order has been made to take account of the revised Working Rule Agreement, the material provisions of which are set out in Schedule 2 to this Order. These differ from the provisions in Schedule 2 to the previous Order in that the guaranteed week is reduced from 40 hours to 39 hours and the provisions relating to the temporary lay off procedure have been redrafted.

items.title: The Sea Fishing (Enforcement of Community Conservation Measures) (Amendment) (No. 2) Order 1989
items.description: This Order further amends the Sea Fishing (Enforcement of Community Conservation Measures) Order 1986 (“the principal Order”), which makes provision for the enforcement of certain of the enforceable Community restrictions and obligations concerning technical measures for the conservation of fishery resources which are contained in Council Regulation (EEC) No. 3094/86 (“the Council Regulation”).

items.title: The Motor Vehicles (Type Approval) (Amendment) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Motor Vehicles (Type Approval) Regulations 1980 so thatthe type approval requirements relating to the installation of lighting and light signalling devices on motor vehicles and their trailers are, as an alternative to the existing requirements, the requirements of Council Directive 76/756/EEC as amended by Commission Directives 80/233/EEC and 82/244/EEC, Council Directive 83/276/EEC, and Commission Directives 84/8/EEC as corrected and 89/278/EEC, as corrected;the type approval requirements, relating to direction indicator lamps for motor vehicles and their trailers are, as an alternative to the existing requirements, the requirements of Council Directive 76/759/EEC as amended by Commission Directive 89/277/EEC, as corrected; andthe type approval requirements relating to the lateral protection (side guards) of certain motor vehicles and their trailers are the requirements of Council Directive 89/297/EEC.

items.title: The Motor Vehicles (Type Approval for Goods Vehicles) (Great Britain) (Amendment) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Motor Vehicles (Type Approval for Goods Vehicles) (Great Britain) Regulations 1982.

items.title: The Motor Vehicles (Type Approval) (Great Britain) (Amendment) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Motor Vehicles (Type Approval) (Great Britain) Regulations 1984.

items.title: The Financial Services Act 1986 (Commencement) (No. 12) Order 1989
items.description: This Order brings sections 24 and 86 of the Financial Services Act 1986 into force on 1st October 1989. Those sections implement Community obligations arising by virtue of Council Directive No. 85/611/EEC (O.J. No. L375/3) as amended by Council Directive No. 88/22O/EEC (O.J. No. L100/31). The relevant provisions concern the right of certain collective investment schemes constituted in other Member States to be recognised schemes for the purposes of the Financial Services Act 1986 and the right of the operators and trustees of such schemes to be authorised persons. The Order repeals the Prevention of Fraud (Investments) Act (Northern Ireland) 1940 and the Prevention of Fraud (Investments) Act 1958 for all remaining purposes with effect from 1st October 1989. It also brings certain related repeals into force.

items.title: The Financial Services (Schemes Authorised in Designated Countries or Territories) (Notification) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations prescribe the period of four months as the period within which the Securities and Investments Board may notify the operator of a scheme which is managed in and authorised under the law of a country or territory designated under section 87 of the Financial Services Act 1986 that the scheme is not to be recognised. The Regulations have effect only where the operator has given notice to the Securities and Investments Board on or after the date on which the Regulations come into force that he wishes the scheme to be recognised. Where notice has been given before that date, the prescribed period is two months.

items.title: The Financial Services (Schemes Constituted in Other Member States) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations prescribe the requirements which a collective investment scheme must satisfy if, subject to the provisions of section 86(2) of the Financial Services Act 1986, it is to be a recognised scheme under section 86 of that Act. Section 86 of the Financial Services Act implements a Community obligation arising by virtue of Council Directive No. 85/611/EEC co-ordinating the laws, regulations and administrative provisions relating to undertakings for collective investment in transferable securities.

items.title: The Financial Services (Recognised Collective Investment Schemes from Other Member States) (Luxembourg) Order 1988 (Revocation) Order 1989
items.description: This Order revokes the Financial Services (Recognised Collective Investment Schemes from Other Member States) (Luxembourg) Order 1988 except insofar as that order has effect in relation to collective investment schemes falling within article 2 of the Financial Services Act 1986 will, in future, apply to other collective investment schemes of a class specified in the order being revoked.

items.title: The Insolvency Practitioners (Amendment) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations amend the Insolvency Practitioners Regulations 1986 so as to alter the prescribed requirements with respect to practical training and experience and the prescribed fee for persons applying to the Secretary of State for authorisation to act as insolvency practitioners in cases where the applicant holds an authorisation granted by him.

items.title: The Local Government (Direct Labour Organisations) (Competition) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations consolidate with amendments the Local Goverment (Direct Labour Organisations) (Competition) Regulations 1983, the Local Government (Direct Labour Organisations) (Competition) (Amendment) Regulations 1987 and the Local Government (Direct Labour Organisations) (Competition) (Amendment) Regulations 1988, all of which are revoked.

items.title: The Local Government (Direct Labour Organisations) (Specified Number of Employed Persons) Order 1989
items.description: At present those local authorities and development bodies which employed more than 30 persons in carrying out construction or maintenance work at any time in the previous financial year are subject to the controls Part III of the Local Government, Planning and Land Act 1980. Local authorities and development bodies which did not employ more than 30 persons in carrying out one of the descriptions of construction or maintenance work (section 10(2)) were exempt from the requirement to keep separate accounts for that description of work.

items.title: The Town and Country Planning General Development (Amendment) (No. 2) Order 1989
items.description: This Order amends the Town and Country Planning General Development Order 1988 (the 1988 Order). Under the Water Act 1989 (c. 15) the functions of water authorities are transferred to the National Rivers Authority and water and sewerage undertakers. This Order therefore makes the National Rivers Authority the consultee for the purposes of article 18 of the 1988 Order (consultations before the grant of planning permission) rather than the water authority concerned (article 2(2)) and transfers the permitted development rights of water authorities to the National Rivers Authority and the water and sewerage undertakers (article 2(1), (3), (4) and (5)). It also makes some minor consequential drafting amendments.

items.title: The Motorcycles (Sound Level Measurement Certificates) (Amendment) (No.2) Regulations 1989
items.description: The Motorcycles (Sound Level Measurement Certificates) Regulations 1980 (“the 1980 Regulations”), as amended, made provision as required under Council Directive 78/1015/EEC (on the approximation of the laws of the Member States on the permissible sound level and the exhaust systems of motorcycles) as amended by Council Directive 87/56/EEC for the issue of a sound level measurement certificate stating that a type of motorcycle complies with the harmonised requirements specified in Council Directive 78/1015/EEC amended as above.

items.title: The Petty Sessional Divisions (Essex) Order 1989
items.description: This Order gives effect to a draft order submitted by the magistrates' courts committee for the county of Essex and provides for the alteration of the name of the petty sessional division of Billericay to Basildon.

items.title: The Criminal Justice Act 1988 (Commencement No. 9) Order 1989
items.description: This Order brings into force on 26th September 1989 provisions of the Criminal Justice Act 1988 which insert in the Backing of Warrants (Republic of Ireland) Act 1965 a new section 2A concerned with appeals by way of case stated. (Sections 1 to 21 of and Schedule 1 to the Criminal Justice Act 1988 are repealed and (with the exception of that amendment of the 1965 Act) re-enacted by the Extradition Act 1989 (c. 33) which comes into force on 27th September 1989 (section 38(2)). Section 2A of the 1965 Act is saved by section 37(5) of the Extradition Act 1989.)

items.title: The Magistrates' Courts (Backing of Warrants) (Amendment) Rules 1989
items.description: These Rules amend the Magistrates' Courts (Backing of Warrants) Rules 1965 (which apply only to England and Wales) in relation to appeals by way of case stated against refusals to order the delivery of persons to the Republic of Ireland under section 2A of the Backing of Warrants (Republic of Ireland) Act 1965 (inserted by Schedule 1 to the Criminal Justice Act 1988). Rule 3 of these Rules requires notice to be given to the Crown Office of the release on bail of the person concerned pending such an appeal. New rules 5A and 5B (inserted by rule 4) specify respectively a period during which an application to state a case must be made and a period during which the court must comply with such an application

items.title: The Magistrates' Courts (Extradition) Rules 1989
items.description: These Rules make procedural provision in relation to extradition proceedings under the Extradition Act 1989 (which consolidates Part I of the Criminal Justice Act 1988, the Extradition Act 1870 (c. 52) (as amended by the 1988 Act) and the Fugitive Offenders Act 1967 (c. 68) (as so amended)). Rule 4 specifies time limits and other requirements in relation to appeals by case stated under section 10 of the 1989 Act against a refusal to make an order of committal. Rule 5 and form 1 in the Schedule to the Rules prescribe the manner in which notice is to be given of waiver of the right to seek habeas corpus under the simplified procedure under section 14 of and paragraph 9 of Schedule 1 to the 1989 Act. Rule 6 enables a magistrate to order the committal for return of a person with his consent at any time after his arrest under the simplified procedure and form 2 in the Schedule prescribes a notice of consent for this purpose.

items.title: The A6(M) Motorway (Stockport North–South Bypass) and Connecting Roads Scheme 1989
items.description: This version of the The A6(M) Motorway (Stockport North–South Bypass) and Connecting Roads Scheme 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The A6 Trunk Road (Hazel Grove Diversion) Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The A6 Trunk Road (Hazel Grove Diversion) Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Dairy Produce Quotas (Amendment) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations, which apply throughout the United Kingdom and come into force on 31 January 1989, amend the Dairy Produce Quotas Regulations 1986 (“the principal regulations”).

items.title: The A6 Trunk Road Hazel Grove (Detrunking) Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The A6 Trunk Road Hazel Grove (Detrunking) Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The A523 Trunk Road (Hazel Grove Diversion) Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The A523 Trunk Road (Hazel Grove Diversion) Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The A523 Trunk Road Hazel Grove (Detrunking) Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The A523 Trunk Road Hazel Grove (Detrunking) Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Customs Duties (ECSC) (Amendment No. 5) Order 1989
items.description: This Order, which comes into force on 7th September 1989, reimposes duties on certain categories of iron or steel products originating in Yugoslavia.

items.title: The Special Hospitals Service Authority (Functions and Membership) Amendment Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations give further directions to the special health authority known as the Special Hospitals Service Authority which was constituted on 1st July 1989. Following the abolition of the Rampton Hospital Board, the Broadmoor Hospital Board and the Moss Side and Park Lane Hospitals Board, these Regulations replace the limited functions which were specified in regulation 2 of the Special Hospitals Service Authority (Functions and Membership) Regulations 1989 and direct the special health authority to perform, on behalf of the Secretary of State, a wider range of functions under the National Health Service Act 1977 relating to special hospitals as well as his functions as manager of those hospitals by virtue of section 145(1) of the Mental Health Act 1983.

items.title: The Motor Vehicles (Driving Licences) (Amendment) (No. 3) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Motor Vehicles (Driving Licences) Regulations 1987.

items.title: The Hovercraft (Fees) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations revoke the Hovercraft (Fees) Regulations 1987.

items.title: The Anglian Water Authority (East Watton Borehole) Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The Anglian Water Authority (East Watton Borehole) Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Severn-Trent Water Authority (Nether Padley) Mains Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The Severn-Trent Water Authority (Nether Padley) Mains Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The A47 Trunk Road (East Norton Bypass) Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The A47 Trunk Road (East Norton Bypass) Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Welsh Water Authority (Abergele Chest Hospital Waterworks) Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The Welsh Water Authority (Abergele Chest Hospital Waterworks) Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Welsh Water Authority (Parc to Glanrhyd Hospital Water Supply) Order 1989
