items.description: This version of the The Welsh Water Authority (Parc to Glanrhyd Hospital Water Supply) Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Welsh Water Authority (Pendine Water Supply) Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The Welsh Water Authority (Pendine Water Supply) Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Welsh Water Authority (North Wales Hospital, Denbigh, Waterworks) Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The Welsh Water Authority (North Wales Hospital, Denbigh, Waterworks) Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Local Government Superannuation (Valuation and Community Charge Tribunals) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations make provision for the superannuation of staff of the valuation and community charge tribunals established under Schedule 11 to the Local Government Finance Act 1988 (c. 41).

items.title: The Bodmin Railway Centre Light Railway Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The Bodmin Railway Centre Light Railway Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Income Support (Transitional) Amendment Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Income Support (Transitional) Regulations 1987 by providing for a person’s transitional addition not to be reduced by any increase in his applicable amount in the benefit week commencing 9th October 1989 consequent upon the introduction of the new pensioner premium for persons 75 and over, or upon the uprating of the higher pensioner premium, or by any increase in the applicable amount consequent upon attendance on a training course (regulation 3(b) in so far as it inserts new paragraphs (1D) and (1E)); and provide, in urgent cases, for the addition to be reduced by any increase in a person’s applicable amount (regulation 3(a)).

items.title: The Social Security (Credits) Amendment Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations amend the Social Security (Credits) Regulations 1975.

items.title: The Assured and Protected Tenancies (Lettings to Students) (Amendment) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations amend the Assured and Protected Tenancies (Lettings to Students) Regulations 1988. A new Schedule is added (as Schedule 2) to those Regulations specifying six bodies for the purposes of paragraph 8 of Schedule 1 to the Housing Act 1988. (Two of these bodies had been so specified in the 1988 Regulations but had also, incorrectly, been specified for the purposes of section 8 of the Rent Act 1977, and the opportunity has been taken to correct that error.)

items.title: The National Health Service (General Dental Services) Amendment (No. 3) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations amend the National Health Service (General Dental Services) Regulations 1973 which provide for the arrangements under which dentists provide dental services under the National Health Service in England and Wales.

items.title: The National Health Service (Service Committees and Tribunal) Amendment Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations further amend the National Health Service (Service Committees and Tribunal) Regulations 1974 (“the 1974 Regulations”) in which provision is made, amongst other things, for the withholding of remuneration by Family Practitioner Committees, at the direction of the Secretary of State, from dentists who have failed to comply with the terms of service for dentists who provide general dental services under the National Health Service Act 1977.

items.title: The Education Reform Act 1988 (Commencement No. 5) Order 1989
items.description: This Order brings into force on 1st March 1989 section 12 of the 1988 Act, except in relation to county schools currently maintained by the Inner London Education Authority. That section relates to the determination by standing advisory councils on religious education of the cases in which the requirement for Christian collective worship is not to apply to a county school. Also brought into force on that date is paragraph 58 of Schedule 12 to the Act, which amends section 80 of the Education Act 1944 (registration of pupils at schools).

items.title: The Personal and Occupational Pension Schemes (Miscellaneous Amendments) (No. 2) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations make miscellaneous amendments to Regulations about personal and occupational pension schemes.

items.title: The Social Security (Abolition of Earnings Rule) (Consequential) Regulations 1989
items.description: The Regulations contained in this instrument are consequential upon the abolition in section 7 of the Social Security Act 1989 (c. 24) (“the 1989 Act”) of the earnings rule for retirement pensions. The instrument is made before the end of the period of 6 months beginning with the coming into force of section 7; the regulations in it are therefore exempted by section 61(5) of the Social Security Act 1986 (c. 50) (as amended by the 1989 Act, Schedule 8, paragraph 12(3)) from the requirement under section 10 of the Social Security Act 1980 (c. 30) to refer the proposals to the Social Security Advisory Committee and are made without reference to that Committee.

items.title: The Gipsy Encampments (Borough of Oadby and Wigston) Order 1989
items.description: This Order designates the Borough of Oadby and Wigston as an area to which section 10 of the Caravan Sites Act 1968 applies.

items.title: The Gipsy Encampments (District of Blaby) Order 1989
items.description: This Order designates the District of Blaby as an area to which section 10 of the Caravan Sites Act 1968 applies.

items.title: The A33 Trunk Road (Chilcomb Link) Detrunking Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The A33 Trunk Road (Chilcomb Link) Detrunking Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Education (Grants) (Grant-Maintained Schools Limited) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations authorise the Secretary of State to pay grants to the company named Grant-Maintained Schools Limited (also known as the Grant-Maintained Schools Trust) to meet or help to meet expenditure incurred or to be incurred by it for the purposes of, or in connection with, the provision or proposed provision of educational services in grant-maintained schools. Grants are payable in respect of expenditure which is approved by the Secretary of State. The Secretary of State may determine the amounts and timing of payments (regulations 1 and 2).

items.title: The A33 Trunk Road (Popham–Kingsworthy) Detrunking Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The A33 Trunk Road (Popham–Kingsworthy) Detrunking Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Adoption Allowance Schemes Order 1989
items.description: Section 57(4) of the Adoption Act 1976 disapplies the prohibition of payments in connection with adoption arrangements where a scheme for payment has been submitted to the Secretary of State by an adoption agency and approved. Section 57(7) provides for subsection (4) to expire on the seventh anniversary of the date on which section 32 of the Children Act 1975 came into force (15th February 1982) unless the Secretary of State repeals subsection (7) prior to the said anniversary.

items.title: The Cardiff-Glan Conwy Trunk Road (A470) The Newtown-Aberystwyth Trunk Road (A44) (Trunk Roads and Junction Improvements, Llangurig) Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The Cardiff-Glan Conwy Trunk Road (A470) The Newtown-Aberystwyth Trunk Road (A44) (Trunk Roads and Junction Improvements, Llangurig) Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Motor Vehicles (Authorisation of Special Types) (Amendment) Order 1989
items.description: This Order further amends the Motor Vehicles (Authorisation of Special Types) General Order 1979, “the 1979 Order”, as follows.

items.title: The A406 Trunk Road (North Circular Road, Brent) (Prescribed Routes) Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The A406 Trunk Road (North Circular Road, Brent) (Prescribed Routes) Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Electricity and Pipe-line Works (Assessment of Environmental Effects) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations are concerned with the implementation of Council Directive 85/337/EEC (OJ No. L175, 5.7.85, p.40) on the assessment of the effects of certain public and private projects on the environment.

items.title: The Offshore Installations and Pipeline Works (First-Aid) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations apply to and in relation to offshore installations and pipeline works within the scope of the Health and Safety at Work etc. Act 1974 (Application outside Great Britain) Order 1989 and to premises and activities connected with those installations or works (regulation 3).

items.title: The Offshore Installations (Operational Safety, Health and Welfare and Life-Saving Appliances) (Revocations) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations revoke provisions relating to first-aid in the Offshore Installations (Operational Safety, Health and Welfare) Regulations 1976 and the Offshore Installations (Life-Saving Appliances) Regulations 1977. The revocations do not extend to tidal waters and parts of the sea in or adjacent to Northern Ireland up to the seaward limits of territorial waters. They will take effect on the day on which the major part of the Offshore Installations and Pipeline Works (First-Aid) Regulations 1989 (S.I. 1989/1671) will come into force.

items.title: The Firearms (Amendment) Act 1988 (Commencement No. 2) Order (Amendment) Order 1989
items.description: This Order amends provisions of the Firearms (Amendment) Act 1988 (Commencement No.2) Order 1989 relating to section 20 of the Firearms (Amendment) Act 1988 which amends section 6 of the Firearms Act 1968 (c. 27) in respect of the Secretary of State’s power to make an order controlling movements of firearms and ammunition between Great Britain and Northern Ireland. The effect of this Order is twofold. First section 20 of the 1988 Act will not come fully into force on 1st October 1989 as provided by the earlier commencement order. Secondly, insofar as section 20 is already in force for the purpose of enabling the Secretary of State to make an order, any such order will, as a consequence, come into force not on 1st October 1989 but on the date on which section 20 comes into force for all other purposes.50p net

items.title: The Superannuation (Valuation and Community Charge Tribunals) Order 1989
items.description: Employment with a valuation and community charge tribunal was on 1st May 1989 added to the employments listed in Schedule 1 to the Superannuation Act 1972 (“the 1972 Act”) by the Local Government Finance Act 1988 (c. 41), section 136 and Schedule 11, paragraph 6(6), and so falls within those employments to which the Principal Civil Service Pension Scheme and the Civil Service Additional Voluntary Contribution Scheme (made under section 1 of the 1972 Act) may apply. This Order removes employment by a valuation and community charge tribunal from the employments listed in that Schedule. Under the power conferred by section 1(8)(a) of the 1972 Act the Order takes effect from 1st May 1989.

items.title: The Orkney Islands Council (Loch of Boardhouse) (Amendment) Water Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The Orkney Islands Council (Loch of Boardhouse) (Amendment) Water Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Social Security (Contributions) (Transitional and Consequential Provisions) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations contain transitional, consequential and saving provisions in connection with the coming into force on 5th October 1989 of section 1 of the Social Security Act 1989 (“the 1989 Act”) by virtue of the Social Security Act 1989 (Commencement No. 1) Order 1989 (S.I. 1989/1238). Section 1 of the 1989 Act contains amendments, relating to primary Class 1 contributions, to section 4 and other provisions of the Social Security Act 1975 (c. 14).

items.title: The Income Support (General) Amendment No. 3 Regulations 1989
items.description: This version of the The Income Support (General) Amendment No. 3 Regulations 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Oxfordshire County Council (Oxford Canal, Bridge) Scheme 1987 Confirmation Instrument 1989
The Social Security (Medical Evidence, Claims and Payments) Amendment Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations amend the Social Security (Medical Evidence) Regulations 1976 (“the Medical Evidence Regulations”) and the Social Security (Claims and Payments) Regulations 1987 (“the Claims and Payments Regulations”).

items.title: The Social Security (Severe Disablement Allowance (Amendment) and Local Councillors Consequential) Regulations 1989
items.description: The Regulations contained in this instrument are made under or are otherwise consequential upon paragraph 2 of Schedule 8 to the Social Security Act 1989 (“the 1989 Act”). The instrument is made before the end of the period of 6 months beginning with the coming into force of that paragraph; the regulations in it are therefore exempted by section 61(5) of the Social Security Act 1986 (c. 50) (as amended by the 1989 Act, Schedule 8, paragraph 12(3)) from the requirement under section 10(1) of the Social Security Act 1980 to refer proposals to the Social Security Advisory Committee and are made without reference to that Committee.

items.title: The Housing Benefit (Permitted Totals) Amendment Order 1989
items.description: This Order amends the Housing Benefit (Permitted Totals) Order 1988 to take account of the replacement of rate rebates by community charge rebates in Scotland.

items.title: The Social Security (Adjudication) Amendment Regulations 1989
items.description: This version of the The Social Security (Adjudication) Amendment Regulations 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Social Security Benefit (Dependency and Computation of Earnings) Amendment Regulations 1989
items.description: The Regulations contained in this instrument are consequential upon section 9 of the Social Security Act 1989 (“the 1989 Act”) which extends the meaning of “earnings” to include personal as well as occupational pensions. The instrument is made before the end of the period of 6 months beginning with the coming into force of section 9; the regulations in it are therefore exempted by section 61(5) of the Social Security Act 1986 (c. 50) (as amended by the 1989 Act, Schedule 8, paragraph 12(3)) from the requirement under section 10 of the Social Security Act 1980 (c. 30) to refer proposals to the Social Security Advisory Committee and are made without reference to that Committee.

items.title: The A23 Trunk Road (Streatham High Road, Lambeth) (Prohibition of Right Turn) Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The A23 Trunk Road (Streatham High Road, Lambeth) (Prohibition of Right Turn) Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Cod (Specified Sea Areas) (Prohibition of Fishing) (No. 4) Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The Cod (Specified Sea Areas) (Prohibition of Fishing) (No. 4) Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Goods Vehicles (Plating and Testing) (Amendment) (No. 2) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations amend the Goods Vehicles (Plating and Testing) Regulations 1988 so as to make provision for supplements to the normal level of fees where an appointment for an examination or test is, at an applicant’s request, on a Saturday.

items.title: The Motor Vehicles (Tests) (Amendment) (No.2) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations amend the Motor Vehicles (Tests) Regulations 1981 so as to make provision for fees for the examination of Class VI vehicles where an appointment for an examination is, at an applicant’s request, on a Saturday. The Regulations do not affect the fees for examinations in other circumstances. They remove the requirement for applications to the Secretary of State for an examination of a Class VI vehicle to be made at particular places (regulation 3) and extend the circumstances in which an application can be made other than in writing to all applications for a re-examination made within 28 days of the previous examination.THE NORMAL FEES AND SATURDAY FEES ARE AS FOLLOWS:Nature of ExaminationNormal feeSaturday feeExamination under regulation 12 of the 1981 Regulations of a vehicle constructed or adapted to carry more than 12 passengers.£36.25£60.75Examination under regulation 12 in any other case.£25.25£43.25Re-examination of a vehicle constructed or adapted to carry more than 12 passengers, within 14 days of previous examination.£17.75£30.00Re-examination of any other vehicle within 14 days of previous examination.£12.50£21.50Re-examination of a vehicle constructed or adapted to carry more than 12 passengers, later than 14 days after previous examination.£36.25£60.75Re-examination of any other vehicle later than 14 days after previous examination.£25.25£43.25

items.title: The Road Vehicles (Construction and Use) (Amendment) (No. 2) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Road Vehicles (Construction and Use) Regulations 1986.

items.title: The Education (Higher Education Corporations) (No. 5) Order 1989
items.description: This Order specifies the Southampton Institute of Higher Education as a local authority maintained institute which appears to the Secretary of State to satisfy the criteria set out in section 121(2) of the Education Reform Act 1988. By virtue of section 121(2), paragraph 1 of Schedule 7 to that Act and the Education Reform Act 1988 (Commencement No. 4) Order 1988 (S.I. 1988/2271) a body corporate by the name of that institution is established on 31st January 1989 for the purpose of conducting that institution as from the transfer date. 1st April 1989 is specified as the transfer date, defined in section 123(2) of the 1988 Act as the date for transfer of local education authority property, rights and liabilities to that corporation. The Order also specifies the County Council of Hampshire as the authority able to nominate a local authority nominee to become a member of the corporation.

items.title: The Building Societies (Liquid Asset) (Amendment) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations remove the restriction in the Building Societies (Liquid Asset) Regulations 1987 which, except in the case of cash, limited the liquid assets which a building society may hold under section 21 of the Building Societies Act 1986 to enable it to meet its liabilities as they arise to assets representing liabilities expressed in sterling.

items.title: The Precept Limitation (Prescribed Maximum) (Inner London Education Authority) Order 1989
items.description: This Order prescribes a maximum precept of 78.42 pence in the pound for the Inner London Education Authority for the financial year beginning on 1st April 1989.

items.title: The A12 Trunk Road (Capel St. Mary Grade-Separated Junction Slip Roads) Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The A12 Trunk Road (Capel St. Mary Grade-Separated Junction Slip Roads) Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The M32 Motorway (Hambrook Interchange to Lower Ashley Road Interchange) and Connecting Roads Scheme 1989
items.description: This version of the The M32 Motorway (Hambrook Interchange to Lower Ashley Road Interchange) and Connecting Roads Scheme 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Merger Reference (Atlas Copco AB and Desoutter Brothers (Holdings) plc) Order 1989
items.description: Atlas Copco AB, its subsidiaries and its associates are prohibited by this Order from acquiring any shares or interests in shares in Desoutter Brothers (Holdings) plc if together they would have 15% or more of any class of voting shares, from exercising 15% or more of voting rights and from making agreements with others to secure control of Desoutter.

items.title: The Petty Sessional Divisions (West Sussex) Order 1989
items.description: This Order gives effect to a draft order submitted by the magistrates' courts committee for the county of West Sussex and provides for the petty sessional divisions of Chichester, Midhurst and Petworth to be combined to form a new petty sessional division of Chichester and District.

items.title: The Further Education (Recoupment) (Amendment) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations amend the Further Education (Recoupment) Regulations 1987 by substituting a new regulation for regulation 2 of the 1987 Regulations. Regulation 2 of those Regulations read together with the Schedule thereto prescribes categories of pupils for the purposes of section 51(1) and (5) of the Education (No. 2) Act 1986.

items.title: The A65 Trunk Road (Draughton Bypass) Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The A65 Trunk Road (Draughton Bypass) Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The A65 Trunk Road (Draughton Bypass De-Trunking) Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The A65 Trunk Road (Draughton Bypass De-Trunking) Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Great Yarmouth Port Authority Harbour Revision Order 1989
items.description: This Order reconstitutes the Great Yarmouth Port and Haven Commissioners as the Great Yarmouth Port Authority, with individual Commissioners to be known in future as Members.

items.title: The Education (Publication and Consultation Etc.) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Education (Publication and Consultation Etc.) (Scotland) Regulations 1981. They take account of the fact that by virtue of the School Boards (Scotland) Act 1988, which sets up School Boards, school councils established under section 125 of the Local Government (Scotland) Act 1973 (c. 65) are abolished with effect from 1st November 1989 (being the day after the final date prescribed for the holding of elections to the first School Boards to be established).

items.title: The Housing (Right to Buy) (Prescribed Persons) Order 1989
items.description: Part V of the Housing Act 1985 provides for the right to buy. Section 130 of the 1985 Act requires discount obtained on a previous purchase from a public sector landlord or a person prescribed by Order under section 130(2)(a) to be taken into account in calculating discount on a later purchase under the right to buy.

items.title: The Police (Amendment) (No. 2) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations amend the Police Regulations 1987, with effect from 1st September 1989 (retrospection is authorised by section 33(4) of the Police Act 1964). Regulation 3(2) provides for the payment of overtime in specified circumstances where the beginning of an officer’s tour of duty is brought forward. Regulation 3(3) makes provision for a detective expenses allowance for officers of the rank of superintendent and precludes the separate reimbursement of small items of expenditure to all officers in receipt of such an allowance. Regulation 3(4) increases pay and regulation 3(5) increases the dog handler’s allowance.

items.title: The Immobilisation of Vehicles Illegally Parked (London Borough of Hammersmith and Fulham) Order 1989
The A5 Trunk Road (Shrawardine Turn) Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The A5 Trunk Road (Shrawardine Turn) Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Anglian Water Authority (High Pale) Boreholes Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The Anglian Water Authority (High Pale) Boreholes Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The National Health Service (Superannuation) (Scotland) Amendment (No.2) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations further amend the National Health Service (Superannuation) (Scotland) Regulations 1980 (“the principal Regulations”) to ensure that the scheme for the superannuation of persons engaged in the National Health Service in Scotland complies with the requirements of the Social Security Act 1986.

items.title: The A6 Trunk Road (Market Harborough Bypass) Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The A6 Trunk Road (Market Harborough Bypass) Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The A6 Trunk Road (Market Harborough Bypass) Detrunking Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The A6 Trunk Road (Market Harborough Bypass) Detrunking Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The City of Manchester (M56 North Cheshire Motorway) (Bowdon to Wythenshawe Airport Link Road Section) Connecting Roads Scheme 1988 Confirmation Instrument 1989
items.description: This version of the The City of Manchester (M56 North Cheshire Motorway) (Bowdon to Wythenshawe Airport Link Road Section) Connecting Roads Scheme 1988 Confirmation Instrument 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Secondary Examinations Council (Designation of Staff) Order 1989
items.description: This Order concerns certain staff of the Secondary Examinations Council. The Council is to be wound up in consequence of the establishment on 15th August 1988 (under section 14 of the Education Reform Act 1988) of the Curriculum Council for Wales, the National Curriculum Council, and the School Examinations and Assessment Council. Under section 15 of that Act, the Secretary of State may designate (either individually or by class or description) employees of the School Examinations Council for automatic transfer to the new Councils. Where an individual is so designated there is no termination of, or change in the terms of, his contract of employment. This Order lists those staff so designated as respects the School Examinations and Assessment Council in addition to those already designated by the School Curriculum Development Committee and Secondary Examinations Council (Designation of Staff) Order 1988 (S.I. 1988/2171).

items.title: The Local Review Committee (Scotland) Amendment Rules 1989
items.description: These Rules amend the Local Review Committee (Scotland) Rules 1967 by providing for the establishment of a local review committee at Polmont young offenders institution.

items.title: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) Amendment No. 3 Order 1989
items.description: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) Order 1989 contains emergency prohibitions restricting various activities in order to prevent human consumption of food rendered unsuitable for that purpose in consequence of the escape in April 1986 of radioactive substances from a nuclear reactor situated at Chernobyl in the Ukraine, USSR.

items.title: The Protection of Wrecks (Designation No. 1 Order 1979) (Amendment) Order 1989
items.description: The Order amends the Protection of Wrecks (Designation No. 1) Order 1979 by redefining the centre of the site of an historic wreck in Cardigan Bay, off Tal-y-bont, Gwynedd and by increasing the restricted area to one of 300 (instead of 150) metres of the site. The amendment is made in order to include in the restricted area an additional part of the wreck recently discovered outside the area originally designated. No part of the restricted area lies above high water mark of ordinary spring tides.

items.title: The Export of Sheep (Prohibition) (No. 2) Amendment No. 3 Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The Export of Sheep (Prohibition) (No. 2) Amendment No. 3 Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (England) Amendment No. 4 Order 1989
items.description: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (England) Order 1987 contains emergency prohibitions restricting various activities in order to prevent the human consumption of food which has been or which may have been rendered unsuitable for that purpose in consequence of the escape of radioactive substances from a nuclear reactor situated at Chernobyl in the USSR.

items.title: The Smoke Control Areas (Exempted Fireplaces) Order 1989
items.description: Section 11 of the Clean Air Act 1956 empowers local authorities to declare the whole or any part of their district to be a smoke control area in which the emission of smoke is generally prohibited. Specified classes of fireplaces may, by order, be exempted from control.

items.title: The Rules of the Supreme Court (Amendment) 1989
items.description: This version of the The Rules of the Supreme Court (Amendment) 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Wales) (No. 5) Amendment No. 4 Order 1989
items.description: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Wales) (No. 5) Order 1987, as amended by S.I. 1988/9, 951, 1329, 1680, 1989/2, 660 and 1279 contains emergency prohibitions restricting various activities in order to prevent human consumption of food which has been or which may have been rendered unsuitable for that purpose in consequence of the escape of radioactive substances from a nuclear reactor situated at Chernobyl in the USSR.

items.title: The Cambridgeshire County Council (River Nene Old Course, Bodsey Bridge Reconstruction) Scheme 1989 Confirmation Instrument 1989
The Calshot Oyster Fishery (Variation) Order 1989
items.description: This Order continues the Calshot Oyster Fishery Order 1982 in force until 1 June 1995 from the day at the end of which it would otherwise have ceased to be in force, namely 7th March 1989.

items.title: The Finance Act 1989 (Repeal of Tithe Redemption Enactments) (Appointed Day) Order 1989
items.description: This Order appoints 30th September 1989 as the day for the repeal of the enactments specified in Part XIV of Schedule 17 to the Finance Act 1989 (“the Act”) other than section 27 of the Tithe Act 1936 (c. 43).

items.title: The Noise at Work Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations give effect as respects Great Britain to provisions of Council Directive 86/188/EEC (OJ No. L137, 24.5.86, p.28) on the protection of workers from the risks related to exposure to noise at work.

items.title: The Road Vehicles Lighting Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations re-enact the Road Vehicles Lighting Regulations 1984 with modifications. In addition to minor and drafting amendments, they make the following changes of substance.

items.title: The A134 Principal Road (Thetford to Tottenhill Trunking) Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The A134 Principal Road (Thetford to Tottenhill Trunking) Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Merchant Shipping (Distress Signals and Prevention of Collisions) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations give effect to the amendments made by Resolution A626(15) of the International Maritime Organization to the International Regulations for Preventing Collisions At Sea 1972. The amendments come into effect internationally on 19th November 1989.

items.title: The London Government Reorganisation (Property etc) (No. 2) Order 1989
items.description: The London Government Reorganisation (Property) Order 1989 (S.I. 1989/1101) transferred certain specified properties to local authorities. This Order gives effect to further proposals by the London Residuary Body for the transfer of its properties to local authorities and to new authorities.

items.title: The Macduff (Pilotage Powers) Order 1989
items.description: This Order provides that the Grampian Regional Council, which is a competent harbour authority for the purposes of the Pilotage Act 1987 in respect of the harbours of Buckie and Burghead, shall also be a competent harbour authority for those purposes in respect of the harbour of Macduff.

items.title: The Scottish Milk Marketing Scheme (Consolidation) Approval Order 1989
items.description: This Order, which comes into force on 25th October 1989, approves a consolidated milk marketing scheme to replace the Scottish Milk Marketing Scheme 1933, as amended. The consolidated scheme is set out in Schedule 1 to the Order. The various Orders and Regulations approving the 1933 Scheme and subsequent amendments thereto are revoked by Schedule 2 to this Order.

items.title: The A6 Trunk Road (Black Lodge Improvement) Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The A6 Trunk Road (Black Lodge Improvement) Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The A6 Trunk Road (Burton Latimer Bypass) Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The A6 Trunk Road (Burton Latimer Bypass) Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Housing Support Grant (Scotland) Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The Housing Support Grant (Scotland) Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Genetic Manipulation Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations supersede the Health and Safety (Genetic Manipulation) Regulations 1978 which they revoke.

items.title: The London Government Reorganisation (Pensions etc.) Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The London Government Reorganisation (Pensions etc.) Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Building Societies (Designation of Qualifying Bodies) (Amendment) (No. 2) Order 1989
items.description: This Order amends the Building Societies (Designation of Qualifying Bodies) Order 1988, “the 1988 Order”, which designates corporate bodies and types of corporate body as suitable for investment or support by building societies under section 18 of the Building Societies Act 1986, by designating S.W.I.F.T. (U.K.) Limited, Switch Card Services Limited, Eurocard International S.A. and MasterCard International Incorporated as such bodies.

items.title: The Building Societies (Gibraltar) Order 1989
items.description: This Order empowers building societies with commercial assets of at least £100 million to make advances on the security of land in Gibraltar, and provides for the classification of those advances within the asset classification provisions of the Building Societies Act 1986. By virtue of article 5 of this Order, the power to make such an advance does not require adoption by a special resolution of a society in order to be exercisable by it.

items.title: The Cereals Co-responsibility Levy (Amendment) (No. 2) Regulations 1989
items.description: This version of the The Cereals Co-responsibility Levy (Amendment) (No. 2) Regulations 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Social Security (Industrial Injuries and Diseases) Miscellaneous Provisions (Amendment) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations, which come into force on 1st November 1989, amend the Social Security (Industrial Injuries and Diseases) Miscellaneous Provisions Regulations 1986 (“the 1986 Regulations”).

items.title: The Education (Abolition of Corporal Punishment) (Independent Schools) (Prescribed Categories of Persons) Regulations 1989
items.description: This version of the The Education (Abolition of Corporal Punishment) (Independent Schools) (Prescribed Categories of Persons) Regulations 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Civil Aviation Authority (Amendment) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations amend the Civil Aviation Authority Regulations 1983. The principal changes are–1Documents may now be served by telex or similar means (regulation 2(3));2Procedures are laid down for dealing with cases where an application is made for the substitution of a public use aerodrome licence for an ordinary aerodrome licence or of an ordinary aerodrome licence for a public use aerodrome licence (regulation 2(1), (4) and (5));3An application for the grant, variation, suspension or revocation of an air transport licence must now be republished at 12 monthly intervals if the case has not been set down for hearing: further objections and representations can be made on republication (regulation 2(10) and (12));4Provision is made for preliminary hearings of allegations of behaviour by the holder of an air transport licence which is damaging to a competitor, for provisional variation of the licence pending a substantive hearing and for an appeal to the Secretary of State against the Civil Aviation Authority’s decision following a provisional hearing (regulation 2(1), (2), (6), (7), (8), (9), (11), (13), (14) and (15));5When the Secretary of State, in determining an appeal from a decision of the Civil Aviation Authority in an air transport licensing case, directs the Authority to re-hear the case, he may now determine whether the Authority’s decision is to have effect pending the further decision (regulation 2(16)).

items.title: The Cholsey and Wallingford Light Railway Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The Cholsey and Wallingford Light Railway Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: Newport–Shrewsbury Trunk Road A4042 (Malpas Interchange to Cwmbran Drive, De-Trunking) Order 1989
items.description: This version of the Newport–Shrewsbury Trunk Road A4042 (Malpas Interchange to Cwmbran Drive, De-Trunking) Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The General Medical Council Professional Conduct Committee (EC Practitioners) (Procedure) Rules Order of Council 1989
items.description: The Rules approved by this Order set out the procedures for the investigation of certain complaints in respect of EC practitioners registered under the terms of the Medical Act 1983 and EC practitioners providing medical services on a temporary basis whilst visiting the United Kingdom.

items.title: The County Court (Amendment No. 3) Rules 1989
items.description: These Rules amend the County Court Rules 1981 in a number of respects.

items.title: The Acquisition of Land (Rate of Interest after Entry) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations increase from 116 per cent to 13 per cent per annum, in respect of any period after the coming into force of these Regulations, the rate of interest payable where entry is made, before payment of compensation, on land in England and Wales which is being purchased compulsorily, and revoke the Acquisition of Land (Rate of Interest after Entry) (No. 2) Regulations 1988.

items.title: The Merchant Shipping (Crew Accommodation) (Amendment) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations further amend the provisions of the Merchant Shipping (Crew Accommodation) Regulations 1978 to align them more closely with the International Labour Conventions Nos 92 and 133 on which they are based.

items.title: The Acquisition of Land (Rate of Interest after Entry) (Scotland) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations increase from 11⅞ per cent to 13 per cent. per annum, in respect of any period after the coming into force of these Regulations, the rate of interest payable where entry is made, before payment of compensation, on land in Scotland which is being purchased compulsorily, and revoke the Acquisition of Land (Rate of Interest after Entry) (Scotland) (No. 2) Regulations 1988.

items.title: The Consumer Credit (Exempt Agreements) (Amendment) Order 1989
items.description: This Order amends the Consumer Credit (Exempt Agreements) Order 1989, insofar as it provides exemption for the purposes of the Consumer Credit Act 1974 of certain consumer credit agreements where the creditor is a body specified in Schedule 1 to that Order, by amending the name of one body in Part I of that Schedule, and by adding to the list in Part III the names of three bodies and to the list in Part IV the name of one body.

items.title: The Wireless Telegraphy (Testing and Development Under Suppressed Radiation Conditions) (Exemption) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations provide for the exemption from the provisions of section 1(1) of the Wireless Telegraphy Act 1949 of stations or apparatus for wireless telegraphy operated under suppressed radiation conditions on specified frequencies for testing or development purposes (which are defined to include the modifying, servicing or repairing of such stations or apparatus and scientific research, training, instruction or experimentation in radio theory or practice). Accordingly, it will not be necessary to hold a licence to establish, instal and use such stations or apparatus.

items.title: The Road Traffic (Driver Licensing and Information Systems) Act 1989 (Commencement No. 1) Order 1989
items.description: This Order brings into force on 8th November 1989 the provisions of the Road Traffic (Driver Licensing and Information Systems) Act 1989 specified in the Schedule to the Order. Such provisions have particular relevance to the driving of public service vehicles by persons who are not licensed to drive such vehicles under section 22 of the Public Passenger Vehicles Act 1981 (c. 14) but who have made an application for the grant of a licence and who have passed the appropriate test of competence to drive.

items.title: The Public Service Vehicles (Temporary Driving Entitlement) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations prescribe certain periods of time for the purposes of paragraph 10 of Part II of Schedule 1 to the Road Traffic (Driver Licensing and Information Systems) Act 1989.

items.title: The East of Christchurch–Tredegar Park Motorway (Connecting Roads Special Roads) (Variation) Scheme 1989
items.description: This version of the The East of Christchurch–Tredegar Park Motorway (Connecting Roads Special Roads) (Variation) Scheme 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: M4/Newport-Shrewsbury Trunk Road A4042 (Relief of Brynglas Tunnels and Malpas Road) Order 1989
items.description: This version of the M4/Newport-Shrewsbury Trunk Road A4042 (Relief of Brynglas Tunnels and Malpas Road) Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Education (Grants for Training of Teachers and Community Education Workers) (Scotland) Regulations 1989
items.description: Under section 73 of the Education (Scotland) Act 1980 the Secretary of State may in accordance with regulations made by him make payment of such sums as appear to him appropriate for various purposes. Section 74(1) of the Act enables the Secretary of State to prescribe in regulations conditions and requirements with which the recipients of the grant must comply.

items.title: The Wireless Telegraphy (Licence Charges) Regulations 1989
items.description: (This Note is not part of the Regulations)

items.title: The Offshore Installations (Safety Zones) (No. 3) Order 1989
items.description: This Order establishes, under section 22 of the Petroleum Act 1987, a safety zone, having a radius of 500 metres from a specified point, around the installation specified in the Schedule to this Order and stationed in waters to which section 21(7) of that Act applies (these include territorial waters and waters in areas designated under section 1(7) of the Continental Shelf Act 1964 (c. 29)).

items.title: The Medicines (Prescription Only, Pharmacy and General Sale) Amendment Order 1989
items.description: This Order further amends the Medicines (Products Other Than Veterinary Drugs) (Prescription Only) Order 1983 (the POM Order) which specifies descriptions and classes of prescription only medicines subject to section 58(2) of the Medicines Act 1968, that is to say, medicinal products which (subject to exemptions) may be sold or supplied by retail only in accordance with a prescription given by an appropriate practitioner and which may be administered only by or in accordance with the direction of such a practitioner.

items.title: The Newtown-Aberystwyth Trunk Road (A489) (Mochdre Roundabout, Newtown) Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The Newtown-Aberystwyth Trunk Road (A489) (Mochdre Roundabout, Newtown) Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Road Vehicles (Construction and Use) (Amendment) (No. 3) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations amend the Road Vehicles (Construction and Use) Regulations 1986 (“the principal regulations”).

items.title: The Yorkshire Water Authority (Alteration of Boundaries of the Lower Ouse Internal Drainage District) Order 1989
items.description: This Order, made by the  Minister of Agriculture, Fisheries and Food, confirms, with a modification, a Scheme submitted to him by the Yorkshire Water Authority making provision for the alteration of the boundaries of the Lower Ouse Internal Drainage District, which District was constituted by the Yorkshire Ouse River Board (Lower Ouse Internal Drainage District) Order 1951 and the Yorkshire Ouse River Board (Lower Ouse Internal Drainage District) (Appointed Day) Order 1951 and for the abolition of the Electoral Divisions of the said Drainage District.

items.title: The East Lewis Salmon Fishery District Designation Order 1989
items.description: This Order creates in the Isle of Lewis a new salmon fishery district, to be known as the East Lewis Salmon Fishery District, by amalgamating the districts of the Rivers Creed or Stornoway and Laxay and of the River Greiss which are abolished (article 2 and the Schedule)

items.title: The Superannuation (Children’s Pensions) (Earnings Limit) Order 1989
items.description: Subject to certain conditions section 21 of the Judicial Pensions Act 1981 permits the payment of a children’s pension under that Act to continue after the child reaches the age of 16 if the child is undergoing training for a trade, profession or vocation. One of the conditions is that the emoluments received during training, excluding any emoluments receivable or payable by way of return of any premium paid in respect of the training, do not exceed £1227 a year. This Order revokes the Superannuation (Children’s Pensions) (Earnings Limit) Order 1988 and increases the limit on such emoluments from £1227 to £1299 a year.

items.title: The Ipswich–Norwich Trunk Road (A140) (Dickleburgh Bypass) Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The Ipswich–Norwich Trunk Road (A140) (Dickleburgh Bypass) Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Ipswich-Norwich Trunk Road (A140) (Dickleburgh Bypass) (Detrunking) Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The Ipswich-Norwich Trunk Road (A140) (Dickleburgh Bypass) (Detrunking) Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Carriage of Passengers and their Luggage by Sea (United Kingdom Carriers) (Amendment) Order 1989
items.description: This Order amends the Carriage of Passengers and their Luggage by Sea (United Kingdom Carriers) Order 1987 as a consequence of the coming into force of the 1976 Protocol to the Athens Convention relating to the Carriage of Passengers and their Luggage 1974 (which replaces the reference to gold francs in the Athens Convention by references to Special Drawing Rights). That Order provides a higher limit for United Kingdom carriers. This Order substitutes references to gold francs by appropriate references to “units of account”, (the definition and conversion into sterling of which are provided in paragraphs 3 and 4 of Part III of Schedule 3 to the Merchant Shipping Act 1979, which comes into force on the same day as this Order).

items.title: The Merchant Shipping Act 1979 (Commencement No. 13) Order 1989
items.description: The Order brings into force on 10th November 1989 section 35(2) of the Merchant Shipping Act 1979 so far as it is not in force—i.e. for fishing vessels. Section 35(2) prevents any person limiting his liability under section 17 of the Act for loss of life or personal injury or other damage caused to a person on a ship who is employed in connection with the ship or with salvage operations. By virtue of section 17 shipowners and salvors can limit their liability in accordance with the Convention on Limitation of Liability for Maritime Claims 1976 (set out in Schedule 4 to the 1979 Act).

items.title: The Merchant Shipping (Sterling Equivalents)(Merchant Shipping Act 1974) Order 1989
items.description: This Order specifies the sterling amounts which are to be taken as equivalent to the amounts expressed in gold francs in the Merchant Shipping Act 1974 (limitation of liability of, and indemnification of shipowners by, the International Oil Pollution Compensation Fund)

items.title: The National Health Service (General Medical and Pharmaceutical Services) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations further amend the National Health Service (General Medical and Pharmaceutical Services) (Scotland) Regulations 1974 (“the principal Regulations”) which provide the arrangements to be made by Health Boards for the provision in their areas of general medical services and pharmaceutical services.

items.title: The Health and Medicines Act 1988 (Superannuation) (Savings for Retired Practitioners) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations make savings in connection with the coming into force of section 9 of the Health and Medicines Act 1988 (“the Act”). That section revokes regulation 76 of the National Health Service (Superannuation) Regulations 1980 under which further employment as a medical or dental practitioner within the National Health Service, between the ages of 65 and 70 years, is disregarded for the purpose of determining whether there should be a reduction in a pension payable under those Regulations. Regulation 2 of these Regulations, however, enables those practitioners who, in the month preceding the coming into force of section 9 of the Act, were taking advantage of regulation 76 to continue to do so as if that provision had not been revoked.

items.title: The Health and Medicines Act 1988 (Commencement No.7) Order 1989
items.description: This Order brings into force in England and Wales on 15th October 1989 the provisions of the Health and Medicines Act 1988 relating to the retirement of medical practitioners.

items.title: The National Health Service (General Medical and Pharmaceutical Services) Amendment (No. 2) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations further amend the National Health Service (General Medical and Pharmaceutical Services) Regulations 1974 (“the principal Regulations”) which regulate the terms on which general medical and pharmaceutical services are provided under the National Health Service Act 1977 (“the 1977 Act”)

items.title: The Excise Duties (Personal Reliefs) (Fuel and Lubricants Imported in Vehicles) Order 1989
items.description: This Order provides for relief from excise duty on hydrocarbon oils of ready use fuel and lubricants carried for the propulsion unit and any refrigeration equipment fitted to motor vehicles (including railway engines) imported by and with any persons entering the United Kingdom.

items.title: The Criminal Appeal (Reviews of Sentencing) Rules 1989
items.description: These Rules make procedural provision for the Attorney General to exercise his power of referring to the Court of Appeal cases where it appears to him that the sentencing of an offender in the Crown Court for an offence triable on indictment only has been unduly lenient. Under Part IV of the Criminal Justice Act 1988 the Court of Appeal has power to review, and alter, the sentencing on any such reference.

items.title: The Land Registration (District Registries) Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The Land Registration (District Registries) Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Health and Safety (Enforcing Authority) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations re-enact the Health and Safety (Enforcing Authority) Regulations 1977 (“the 1977 Regulations”) with amendments. The 1977 Regulations made provision with respect to enforcement by local authorities of the Health and Safety at Work etc. Act 1974 (“the 1974 Act”) and the other relevant statutory provisions within the meaning of that Act. In addition to minor and drafting amendments, these Regulations make the following changes of substance:—

items.title: The Income Tax (Reserve and Auxiliary Forces) (Amendment) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations amend the Income Tax (Reserve and Auxiliary Forces) Regulations 1975 (S.I. 1975/91) (“the 1975 Regulations”) and come into force on 8th November 1989.

items.title: The Community Service Orders Rules 1989
items.description: These Rules regulate the performance of work under community service orders and the arrangements for persons to perform such work. Rule 2 requires the arrangements made for community service work to include the provision of information for those required to perform it; and rule 3 requires those arrangements to make provision for certain kinds of work. Rule 4 requires work under a community service order normally to be commenced soon after the making of the order. Rules 5, 6 and 7 make provision as to the reckoning of time worked under such an order. Rule 8 establishes procedures to ensure the satisfactory performance of work. Rule 9 provides for the maintenance of records of work performed.

items.title: The Public Telecommunication System Designation (Cable Camden Limited) Order 1989
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to Cable Camden Limited on 7th August 1989 a licence under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in Annex A to that licence, in the London Borough of Camden. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 19th October 1989. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently, by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, Cable Camden Limited will be a public telecommunications operator when the Order comes into force.

items.title: The Public Telecommunication System Designation (CableVision Bedfordshire Limited) Order 1989
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to CableVision Bedfordshire Limited on 23rd August 1989 a licence under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in Annex A to that licence, in the District of South Bedfordshire and the Borough of Luton. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 19th October 1989. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently, by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, CableVision Bedfordshire Limited will be a public telecommunications operator when the Order comes into force.

items.title: The Export of Goods (Control) (Amendment No. 5) Order 1989
items.description: This Order further amends the Export of Goods (Control) Order 1987.

items.title: The County Court (Forms) (Amendment No. 2) Rules 1989
items.description: This version of the The County Court (Forms) (Amendment No. 2) Rules 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Medicines Act 1968 (Commencement No. 8) Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The Medicines Act 1968 (Commencement No. 8) Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Severn Bridge Tolls Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The Severn Bridge Tolls Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Highland Regional Council (Allt Bail'an Tuim Bhuidhe) Water Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The Highland Regional Council (Allt Bail'an Tuim Bhuidhe) Water Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Fair Employment (Northern Ireland) Act 1989 (Commencement) Order 1989
items.description: This Order brings into force on 1st January 1990 all of the provisions of the Fair Employment (Northern Ireland) Act 1989 except those provisions specified in article 2(2) which are brought into force on 1st November 1989. The commencement does not apply for the purposes set out in article 2(3) and (4).

items.title: The Wireless Telegraphy (Licence Charges) (Amendment) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations amend the Wireless Telegraphy (Licence Charges) Regulations 1989 (S.I. 1989/1850) in relation to Maritime Business Radio and Private Mobile Radio (Standard) Licences.

items.title: The Town and Country Planning (Fees for Applications and Deemed Applications) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations consolidate, with amendments, the Town and Country Planning (Fees for Applications and Deemed Applications) Regulations 1983, the Town and Country Planning (Fees for Applications and Deemed Applications) (Amendment) Regulations 1985 and the Town and Country Planning (Fees for Applications and Deemed Applications) (Amendment) Regulations 1987. They make provision for the payment of fees to local planning authorities in respect of applications made under Part III of the Town and Country Planning Act 1971 for planning permission for development or for approval of matters reserved by an outline planning permission and in respect of applications for consent for the display of advertisements; and for the payment of fees to the Secretary of State in respect of applications for planning permission which are deemed to have been made, by virtue of the provisions of section 88B(3) or 95(6) of the Act of 1971, in connection with an appeal against an enforcement notice or in connection with an application for an established use certificate.

items.title: The Income Tax (Interest Relief) (Qualifying Lenders) (No. 2) Order 1989
items.description: By article 2, the bodies named in article 3 are prescribed as qualifying lenders so that interest on qualifying loans made by them may be paid to them under deduction of tax under the mortgage interest relief at source scheme (MIRAS) (introduced by the Finance Act 1982 (c. 39)) from the dates specified in the Order.

items.title: The Sale of Registration Marks Regulations 1989
items.description: The scheme made by these Regulations provides for registration marks to which section 12 of the Finance Act 1989 applies to be assigned to vehicles registered in the names of, or of the nominees of, persons who have acquired rights under the scheme to have the marks so assigned.

items.title: The Water (Pensions and Compensation) (Transfer of Liabilities) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations are consequential upon the Water Act 1989. They transfer to the holding companies of the companies which succeeded the water authorities two sets of liabilities of those successor companies. The first set relates to the payment of pensions to chairmen and members of water authorities. The second set relates to the payment of compensation to such persons when they cease to be water authority members. These liabilities were excluded from the general transfer of pension and compensation liabilities made by the Water Reorganisation (Pensions etc.) Regulations 1989 (S.I. 1989/1161).

items.title: The Adoption Allowance Schemes (Scotland) Order 1989
items.description: Section 51 of the Adoption (Scotland) Act 1978 prohibits the making of payments in connection with certain adoption procedures and arrangements. Subsection (5) of section 51 disapplies the provisions of that section where a scheme for payment has been submitted to the Secretary of State by an adoption agency and he has approved the scheme. Section 51(8) provides for subsection (5) to expire on the seventh anniversary of section 32 of the Children Act 1975 (c. 72) coming into force unless the Secretary of State repeals subsection (8)prior to the said anniversary. (Section 32 of that Act was commenced on 15th February 1982 by the Children Act 1975 (Scotland) (Commencement No. 3) Order 1982 (S.I. 1982/33)).

items.title: The Offshore Installations (Life-saving Appliances and Fire-fighting Equipment) (Amendment) Regulations 1989
items.description: The Offshore Installations (Life-saving Appliances) Regulations 1977 prohibit the presence of any person on an installation to which the Regulations apply unless the life-saving appliances on that installation have been examined by a person acting at the direction of the Secretary of State. The Offshore Installations (Fire-fighting Equipment) Regulations 1978 contain a similar prohibition in respect of the fire-fighting equipment provided on an offshore installation. Both the 1977 and the 1978 Regulations require the owner of an installation on which an examination is carried out to pay a fee to the Secretary of State, comprising a sum based on the time spent by the examiner in carrying out the examination and in travelling to and from the installation, calculated in accordance with a table set out in the Schedule to the Regulations, and the cost of his travelling and subsistence expenses. These Regulations revoke previous Amendment Regulations, and substitute in the 1977 Regulations and the 1978 Regulations a new table specifying the rates payable in respect of the time spent by an examiner. These new rates replace those having effect by virtue of the Offshore Installations (Life-saving Appliances and Fire-fighting Equipment) (Amendment) Regulations 1987 which were as follows–Time spentRate per hour or part thereofOn any day except a Saturday, Sunday or public holiday–between 8 a.m. and 6 p.m.£45.00before 8 a.m. or after 6 p.m.£68.25On a Saturday£68.25On a Sunday or public holiday£91.00

items.title: The Cowes Harbour Revision Order 1989
items.description: This Order amends the borrowing powers of the Commissioners for the Harbour of Cowes contained in section 46 of the Cowes Harbour Act 1897 and, in particular, empowers the Commissioners to borrow sums not exceeding £1 million.

items.title: The Plant Health (Forestry) (Great Britain) (Amendment) Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The Plant Health (Forestry) (Great Britain) (Amendment) Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Insurance Companies (Accounts and Statements) (Amendment) Regulations 1989
The Condensed Milk and Dried Milk (Amendment) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations, which apply to England and Wales only, come into force on 24th November 1989. They amend the Condensed Milk and Dried Milk Regulations 1977 by introducing a provision that the methods of sampling for analysis of condensed milk products (including evaporated milk) and dried milk products shall be those described in the Annex to First Commission Directive 87/524/EEC (Regulation 2), which sets out (unlike the Food Act 1984) the equipment required to be used and the manner of its use.

items.title: The Sea Fishing (Enforcement of Community Quota Measures) (Amendment) Order 1989
items.description: This Order amends the Sea Fishing (Enforcement of Community Quota Measures) Order 1988 (“the principal Order”), which makes provision for the enforcement of certain of the enforceable Community obligations relating to sea fishing which are contained in Council Regulation (EEC) No. 4194/88 (“the Council Regulation”). The Council Regulation fixes total allowable catches and quotas for 1989.

items.title: The Poultry Breeding Flocks and Hatcheries (Registration and Testing) Order 1989
items.description: This Order prohibits (with two exceptions) a person from keeping a breeding flock on any premises or from using any premises as a hatchery unless his name is entered in the Breeding Flocks Register or in the Hatcheries Register in respect of those premises, both such registers being kept by the appropriate Minister (the Minister of Agriculture, Fisheries and Food or the Secretary of State for Scotland or Wales) for the purposes of the Order. Such registration will remain in force for a period of one year and will thereafter be renewed annually by the appropriate Minister unless following an inspection carried out on the premises, he is satisfied that the registered person has failed to comply with certain specified requirements relating to the keeping of a breeding flock on the premises or to the use of the premises as a hatchery (articles 3 and 4 and Schedules 1 and 2).

items.title: The Poultry Laying Flocks (Testing and Registration etc.) Order 1989
items.description: This Order which revokes the Testing of Poultry Flocks Order 1989 and the Poultry Laying Flocks (Collection and Handling of Eggs and Control of Vermin) Order 1989 requires the owner or person in charge of a laying flock, that is to say a flock of poultry consisting of not less than 25 birds which are kept for the production of eggs for human consumption (including birds reared for that purpose) or of less than 25 birds the eggs of which are sold for human consumption, to ensure that samples are taken in respect of the flock and are submitted to a laboratory for testing for the presence of salmonella (article 3 and Schedule 1).

items.title: The Water (Consequential Amendments) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations are consequential on the Water Act 1989. Regulation 2 amends the public general enactments listed in Schedule 1 and regulation 3 amends the items of subordinate legislation listed in Schedule 2.

items.title: The Fishguard–Bangor Trunk Road A487 (Port Dinorwic By-Pass) Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The Fishguard–Bangor Trunk Road A487 (Port Dinorwic By-Pass) Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Condensed Milk and Dried Milk (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations, which apply to Scotland only, amend the Condensed Milk and Dried Milk (Scotland) Regulations 1977 by introducing a provision that the methods of sampling for analysis of condensed milk products (including evaporated milk) and dried milk products shall be those described in the Annex to First Commission Directive 87/524/EEC, which sets out (unlike the Food and Drugs (Scotland) Act 1956) the equipment required to be used and the manner of its use, (regulation 2).

items.title: The Borough of Warrington (Electoral Arrangements) Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The Borough of Warrington (Electoral Arrangements) Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Unitary Development Plans (South Yorkshire and Hounslow) (Appointed Day) Order 1989
items.description: The Local Government Act 1985 abolished the Greater London Council and metropolitan county councils. The Act consequently provides for the replacement in their areas of the existing structure and local development plans under town and country planning legislation by “unitary development plans”. This Order brings the provisions of the 1985 Act relating to unitary development plans into force in the county of South Yorkshire other than the metropolitan districts of Barnsley and Rotherham, and in the London Borough of Hounslow, on 28th November 1989.

items.title: The (A453) North East of Birmingham–Nottingham Trunk Road (Diversion at Long Hedge Lane) Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The (A453) North East of Birmingham–Nottingham Trunk Road (Diversion at Long Hedge Lane) Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Health and Medicines Act 1988 (Commencement No.8) Order 1989
items.description: This Order brings into force on 31st October 1989 the provisions of the Health and Medicines Act 1988 for which days for coming into force have not already been appointed.

items.title: The Medicines (Pharmacies) (Applications for Registration and Fees) Amendment Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Medicines (Pharmacies) (Applications for Registration and Fees) Regulations 1973 (“the principal regulations”). They increase the fees for registration of premises at which a retail pharmacy business is or is to be carried on, the subsequent annual fees (retention fees) and the penalty (payable in circumstances specified in section 76(2) of the Medicines Act 1968) for failure to pay the retention fee.

items.title: The Social Security (Industrial Injuries and Diseases) Miscellaneous Provisions (Amendment) (No. 2) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations, which come into force on 31st October 1989, amend the Social Security (Industrial Injuries and Diseases) Miscellaneous Provisions (Amendment) Regulations 1989 by deleting from regulation 2 of those Regulations paragraph (2) which provides that regulation 13 of the Social Security (Industrial Injuries and Diseases) Miscellaneous Provisions Regulations 1986 (S.I. 1986/1561) is to cease to have effect on 1st November 1989. Regulation 13 enables late claims for industrial injuries and diseases to be determined in certain circumstances as though they had been made on 30th September 1986.

items.title: The (A453) North East of Birmingham–Nottingham Trunk Road The Birmingham–Nottingham Route (A42 Ashby-de-la-Zouch to Kegworth Section Slip Roads) Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The (A453) North East of Birmingham–Nottingham Trunk Road The Birmingham–Nottingham Route (A42 Ashby-de-la-Zouch to Kegworth Section Slip Roads) Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The National Health Service (General Medical and Pharmaceutical Services) (Scotland) Amendment (No.2) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations further amend the National Health Service (General Medical and Pharmaceutical Services) (Scotland) Regulations 1974 (“the principal Regulations”) which regulate the terms on which general medical and pharmaceutical services are provided under the National Health Service (Scotland) Act 1978 (“the 1978 Act”).

items.title: The Merchant Shipping (Ministry of Defence Ships) Order 1989
items.description: This Order makes provision for the registration under the Merchant Shipping Acts 1894–1988 of ships belonging to the Secretary of State for Defence and in the service of the Ministry of Defence, and for certain modifications and exceptions in the application of those Acts to such ships.

items.title: The Local Authorities (Armorial Bearings) (No. 2) Order 1989
items.description: This Order authorises Barnstaple town council to bear and use the armorial bearings of the former Barnstaple borough council.

items.title: The Falkland Islands (Territorial Sea) Order 1989
items.description: This order extends the boundaries of the Falkland Islands, so as to include, as territorial sea, the sea within twelve nautical miles of the baselines, together with its seabed and subsoil, and makes other provisions in this connection. In particular, it defines the baseline from which the breadth of the territorial sea is measured as generally the low-water line, except that a series of straight baselines joining specified points is provided for. The effect of the Order is to establish around all of the Falkland Islands (including Beauchene Island) a territorial sea extending to 12 nautical miles from the appropriate baselines.

items.title: The St. Helena and Dependencies (Territorial Sea) Order 1989
items.description: This Order extends the boundaries of the Colony of St. Helena and its Dependencies so as to include, as territorial sea, the sea within twelve miles of the baselines of St. Helena, Ascension, Triston da Cunha, Gough Island, Nightingale Island and Inaccessible Island, together with its seabed and subsoil, and makes other provisions in this connection. In particular, it defines the baseline from which the breadth of the territorial sea is measured as generally the low water line, except where there are bays.

items.title: The South Georgia and South Sandwich Islands (Territorial Sea) Order 1989
items.description: This Order extends the boundaries of South Georgia and South Sandwich Islands, so as to include, as territorial sea, the sea within twelve nautical miles of the baselines, together with its seabed and subsoil, and makes other provisions in this connection. In particular, it defines the baseline from which the breadth of the territorial sea is measured as generally the low-water line, except that around South Georgia and other islands in its immediate vicinity a series of straight baselines joining specified points is provided for. The effect of the Order is to establish around South Georgia (including Shag Rocks, Black Rock, Clerke Rocks and the Office Boys) and all islands in the South Sandwich Islands a territorial sea extending to 12 nautical miles from the appropriate baselines.

items.title: The Turks and Caicos Islands (Territorial Sea) Order 1989
items.description: This Order extends the boundaries of the Turks and Caicos Islands so as to include, as territorial sea, the sea within twelve nautical miles of the baselines, together with its seabed and subsoil, and makes other provisions in this connection.
