In particular, it defines the baseline from which the breadth of the territorial sea is measured as generally the low water line, but between North West Point, Providenciales and Company Point, West Caicos; between South West Point, West Caicos and Toney Rock – SE; and between Toney Rock – NE and Drum Point, East Caicos a series of straight lines joining specified points lying on the seaward side of the Turks and Caicos group of islands are used.

items.title: The Copyright, Designs and Patents Act 1988 (Guernsey) Order 1989
items.description: This Order extends to the Bailiwick of Guernsey, with the exceptions and modifications specified in the Schedule, sections 297 to 299 of the Copyright, Designs and Patents Act 1988 (which relate to fraudulent reception of transmissions).

items.title: The Fuel and Electricity (Control) Act 1973 (Continuation) (Jersey) Order 1989
items.description: This Order continues provisions of the Fuel and Electricity (Control) Act 1973 in force in the Bailiwick of Jersey for one year from 30th November 1989 (being the date to which those provisions were continued in force by the Fuel and Electricity (Control) Act 1973 (Continuation) (Jersey) Order 1988 (S.I. 1988/1848)).

items.title: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Wales) (No.5) Amendment Order 1989
items.description: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Wales) (No.5) Order 1987, as amended by S.I. 1988/9, 951, 1329 and 1680 contains emergency prohibitions restricting various activities in order to prevent human consumption of food which has been or which may have been rendered unsuitable for that purpose in consequence of the escape of radioactive substances from a nuclear reactor situated at Chernobyl in the USSR.

items.title: The (A453) North East of Birmingham–Nottingham Trunk Road The Birmingham–Nottingham Route (A42 Ashby-de-la-Zouch to Kegworth Section) Order 1986 Variation Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The (A453) North East of Birmingham–Nottingham Trunk Road The Birmingham–Nottingham Route (A42 Ashby-de-la-Zouch to Kegworth Section) Order 1986 Variation Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Judicial Committee (Fees) Order 1989
items.description: This Order introduces a revised scale of fees in respect of Appeals and other proceedings before the Judicial Committee of the Privy Council. In general, the fees, which were last fixed in 1981, are being increased by 50%, although more substantial increases are made to the fees for lodging a case or a petition for special leave to appeal. The range of fees has been rationalised, reducing the table from 24 to 18 items.

items.title: The Social Security (Isle of Man) (No. 2) Order 1989
items.description: This Order gives effect to an Agreement made between the Secretary of State for Social Security with the concurrence of the Department of Health and Social Services for Northern Ireland of the one part and the Lieutenant-Governor of the Isle of Man of the other which permits each party to the Agreement to pay minimum contributions to personal pension schemes and to make payments to occupational pension schemes in the territory of the other party.

items.title: The Social Security (Philippines) Order 1989
items.description: This Order makes provision for the modification of the Social Security Act 1975 so as to give effect to the Convention on social security (set out in the Schedule) made between the Government of the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland and the Government of the Republic of the Philippines. The Convention covers United Kingdom benefits for old age, survivors (widows) and industrial accidents and corresponding benefits under the social security scheme in the Philippines.

items.title: The Fraudulent Reception of Transmissions (Guernsey) Order 1989
items.description: This Order applies the provisions of sections 297 and 298 of the Copyright, Designs and Patents Act 1988, which relate to the unauthorised reception of programmes included in a broadcasting or cable programme service and of encrypted transmissions, to programmes and encrypted transmissions from Guernsey.

items.title: The Air Navigation Order 1989
items.description: This Order consolidates the Air Navigation Order 1985, as amended. In addition to some minor drafting amendments the following changes are made:1A glider which is flying for the purpose of aerial work which consists of the giving of instruction in flying or the conducting of flying tests in circumstances where the glider is owned or operated by a flying club of which the person giving the instruction or conducting the test and the person receiving the instruction or undergoing the test are both members, need not be registered and need not have a certificate of airworthiness. A flight crew licence is not required by a member of the flight crew of a glider flying for the purpose of aerial work which consists of conducting flying tests in the same circumstances. If valuable consideration is given or promised for the purpose of conferring on a member of a flying club the right to fly a glider owned or operated by the flying club it shall not thereby be deemed to be a flight for the purpose of public transport for airworthiness purposes (articles 3(1), 7(1), 20(8) and 107(2)).2The Authority must now cancel the registration of an aircraft within 2 months of being satisfied that there has been a change of ownership. This reflects the present practice whereby a period of grace is allowed to enable the new owner to register (article 4(13)).3When a copy of the technical log or load sheet is permitted to be carried in an aircraft instead of being left on the ground it may now be so carried in any container approved by the Authority for that purpose (articles 10(4) and 29(5)).4With effect from 4th December 1989, no further Senior Commercial Pilot’s Licences (Aeroplanes) (SCPL) shall be granted by the Authority (except renewals for persons holding a SCPL on 3rd December 1989). Any SCPLs which would not otherwise expire on or before 3rd December 1994 will be deemed to expire on that date (article 21(2) and Schedule 8).5The privileges of a Basic Commercial Pilot’s Licence (Aeroplanes), a Commercial Pilot’s Licence (Aeroplanes) and a Commercial Pilot’s Licence (Helicopters and Gyroplanes) are being altered. Whereas the holder of a BCPL(A) has hitherto been entitled in certain cases to act as co-pilot of an aircraft flying for the purpose of public transport only if its maximum total weight authorised is 5700kg. or less and the holder of a CPL(A) or a CPL(H) has hitherto been entitled to act as pilot in command of an aircraft flying for the purpose of public transport only if its maximum total weight authorised is 5700kg. or less, he will now be entitled to act as co-pilot or pilot in command (as the case may be) of such an aircraft only if it is certificated for single pilot operation. The date on which the altered privileges come into force is determined as follows:aany such licence initially issued on or after 4th December 1989 shall have the altered privileges from the outset;bpersons holding such a licence on 3rd December 1989 will not be affected by the altered privileges during the validity of that licence or any renewal thereof until 3rd December 1994 whereafter the altered privileges will apply.

items.title: The Collision Regulations (Seaplanes) Order 1989
items.description: This Order applies to seaplanes on the water which are registered in the United Kingdom wherever they may be and to other seaplanes on the water while they are within the United Kingdom or the territorial waters thereof. It revokes the Collision Rules (Seaplanes) Order 1983; and provides that regulation 1(2) (interpretation) (with modifications) and the International Regulations set out in Schedule 1 to the Merchant Shipping (Distress Signals and Prevention of Collisions) Regulations 1989 (S.I. 1989/1798) shall have effect (with the exception of Annex IV) as the Collision Regulations for the purposes of sections 418, 419 and 421 of the Merchant Shipping Act 1894 as extended to seaplanes on the surface of the water.

items.title: The Merchant Shipping Act 1988 (Amendment) Order 1989
items.description: This Order gives effect to an interim order of the President of the European Court of Justice in Commission v United Kingdom (Case 246/89R), made on 10th October 1989. The interim order requires the United Kingdom, pending delivery of the judgment of the Court in the main proceedings, (Case 246/89), to suspend the British citizenship requirements (but not the United Kingdom residence and domicile requirements) of section 14 of the Merchant Shipping Act 1988 so far as they relate to fishing vessels which were fishing under the British flag up to March 31st 1989 and were owned by nationals of member States of the European Communities. Pending publication of the Order in the Official Journal of the European Communities, copies of the order may be obtained from the Treasury Solicitor (European Section), Queen Anne’s Chambers, 28 Broadway, London SW1H 9JS.

items.title: The Shrewsbury–Whitchurch–Warrington Trunk Road (A49, Tarporley Road, Stretton) (Trunking) Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The Shrewsbury–Whitchurch–Warrington Trunk Road (A49, Tarporley Road, Stretton) (Trunking) Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Financial Services (Disclosure of Information) (Designated Authorities) (No. 6) Order 1989
items.description: This Order designates the Director General of Fair Trading and the Monopolies and Mergers Commission as authorities for the purposes of section 180 of the Financial Services Act 1986 in relation to specified functions, and the Director General as an authority for the purposes of section 449 of the Companies Act 1985 in relation to specified functions.

items.title: The Crick (Northamptonshire) to Doncaster Bypass Motorway Connecting Roads (No 2) Special Roads Supplementary Scheme 1986 Variation Scheme 1989
items.description: This version of the The Crick (Northamptonshire) to Doncaster Bypass Motorway Connecting Roads (No 2) Special Roads Supplementary Scheme 1986 Variation Scheme 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The A406 Trunk Road (South Woodford to Barking Relief Road, Redbridge, Newham, Barking and Dagenham) (Prescribed Routes) Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The A406 Trunk Road (South Woodford to Barking Relief Road, Redbridge, Newham, Barking and Dagenham) (Prescribed Routes) Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Bristol Development Corporation (Vesting of Land) (British Railways Board) Order 1989
items.description: This Order vests in the Bristol Development Corporation certain land which is at present vested in the British Railways Board (or in another body mentioned in section 141(1) of the Local Government, Planning and Land Act 1980), and related easements and similar rights. The land is shown coloured black on the map forming part of this note.

items.title: The Feeding Stuffs (Amendment) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations amend the Feeding Stuffs Regulations 1988. Regulation 3 makes a number of minor amendments to the body of the principal Regulations, and regulation 4 amends Schedules 1, 4 and 7 thereto, which relate respectively to the contents of the statutory statement to be given by the seller to the purchaser on the sale of any feeding stuff, to permitted additives in feeding stuffs and to the control of certain protein sources.

items.title: The Sea Fish Licensing Order 1989
items.description: This Order consolidates with an amendment, and replaces, the Sea Fish Licensing Order 1983, as amended, “the 1983 Order” which prohibited fishing, subject to exceptions, by British fishing boats (including boats registered in the Isle of Man and the Channel Islands) in specified areas of sea for the principal species of sea fish, unless authorised by a licence. That prohibition on fishing did not apply to fishing by any boat whose length was not more than 10 metres, except in the case of a boat fishing for herring in specified areas. This Order amends the definition of “length” to that calculated in accordance with the rules specified in Article 2(1) of Council Regulation (EEC) No. 2930/86 (OJ No. L274, 25.9.86, p. 1 (article 2).

items.title: The Passenger and Goods Vehicles (Recording Equipment) (Approval of Fitters and Workshops) (Fees) (Amendment) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations amend the Passenger and Goods Vehicles (Recording Equipment) (Approval of Fitters and Workshops) (Fees) Regulations 1986 to increase the fees for the approval of fitters or workshops for the installation or repair of recording equipment (Tachographs). The fees were last increased in December 1988.

items.title: The Water Reorganisation (Capital Allowances) Order 1989
items.description: Section 2(3) of the Capital Allowances Act 1968 provides for the calculation of the writing-down allowance in respect of a period which ends after the sale of the relevant interest in an industrial building or structure by reference to the residue of certain expenditure. The writing-down allowance for a chargeable period is the residue reduced in the proportion which the length of the chargeable period bears to the unexpired part, at the date of sale, of the period of 25 or, in certain cases, 50 years beginning with the date on which the building or structure was first used.

items.title: The Water Reorganisation (Nominated Holding Companies) (Extinguishment of Loans) Order 1989
items.description: On 1st September 1989 certain financial liabilities of the companies which succeeded the water authorities were transferred, by virtue of the Water Reorganisation (Successor Companies) (Transfer of Loans) Order 1989 (S.I. 1989/1532), to those companies' nominated holding companies. This Order extinguishes those transferred liabilities on 15th November 1989. It also extinguishes on that date all liabilities of the successor companies under debentures issued in respect of the transferred liabilities. The Order also revokes S.I. 1989/1532.

items.title: The North East of Birmingham–Nottingham Trunk Road (Breedon-On-The-Hill Bypass) Order 1957 Revocation Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The North East of Birmingham–Nottingham Trunk Road (Breedon-On-The-Hill Bypass) Order 1957 Revocation Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Renfrew and Cunninghame Districts (Caldwell House Estate) Boundaries Amendment Order 1989
items.description: This Order transfers an area of approximately 103 hectares at Caldwell House Estate from Cunninghame District to Renfrew District and makes consequential changes to electoral arrangements.

items.title: The Housing Act 1988 (Commencement No. 3) Order 1989
items.description: This Order brings into force on 10th March 1989 section 122 (variation of cost floor for right to buy discount) and section 124 (right to buy: tenant’s sanction for landlord’s delays) of the Housing Act 1988, together with a minor amendment to the Housing Act 1985 (c. 68) related to section 124.

items.title: The Public Telecommunication System Designation (Merseyside Cablevision Limited) Order 1989
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to Merseyside Cablevision Limited on 14th October 1989 a licence under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in Annex A to that licence, in the Southern parts of the Boroughs of Liverpool and Knowsley, as described in the map which forms Annex B to that licence. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 6th November 1989. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, Merseyside Cablevision Limited will be a public telecommunications operator when the Order comes into force.

items.title: The Public Telecommunication System Designation (Lancashire Cable Television Limited) Order 1989
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to Lancashire Cable Television Limited on 14th October 1989 a licence under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in Annex A to that licence, in the whole of the District of South Ribble, the greater parts of the Districts of Preston, Chorley and Fylde and a small part of the District of Ribble Valley, as described in the map which forms Annex B to that licence. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 6th November 1989. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently, by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, Lancashire Cable Television Limited will be a public telecommunications operator when the Order comes into force.

items.title: The Boston Harbour Revision Order 1989
items.description: This Order designates Port of Boston Limited as the harbour authority for the Boston port, harbour and dock, and transfers to and vests in Port of Boston Limited the Boston port, harbour and dock undertaking together with all rights, interests and privileges presently vested in or used or enjoyed by the Boston Borough Council in relation to that undertaking. Provision is made for all purchases, sales, conveyances, deeds, contracts and agreements entered into or made by Boston Borough Council in relation to that undertaking to remain binding and in full force against or in favour of the Company and for all legal or other proceedings in relation to that undertaking to be carried on with the substitution of the Company as a party to the proceedings in lieu of the Council.

items.title: The Education (Areas to which Pupils and Students Belong) Regulations 1989
items.description: This note is not part of the Regulation)

items.title: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Contamination of Feeding Stuff) (England) Order 1989
items.description: This Order contains emergency prohibitions restricting various activities in order to prevent human consumption of food rendered unsuitable for that purpose in consequence of the escape of lead into imported animal feeding stuff.

items.title: The Highland Regional Council (Loch Beannach) Water Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The Highland Regional Council (Loch Beannach) Water Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The National Savings Bank (Amendment) (No. 2) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations amend the National Savings Bank Regulations 1972 with effect from 1st January 1990 to enable accounts to be opened only by a class of depositors which is narrower than the class presently entitled to open accounts under the Regulations. The maximum number of joint holders is reduced to two; trust accounts under regulation 5(2) may be opened where there are not more than two trustees, and not more than two beneficiaries both of whom must be individuals; trust accounts under regulation 9(1) may be opened where the beneficiary is a sole individual; and friendly societies, building societies, industrial and provident societies, charitable and other societies and corporations, will cease to be entitled to open accounts (other than as trustees). The Regulations also provide that in relation to accounts opened on or after 1st January 1990 deposits held by a minor under seven may be repaid to any person who satisfies the Director of Savings that he is a proper person to receive payments.

items.title: The National Savings Stock Register (Amendment) (No. 2) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations amend the National Savings Stock Register Regulations 1976.

items.title: The Certification Officer (Amendment of Fees) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations, which come into force on 1st April 1989, increase the fee payable to the Certification Officer on an application for approval of a proposed instrument of amalgamation or transfer from £175 to £200 and the fee payable to him on an application by a trade union for a certificate of independence from £194 to £225.

items.title: The Movement of Animals (Records) (Amendment) (No. 2) Order 1989
items.description: This Order further amends the Movement of Animals (Records) Order 1960. It provides that the references in the required form of movement record to an ear-mark or an ear-tag applied to bovine animals shall include a reference to any mark or other means of identification applied for the purposes of the Tuberculosis (Scotland) Order 1984 as well as to any mark or other means of identification applied for the purposes of the Tuberculosis (England and Wales) Order 1984 (S.I. 1924/1943) (article 2).

items.title: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Contamination of Feeding Stuff) (England) Amendment Order 1989
items.description: This Order amends the Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Contamination of Feeding Stuff) (England) Order 1989 (“the principal Order”) which contains emergency prohibitions restricting various activities in order to prevent human consumption of food rendered unsuitable for that purpose in consequence of the escape of lead into imported animal feeding stuff.

items.title: The Education (Designated Institutions) (Amendment) Order 1989
items.description: This Order corrects a defect in the Education (Designated Institutions) Order 1989 (S.I. 1989/282), which came into force on 1st April 1989. That Order designated certain institutions under section 129 of the Education Reform Act 1988 as eligible to receive funds administered by the Polytechnics and Colleges Funding Council, and applied section 130 of that Act to them.

items.title: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Contamination of Feeding Stuff) (Wales) Order 1989
items.description: This Order contains emergency prohibitions restricting various activities in order to prevent human consumption of food rendered unsuitable for that purpose in consequence of the escape of lead into imported animal feeding stuff.

items.title: The Motor Cars (Driving Instruction) Regulations 1989
items.description: These regulations re-enact the Motor Cars (Driving Instruction) Regulations 1977 with modifications. In addition to minor and drafting amendments, they make the following changes of substance.

items.title: The Goods Vehicles (Authorisation of International Journeys) (Fees) (Amendment) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations amend the Goods Vehicles (Authorisation of International Journeys) (Fees) Regulations 1983 as previously amended by the Goods Vehicles (Authorisation of International Journeys) (Fees) (Amendment) Regulations 1987. The effect of the amendments is to reduce the fees payable on the issue of certain authorisations, licences and permits required for international road haulage journeys as follows –Valid for a period of one yearValid for a period of less than one yearcommunity authorisationA decrease from £100 to £80A decrease from £26 to £20 for each period of three months or part therof for which it is valid.E.C.M.T. licenceA decrease from £100 to £80.A decrease from £26 to £20 for each period of three months or part thereof for which it is valid.period permitA decrease from £50 to £40.A decrease from £14 to £10 for each period of three months or part thereof for which it is valid.

items.title: The London–King’s Lynn Trunk Road (A10) (Brandon Creek – Southery Improvement) Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The London–King’s Lynn Trunk Road (A10) (Brandon Creek – Southery Improvement) Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Access to Personal Files (Social Services) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations provide for individuals to have access to information relating to themselves held by local social services authorities.

items.title: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Contamination of Feeding Stuff) (England) Amendment No.2 Order 1989
items.description: This Order further amends the Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Contamination of Feeding Stuff) (England) Order 1989 (“the principal Order”) which contains emergency prohibitions restricting various activities in order to prevent human consumption of food rendered unsuitable for that purpose in consequence of the escape of lead into imported animal feeding stuff.

items.title: The Bovine Offal (Prohibition) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations, which apply to England and Wales only, come into force on 13th November 1989.

items.title: The Civil Aviation (Investigation of Air Accidents) Regulations 1989
items.description: This version of the The Civil Aviation (Investigation of Air Accidents) Regulations 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Building Societies (Deferred Shares) Order 1989
items.description: This Order is made under section 119(1) of the Building Societies Act 1986 which provides that “deferred shares” means shares of a class defined by order. Definition of such a class has two primary effects. Before a society is authorised under section 9 of the Act to raise funds and borrow money it may nevertheless accept payments by way of subscription for deferred shares up to limits specified in section 9. By virtue of section 27(8) of the Act deferred shares are not to count as protected investments for the purposes of the investor protection fund set up under Part IV of the Act.

items.title: The Public Telecommunication System Designation (Birmingham Cable Limited) Order 1989
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to Birmingham Cable Limited on 2nd November 1989 a licence under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in Annex A to that licence, in the whole of the City of Birmingham and parts of the Solihull and Sandwell Metropolitan Boroughs and Bromsgrove District Borough. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 13th November 1989. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently, by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, Birmingham Cable Limited will be a public telecommunications operator when the Order comes into force.

items.title: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Contamination of Feeding Stuff) (England) (No. 2) Order 1989
items.description: This Order consolidates with amendments the Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Contamination of Feeding Stuff) (England) Order 1989, as amended. It contains emergency prohibitions restricting various activities in order to prevent human consumption of food rendered unsuitable for that purpose in consequence of the escape of lead into imported animal feeding stuff.

items.title: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Contamination of Feeding Stuff) (Wales) Amendment Order 1989
items.description: This Order amends the Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Contamination of Feeding Stuff) (Wales) Order 1989 (“The principal Order”) which contains emergency prohibitions restricting various activities in order to prevent human consumption of food rendered unsuitable for that purpose in consequence of the escape of lead into imported animal feeding stuff.

items.title: The Building Societies (Designated Capital Resources) (Deferred Shares) Order 1989
items.description: This Order specifies that where a building society has issued shares (“old deferred shares”) in accordance with the terms of regulations made under the Building Societies Act 1962 or shares (“new deferred shares”) in accordance with the terms prescribed for deferred shares by the Building Societies (Deferred Shares) Order 1989 (S.I. 1989/207), they may be treated as capital resources which may be aggregated to the limits mentioned below with reserves for the purposes of the first criterion of prudent management (maintenance of adequate reserves and other designated capital resources) under section 45(3) of the Building Societies Act 1986. The prescribed terms restrict payment of interest and repayment of capital until all other shareholders have been paid. The limits to which such aggregation is permitted are, in the case of old deferred shares, £75,000, and, in the case of new deferred shares, £250,000 less the amount of any old deferred shares for the time being so aggregated.

items.title: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Contamination of Feeding Stuff) (England) (No. 2) Amendment Order 1989
items.description: This Order amends the Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Contamination of Feeding Stuff) (England) (No. 2) Order 1989 (“the principal Order”) which contains emergency prohibitions restricting various activities in order to prevent human consumption of food rendered unsuitable for that purpose in consequence of the escape of lead into imported animal feeding stuff.

items.title: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Contamination of Feeding Stuff) (England) (No. 2) Amendment No. 2 Order 1989
items.description: This Order further amends the Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Contamination of Feeding Stuff) (England) (No. 2) Order 1989, as amended, (“the principal Order”) which contains emergency prohibitions restricting various activities in order to prevent human consumption of food rendered unsuitable for that purpose in consequence of the escape of lead into imported animal feeding stuff.

items.title: The Protection of Wrecks (Designation No. 1) Order 1989
items.description: This Order designates as restricted areas for the purposes of the Protection of Wrecks Act 1973 two areas in the Goodwin Sands round the sites of what are, or may prove to be, the wrecks of two vessels which are of historical and archaeological importance.

items.title: The Recreation Grounds (Revocation of Parish Council Byelaws) Order 1989
items.description: This Order revokes the byelaws specified in the Schedule to the Order, which were made in exercise of the powers conferred on parish councils by section 8(1) (d) of the Local Government Act 1894 (56 and 57 Vict. c.73). The relevant part of that section was repealed by section 272(1) of, and Schedule 30 to, the Local Government Act 1972 but the byelaws were continued in force, notwithstanding that repeal, by virtue of section 262(1) of the 1972 Act. The byelaws are revoked as being substantially superseded by byelaws made under other enactments.

items.title: The Chester-Bangor Trunk Road (A55) (Rhuallt Hill Improvement and Slip Roads) Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The Chester-Bangor Trunk Road (A55) (Rhuallt Hill Improvement and Slip Roads) Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Contamination of Feeding Stuff) (Wales) (No. 2) Order 1989
items.description: This Order consolidates with amendments the Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Contamination of Feeding Stuff) (Wales) Order 1989, as amended. It contains emergency prohibitions restricting various activities in order to prevent human consumption of food rendered unsuitable for that purpose in consequence of the escape of lead into imported animal feeding stuff.

items.title: The Security Service Act 1989 (Commencement) Order 1989
items.description: This Order brings into force on 18th December 1989 all the provisions of the Security Service Act 1989.

items.title: The Church Representation Rules (Amendment) (No. 1) Resolution 1989
items.description: (This Note is not part of the Resolution)

items.title: The Church Representation Rules (Amendment) (No. 2) Resolution 1989
items.description: (This Note is not part of the Resolution)

items.title: The Haddock (Specified Sea Areas) (Prohibition of Fishing) Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The Haddock (Specified Sea Areas) (Prohibition of Fishing) Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Cumbria, Northumberland and North Yorkshire (County Boundaries) Order 1989
items.description: This Order transfers areas between the counties of Cumbria, Northumberland and North Yorkshire. Consequential changes to districts, parishes and local government electoral areas are made.

items.title: The Alcoholic Liquor Duties Act 1979 (Repeal of Section 31) Order 1989
items.description: This Order repeals section 31 of the Alcoholic Liquor Duties Act 1979 (which imposes restrictions on the delivery of immature spirits for home use) with effect from 15th December 1989 when the substantive articles of Council Regulation (EEC) No.1576/89 (OJ No.L160, 12.6.89, p.1) laying down general rules on the definition, description and presentation of spirit drinks become applicable.

items.title: The Lincolnshire and Nottinghamshire (County Boundaries) Order 1989
items.description: This Order transfers areas between the county of Lincolnshire and districts of North Kesteven and West Lindsey and the county of Nottinghamshire and district of Newark and Sherwood.

items.title: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Contamination of Feeding Stuff) (England) (No. 3) Order 1989
items.description: This Order consolidates with amendments the Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Contamination of Feeding Stuff) (England) (No. 2) Order 1989, as amended. It contains emergency prohibitions restricting various activities in order to prevent human consumption of food rendered unsuitable for that purpose in consequence of the escape of lead into imported animal feeding stuff.

items.title: The East Surrey Water (Constitution and Regulation) Order 1989
items.description: Section 101(2) of the Water Act 1989 provides for the replacement of provisions relating to the constitution and regulation of a statutory water company and contained in its local Acts and Orders by provisions contained in a memorandum and articles of association which have been approved by special resolution of the members of the company. The replacement provisions have effect only if they have been approved by the Secretary of State and on such date as he determines.

items.title: The Housing (Right to Buy)(Priority of Charges)(No. 2) Order 1989
items.description: Under the Housing Act 1985 (“the 1985 Act”) liability to repay discount following the exercise of the right to buy or the right to be granted a shared ownership lease is secured by a charge on the dwelling-house. Such a charge does not have priority over certain charges securing advances by bodies which are approved lending institutions for the purposes of section 156 of that Act.

items.title: The Home Purchase Assistance (Recognised Lending Institutions) (No. 2) Order 1989
items.description: This Order adds four additional bodies to the list in section 447(1) of the Housing Act 1985. (Other bodies have been added to this list by previous orders). Bodies which are so listed are lending institutions recognised for the purposes of section 445 (assistance for first-time buyers) of the Act and may receive advances from the Secretary of State under that section to enable them to provide assistance to first-time purchasers of house property (the “Homeloan” Scheme).

items.title: The Mortgage Indemnities (Recognised Bodies) (No. 2) Order 1989
The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Contamination of Feeding Stuff) (Wales) (No. 2) Amendment Order 1989
items.description: This Order amends the Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Contamination of Feeding Stuff) (Wales) (No. 2) Order 1989 (“The principal Order”) which contains emergency prohibitions restricting various activities in order to prevent human consumption of food rendered unsuitable for that purpose in consequence of the escape of lead into imported animal feeding stuff.

items.title: The Human Organ Transplants Act 1989 (Commencement) Order 1989
items.description: Section 2(1) of the Human Organ Transplants Act 1989 prohibits the transplant of organs from, and to, living persons who are not genetically related; and this Order appoints 1st April 1990 for it to come into force.

items.title: The Human Organ Transplants (Establishment of Relationship) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations provide that the genetic relationship between proposed living donors of human organs and proposed recipients is to be established in each case by persons approved for that purpose by the Secretary of State using tests specified in the Regulations.

items.title: The Human Organ Transplants (Supply of Information) Regulations 1989
items.description: (This Note is not part of the Regulations)

items.title: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Contamination of Feeding Stuff) (Wales) (No. 3) Order 1989
items.description: This Order consolidates with amendments the Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Contamination of Feeding Stuff) (Wales) (No. 2) Order 1989, as amended. It contains emergency prohibitions restricting various activities in order to prevent human consumption of food rendered unsuitable for that purpose in consequence of the escape of lead into imported animal feeding stuff.

items.title: The Unitary Development Plans (Greater Manchester and Sunderland) (Appointed Day) Order 1989
items.description: The Local Government Act 1985 abolished the Greater London Council and metropolitan county councils. The Act consequently provides for the replacement in their areas of the existing structure and local development plans under town and country planning legislation by “unitary development plans”. This Order brings the provisions of the 1985 Act relating to unitary development plans into force in the county of Greater Manchester and in the Metropolitan district of Sunderland in Tyne and Wear on 19th December 1989.

items.title: The Air Navigation (Restriction of Flying) (Security Establishments in Northern Ireland) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations consolidate the permanent Restriction of Flying Regulations relating to security establishments in Northern Ireland.

items.title: The Air Navigation (Restriction of Flying) (High Security Prisons) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations impose restrictions on helicopter flying in specified airspace around high security prisons.

items.title: The Shrewsbury–Whitchurch–Warrington Trunk Road (A49 Weaverham Diversion, Cheshire) Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The Shrewsbury–Whitchurch–Warrington Trunk Road (A49 Weaverham Diversion, Cheshire) Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Shrewsbury – Whitchurch – Warrington Trunk Road A49 Trunk Road Weaverham (Detrunking) Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The Shrewsbury – Whitchurch – Warrington Trunk Road A49 Trunk Road Weaverham (Detrunking) Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Passenger and Goods Vehicles (Recording Equipment) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations further amend section 97 of the Transport Act 1968 which requires the installation and use of recording equipment in certain goods vehicles (regulation 2) and inserts a new section 97AA in that Act concerning the forgery of seals on such equipment (regulation 3).

items.title: The Social Security (Unemployment, Sickness and Invalidity Benefit) Amendment No. 3 Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Social Security (Unemployment, Sickness and Invalidity Benefit) Regulations 1983 in the following respects—

items.title: The Social Security Benefit (Computation of Earnings) Amendment Regulations 1989
items.description: This version of the The Social Security Benefit (Computation of Earnings) Amendment Regulations 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Contamination of Feeding Stuff) (England) (No.3) Amendment Order 1989
items.description: This Order amends the Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Contamination of Feeding Stuff) (England) (No.3) Order 1989 (“the principal Order”) which contains emergency prohibitions restricting various activities in order to prevent human consumption of food rendered unsuitable for that purpose in consequence of the escape of lead into imported animal feeding stuff.

items.title: The Edinburgh City Bypass (Sighthill, Colinton, Burdiehouse, Gilmerton and Millerhill Sections and Connecting Roads) (Speed Limit) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations impose a maximum speed limit of 70mph on motor vehicles using the special roads described in the Schedules to the Edinburgh City Bypass (Colinton Section and Connecting Roads) Special Roads Scheme 1980 Confirmation Instrument 1981 and the Edinburgh City Bypass (Sighthill, Burdiehouse, Gilmerton and Millerhill Sections and Connecting Roads) Special Roads Scheme 1988 and the Edinburgh City Bypass (Sighthill, Burdiehouse, Gilmerton and Millerhill Sections and Connecting Roads) Special Roads (Appropriation) Order 1988 Confirmation Instrument 1989 and revoke the Edinburgh City Bypass (Colinton Section and Connecting Roads) (Speed Limit) Regulations 1983.

items.title: The Rules of Procedure (Army) (Amendment) Rules 1989
items.description: These Rules amend the Rules of Procedure (Army) 1972 to enable an accused to apply to the convening officer before trial by court-martial or to the court during his trial for a transcript of any tape recording made by the service police of an interview with him.

items.title: The Police and Criminal Evidence Act 1984 Codes of Practice (Armed Forces) Order 1989
items.description: This Order appoints 1st January 1990 as the date on which a code of practice made under section 113(3) and (4)(a) of the Police and Criminal Evidence Act 1984 will come into force.

items.title: The Rules of Procedure (Air Force) (Amendment) Rules 1989
items.description: These Rules amend the Rules of Procedure (Air Force) 1972 to enable an accused to apply to the convening officer before trial by court martial or to the court during his trial for a transcript of any tape recording made by the service police of an interview with him.

items.title: The Standing Civilian Courts (Amendment) Order 1989
items.description: This Order amends the Standing Civilian Courts Order 1977 to enable an accused to apply to the directing officer before trial by Standing Civilian Court or to the court during his trial for a transcript of any tape recording made by the service police of an interview with him.

items.title: The Meat and Meat Products (Hormonal Substances) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations, which apply only to England and Wales, come into force on 13th December 1989. They implement Article 2(c) and (d) of Council Directive 81/602/EEC (OJ No. L222, 7.8.81, p. 32) concerning the prohibition of certain substances having a hormonal action and of any substances having a thyrostatic action, in part Articles 4, 5 and 6 of Council Directive 85/358/EEC (OJ No. L191, 23.7.85, p. 46) supplementing Directive 81/602/EEC and Article 1 of Council Directive 86/469/EEC (OJ No. L275, 26.9.86, p. 36) concerning the examination of animals and fresh meat for the presence of residues.

items.title: The Traffic Signs (Amendment) Regulations 1989 and the Traffic Signs (Amendment) General Directions 1989
items.description: Part I of this instrument amends the Traffic Signs Regulations 1981 as follows.The offence specified in section 36 of the Road Traffic Act 1988 – drivers to comply with traffic signs – is applied to the sign shown in diagram 629.2 (vehicles exceeding height indicated prohibited).The sign shown in diagram 629.2 is specified for the purposes of the relevant entry in Schedule 2 of the Road Traffic Offenders Act 1988 so that the offence committed by failing to comply with the sign involves discretionary disqualification.References to the Road Traffic Act 1972 in regulation 7 are updated to refer to the Road Traffic Act 1988 or the Road Traffic Offenders Act 1988 where applicable.

items.title: The Sex Discrimination Act 1975 (Exemption of Special Treatment for Lone Parents) Order 1989
The Prison (Amendment) (No. 2) Rules 1989
items.description: These Rules amend the Prison Rules 1964, as amended, in two respects.

items.title: The Young Offender Institution (Amendment) (No. 2) Rules 1989
items.description: These Rules amend the Young Offender Institution Rules 1988, as amended, in two respects.

items.title: The A12 Trunk Road (Colchester Road, Havering) (Prescribed Routes) Order 1989
The Colne Valley Water Company (Pipelaying and Other Works) (Code of Practice) Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The Colne Valley Water Company (Pipelaying and Other Works) (Code of Practice) Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Design Right (Semiconductor Topographies) (Amendment) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations amend the Schedule to the Design Right (Semiconductor Topographies) Regulations 1989 to give effect to a communication from the European Commission pursuant to paragraph 3 of Article 1 of Council Decision 88/311/EEC (O.J. No. L140, 7.6.1988, page 13). This communication determines that Sweden is a country which satisfies the conditions in paragraph 2 of that Article that protection of topographies of semiconductor products under Swedish law has been extended to all classes of persons in the Member States of the European Economic Community.

items.title: The Building Societies (Designation of Qualifying Bodies) (Amendment) Order 1989
items.description: This Order amends the Building Societies (Designation of Qualifying Bodies) Order 1988, which designates corporate bodies and types of corporate body as suitable for investment or support by building societies under section 18 of the Building Societies Act 1986, so as to add “appropriate mortgage company” as a further type of designated body.

items.title: The Meat and Meat Products (Hormonal Substances) (Scotland) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations, which apply to Scotland only, come into force on 22nd December 1989. They implement Article 2(c) and (d) of Council Directive 81/602/EEC (OJ No.L 222, 7.8.81, p.32) concerning the prohibition of certain substances having a hormonal action and of any substances having a thyrostatic action, in part Articles 4, 5 and 6 of Council Directive 85/358/EEC (OJ No. L 191, 23.7.85, p.46) supplementing Directive 81/602/EEC and Article 1 of Council Directive 86/469/EEC (OJ No. L 275, 26.9.86, p.36) concerning the presence of animals and fresh meat for the presence of residues.

items.title: The Revenue Support Grant (Specified Bodies) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations specify the Fire Service College, being a body which provides services for local authorities, as a body to which revenue support grant is payable in accordance with Part V of the Local Government Finance Act 1988.

items.title: The Guarantee Payments (Exemption) (No. 26) Order 1989
items.description: This Order excludes from the operation of section 12 of the Employment Protection (Consolidation) Act 1978 employees to whom the Lay Off and Short Time Working Scheme of Airflow Streamlines plc relates. The provisions of the collective agreement are set out in Schedule 2.

items.title: The Insider Dealing (Public Servants) Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The Insider Dealing (Public Servants) Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Insider Dealing (Recognised Stock Exchange) Order 1989
items.description: This Order declares the investment exchange known as NASDAQ to be a recognised stock exchange for the purposes of the Company Securities (Insider Dealing) Act 1985 (“the Act”). The Act provides, amongst other matters, that it shall be an offence to deal in securities on a recognised stock exchange whilst in possession of unpublished price sensitive information (as defined in section 10) in relation to those securities, or to counsel or procure such dealing.

items.title: The Commons Registration (General) (Amendment) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations provide for the increase of fees prescribed in the Commons Registration (General) Regulations 1966 (and previously increased by the Commons Registration (General) (Amendment) Regulations 1980, S.I. 1980/1195) for searches of the Registers of Common Land and Town and Village Greens and for providing certified copies of entries in the Registers.

items.title: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Contamination of Feeding Stuff) (England) (No.3) Amendment No.2 Order 1989
items.description: This Order further amends the Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Contamination of Feeding Stuff) (England) (No.3) Order 1989 (“the principal Order”) which contains emergency prohibitions restricting various activities in order to prevent human consumption of food rendered unsuitable for that purpose in consequence of the escape of lead into imported animal feeding stuff.

items.title: The Pressure Systems and Transportable Gas Containers Regulations 1989
items.description: These regulations impose safety requirements with respect to pressure systems and transportable gas containers which are used or intended to be used at work. They also impose safety requirements to prevent certain vessels from becoming pressurised. Exceptions to the Regulations are set out in Schedule 2 (regulation 3(1)).

items.title: The Third Country Fishing (Enforcement) Order 1989
items.description: This Order, which replaces and revokes the Third Country Fishing (Enforcement) Order 1988, makes breaches of specified articles of the Community Regulations set out in column 1 of the Schedule to the Order offences for the purposes of United Kingdom law where they occur within British fishery limits.

items.title: The Insolvency Practitioners (Amendment) (No.2) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations amend the Insolvency Practitioners Regulations 1986 (the 1986 Regulations). For the purposes of Regulation 6(2) of the 1986 Regulations (which prescribes requirements with respect to practical training and experience), they amend the definition of “office-holder” and provide for the interpretation of references to the appointment of office-holders. The 1986 Regulations have been amended twice before, by S.I. 1986/2247 and S.I. 1989/1587.

items.title: The Price Marking (Food) (Amendment) Order 1989
items.description: This Order amends the Price Marking (Food) Order 1978 by providing that the exemption contained in article 2(2)(a) in relation to food sold in a shop and either handed to the purchaser or delivered as the purchaser may direct shall only apply where the number of regular full-time employees normally required to work in the shop is no more than two. For this purpose, full-time employment is regarded as a requirement of sixteen or more hours' work per week (the minimum fixed by section 146(4) of the Employment Protection (Consolidation) Act 1978, as amended by the Employment Act 1982, for an employee to qualify inter alia for a right to written particulars of terms of employment and itemised pay statements).

items.title: The Local Government and Housing Act 1989 (Commencement No.1) Order 1989
items.description: This Order appoints the days for the coming into force of certain specified provisions of the Local Government and Housing Act 1989 c. 42 (“the Act”). Those provisions relate to housing, local government and local government finance matters in Scotland, although the Order extends throughout Great Britain.

items.title: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Contamination of Feeding Stuff) (Wales) (No. 3) Amendment Order 1989
items.description: This Order amends the Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Contamination of Feeding Stuff) (Wales) (No. 3) Order 1989 (“The principal Order”) which contains emergency prohibitions restricting various activities in order to prevent human consumption of food rendered unsuitable for that purpose in consequence of the escape of lead into imported animal feeding stuff.

items.title: The Public Telecommunication System Designation (East London Telecommunications Limited) Order 1989
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to East London Telecommunications Limited on 16th November 1989 a licence under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunications systems specified in Annex A to that licence, in the whole of the London Boroughs of Redbridge and Barking and Dagenham and the whole of the London Borough of Bexley except Thamesmead Town. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 27th November 1989. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently, by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, East London Telecommunications Limited will be a public telecommunications operator when the Order comes into force.

items.title: The Goods Vehicles (Operators' Licences) (Temporary Use in Great Britain) (Amendment) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations amend the Goods Vehicles (Operators' Licences) (Temporary Use in Great Britain) Regulations 1980. Regulation 2 amends regulation 19 of the 1980 Regulations by abolishing, as from 1st January 1990, the requirement for permits for goods vehicles from the Republic of Ireland to circulate in Great Britain.

items.title: The Goods Vehicles (Authorisation of International Journeys) (Fees) (Amendment) (No.2) Regulations 1989
items.description: These regulations amend the Goods Vehicles (Authorisation of International Journeys) (Fees) Regulations 1983. The amendment deletes the reference to the Agreement dated 9th April 1980 between the United Kingdom and the Republic of Ireland on the International Carriage of Goods by Road from Schedule 1 to the Regulations of 1983 because of the abolition of the requirements for permits for goods vehicles from the Republic of Ireland by the Goods Vehicles (Operators' Licences) (Temporary Use in Great Britain) (Amendment) Regulations 1989 S.I. 1989/2183.

items.title: The Local Government and Housing Act 1989 (Commencement No. 2) Order 1989
items.description: This Order brings into force on 29th November 1989 the provisions of section 1(5) and (6) of the Local Government and Housing Act 1989 throughout Great Britain. These subsections enable the Secretary of State to prescribe requirements for restricting the political activities of every person holding a politically restricted post under a local authority and provide that their terms of appointment and conditions of employment shall be deemed to include such requirements.

items.title: The A11 Trunk Road (Red Lodge Bypass and Slip Roads) Order 1989
items.description: The Order lays down a line for a trunk road bypass of Red Lodge, Suffolk, which also runs over land in Cambridgeshire, and the Order provides for the detrunking of the superseded length of trunk road. The Order also provides for the revocation of an order made under section 7 of the Highways Act 1959, which made similar provision but was not acted upon.

items.title: The Public Lending Right Scheme 1982 (Commencement of Variations) Order 1989
items.description: This Order brings into force on 21st December 1989 variations to the Public Lending Right Scheme 1982.

items.title: The County Council of Suffolk (Mutford Bridges) Scheme 1989 Confirmation Instrument 1989
The Farm and Conservation Grant Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations implement provisions in Title 1 of Council Regulation (EEC) No. 797/85 (OJ No. L93, 30.3.85, p. 1) on improving the efficiency of agricultural structures, as amended by Council Regulation (EEC) No. 1760/87 (OJ No. L167, 26.6.87, p. 1) and Council Regulation (EEC) No. 1137/88 (OJ No. L108, 29.4.88, p. 1), and Article 1 of Council Directive No. 75/268/EEC (OJ No. L128, 19.5.75, p.1) on mountain and hill farming and farming in certain less-favoured areas, as amended by Council Regulation (EEC) No. 797/85.

items.title: The Gaming Act (Variation of Monetary Limits) (No. 2) Order 1989
items.description: This Order increases in England and Wales the maximum amount which may be charged for playing a game once and the maximum amounts which may be offered as prizes in amusements by means of machines provided at certain fairs, licensed club premises and other commercial entertainments under section 34 of the Gaming Act 1968.

items.title: The County Council of Avon (M32 Motorway (Hambrook Interchange to Lower Ashley Road Interchange) and Connecting Roads) (Revocation) Scheme 1989 Confirmation Instrument 1989
items.description: This version of the The County Council of Avon (M32 Motorway (Hambrook Interchange to Lower Ashley Road Interchange) and Connecting Roads) (Revocation) Scheme 1989 Confirmation Instrument 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Wirral Metropolitan Borough Council M53 Motorway (Moreton Spur Extension to Upton Bypass) Scheme 1988 Confirmation Instrument 1989
items.description: This version of the The Wirral Metropolitan Borough Council M53 Motorway (Moreton Spur Extension to Upton Bypass) Scheme 1988 Confirmation Instrument 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Contaminatoin of Feeding Stuff) (England) (No. 3) Amendment No. 3 Order 1989
items.description: This Order further amends the Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Contamination of Feeding Stuff) (England) (No. 3) Order 1989 (“the principal Order”) which contains emergency prohibitions restricting various activities in order to prevent human consumption of food rendered unsuitable for that purpose in consequence of the escape of lead into imported animal feeding stuff.

items.title: The Pyramid Selling Schemes Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations revoke the Pyramid Selling Schemes Regulations 1973 (except for regulations 5 and 6 and 10(1), (2) and (3) which continue to apply to contracts made before 1st March 1990) and replace them with new provisions.

items.title: The Import and Export (Plant Health Fees) (Scotland) Order 1989
items.description: This Order, which applies to Scotland only, and which revokes and replaces the Import and Export (Plant Health Fees) (Scotland) Order 1988, prescribes fees in respect of the services listed in Schedules 1 and 2 to the Order (articles 3 and 4).

items.title: The Higher Education (Wales) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations made under sections 218(9)(d) and 232(5) of the Education Reform Act 1988, which apply only in relation to maintained institutions in Wales and to the institutions in Wales designated by the Schedule thereto, provide for the provision of certain courses of higher education as therein specified to be subject to the approval of the Secretary of State (regulation 3). The Regulations enable the Secretary of State to direct that such a course shall cease to be provided (regulation 4) and, in the case of a course of initial teacher training, to give directions with respect to the number and categories of students to be admitted to a course (regulation 5). The Regulations succeed, as regards Wales, provisions contained in the Education (Schools and Further Education) Regulations 1981 (S.I. 1981/1086) as amended by the Education (Schools and Further Education) (Amendment) Regulations 1983 and 1987 (S.I. 1983/262 and 1987/879). Those 1981 Regulations (as amended) were made under section 27 of the Education Act 1980 which is superseded by section 218 of the Education Reform Act 1988 under which the current regulations are made.

items.title: The Town and Country Planning (Central Manchester Urban Development Area) Special Development Order 1989
items.description: This Order is a special development order applying to land within the Central Manchester urban development area.

items.title: The Town and Country Planning (Sheffield Urban Development Area) Special Development Order 1989
items.description: This Order is a special development order applying to land within the Sheffield urban development area.

items.title: The Town and Country Planning (Bristol Urban Development Area) Special Development Order 1989
items.description: This Order is a special development order applying to land within the Bristol urban development area.

items.title: The Town and Country Planning (Leeds Urban Development Area) Special Development Order 1989
items.description: This Order is a special development order applying to land within the Leeds urban development area.

items.title: The Classification, Packaging and Labelling of Dangerous Substances (Amendment) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations amend the Classification, Packaging and Labelling of Dangerous Substances Regulations 1984 (“the principal Regulations”) to give effect with respect to Great Britain to the provisions of the Commission Directive 87/432/EEC (OJ No. L239, 21.8.87, p.1) adapting to technical progress for the eighth time Council Directive 67/548/EEC (OJ No. L196, 16.8.67, p.1) (OJ/SE 197, p.234) on the classification, packaging and labelling of dangerous substances in relation to substances dangerous for supply.

items.title: The Construction (Head Protection) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations impose requirements for the provision of suitable head protection for and the wearing of suitable head protection by persons at work on building operations or works of engineering construction within the meaning of the Factories Act 1961.

items.title: The Suppression of Terrorism Act 1978 (Designation of Countries) Order 1989
items.description: This Order designates the Republic of Ireland for the purposes of the Suppression of Terrorism Act 1978 so that it becomes a convention country within the meaning of the Act.

items.title: The Gipsy Encampments (Metropolitan District of Doncaster) Order 1989
items.description: This Order designates the Metropolitan District of Doncaster as an area to which section 10 of the Caravan Sites Act 1968 applies.

items.title: The Preservatives in Food (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations, which apply to Scotland only, amend the Preservatives in Food (Scotland) Regulations 1989 by extending the date of the availability of the defence to proceedings by one year to 31st December 1990 in respect of the sale or importation of food having in it or on it ethylene oxide where the presence of that substance is due to its use for pathogen reduction (regulation 11(3)). This extension only applies to dried herbs and spices and dried herbs intended exclusively for marketing without further processing as medicinal products, in accordance with the provisions of Council Directive 79/117/EEC, as amended by Council Directive 86/355/EEC and Council Directive 89/365/EEC.

items.title: The Taunton – Fraddon Trunk Road A361 (Landkey Link) (Detrunking) Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The Taunton – Fraddon Trunk Road A361 (Landkey Link) (Detrunking) Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Industrial Training Levy (Hotel and Catering) Order 1989
items.description: This Order, which comes into force on the day after the day on which it is made, gives effect to proposals of the Hotel and Catering Industry Training Board which were submitted to and approved by the Training Commission, and thereafter submitted by the Training Commission to the Secretary of State. The proposals are for the imposition of a levy on employers in the hotel and catering industry for the purpose of raising money towards meeting the expenses of the Board.

items.title: The Local Authority Social Services (Designation of Functions) Order 1989
items.description: The councils of non-metropolitan counties, metropolitan districts and London boroughs and the Common Council of the City of London are each required by the Local Authority Social Services Act 1970 to establish a social services committee. This Order places within the responsibility of that committee certain functions of those authorities under the Disabled Persons (Services, Consultation and Representation) Act 1986 and under the Access to Personal Files Act 1987.

items.title: The Civil Aviation (Canadian Navigation Services) (Second Amendment) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Civil Aviation (Canadian Navigation Services) Regulations 1986, by increasing the charges for air navigation services provided by or on behalf of the Government of Canada as follows:–

items.title: The Civil Aviation (Joint Financing) (Amendment) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations amend the Civil Aviation (Joint Financing) Regulations 1988, as follows –

items.title: The Police (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations amend the Police (Scotland) Regulations 1976. Regulation 4 provides a procedure for the resolution of a difference of medical opinion as to an officer’s fitness to return to duty after a period of sick leave. Regulation 5 provides for officers of the rank of superintendent and above to be reimbursed the cost of first-class rail travel on duty. Regulation 6 has the effect of restoring to an officer, who was in receipt on 31st March 1989 of a rent allowance which was reduced as a result of the abolition of domestic rates and whose circumstances have not changed, the level of allowance (including the rates element) payable to him on that date. Regulation 7 increases the rates of detective expenses allowances. Regulation 8 provides for increases in the rates of pay of police constables, sergeants, inspectors and chief inspectors and corrects a minor anomaly to ensure that officers who are promoted gain financially thereby. Regulation 9 provides for increases in the rates of the dog handler’s allowance.

items.title: The Local Statutory Provisions (Postponement of Repeal) (Scotland) Order 1989
items.description: Section 225(6) of the Local Government (Scotland) Act 1973 as amended provides that all local statutory provisions to which the subsection applies shall cease to have effect at the end of 1984. Previous Orders (S.I. 1984/1926, 1985/1629, 1986/2034, 1987/2090 and 1988/2106) have postponed until the end of 1989 the date on which such local statutory provisions applying to the local government areas or parts thereof listed in the Schedules to those Orders were to cease to have effect. This Order further postpones that date until the end of 1990 in respect of those local government areas listed in the Schedule to this Order.

items.title: The City of Glasgow and East Kilbride Districts (Mid Lettrick Farm) Boundaries Amendment Order 1989
items.description: This Order transfers an area of approximately 17 acres at Mid Lettrick Farm from East Kilbride District to the City of Glasgow District and makes consequential changes to electoral arrangements.

items.title: The Cosmetic Products (Safety) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations consolidate with amendments the Cosmetic Products (Safety) Regulations 1984 as amended. They implement the requirements of Council Directives No. 76/768/EEC, as amended the last amendments being Council Directive No. 88/667/EEC and Commission Directive 89/174/EEC, and of Commission Directives No. 80/1335/EEC, as amended, No. 82/434/EEC, No. 83/514/EEC and No. 85/490/EEC, relating to cosmetic products, and, as permitted by the Directives, impose certain additional requirements relating to the composition of cosmetic products. The Regulations are made under the Consumer Protection Act 1987 and the European Communities Act 1972.

items.title: The Personal Community Charge (Exemption for the Severely Mentally Impaired) (Scotland) Regulations 1989
items.description: (This Note is not part of the Regulations)

items.title: The Combined Probation Areas (West Sussex) (No.2) Order 1989
items.description: This Order amends the Combined Probation Areas Order 1986 so as to change the number of justices appointed to the West Sussex Probation Committee for some of the petty sessional divisions in West Sussex. The petty sessional division of Chichester and District will appoint 3 justices rather than 5 and Mid-Sussex will appoint 3 rather than 2.

items.title: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Contamination of Feeding Stuff) (England) (No. 4) Order 1989
items.description: This Order consolidates with amendments the Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Contamination of Feeding Stuff) (England) (No. 3) Order 1989, as amended. It contains emergency prohibitions restricting various activities in order to prevent human consumption of food rendered unsuitable for that purpose in consequence of the escape of lead into imported animal feeding stuff.

items.title: The Local Government Reorganisation (Croxteth Hall and Park) Order 1989
items.description: This Order transfers to Liverpool City Council the Merseyside Residuary Body’s interests in Croxteth Hall and Park.

items.title: The Combined Probation Areas (Lancashire) Order 1989
items.description: This Order amends the Combined Probation Areas Order 1986 so as to change the number of justices appointed to the Lancashire Probation Committee for some of the petty sessional divisions in Lancashire. The petty sessional division of Burnley will appoint 3 justices instead of 2; Darwen will appoint 2 instead of 1; Fylde 2 instead of 1; Ormskirk 3 instead of 2; Preston 3 instead of 4; Ribble Valley 2 instead of 1 and South Ribble 3 instead of 2.

items.title: The Combined Probation Areas (Warwickshire) Order 1989
items.description: This Order amends the Combined Probation Areas Order 1986 so as to change the number of justices appointed to the Warwickshire Probation Committee for some of the petty sessional divisions in Warwickshire. The petty sessional division of Nuneaton will appoint 4 justices rather than 3, Rugby will appoint 3 rather than 2 and in South Warwickshire 3 will be appointed instead of 5. The number of justices on the Committee will, however, remain the same.

items.title: The Education (Higher Education Corporations) (No. 6) Order 1989
items.description: This Order makes provision for the establishment of a body corporate to conduct Harrow College of Higher Education. That body which, by virtue of paragraph 1(2) of Schedule 7 to the Education Reform Act 1988 will have the name of Harrow College of Higher Education, is established on 1st January 1990 to prepare itself for the conduct of that institution from 1st April 1990 which is appointed as the transfer date for the purpose of section 126 of that Act. It also specifies the London Borough of Harrow as the local authority able to nominate one local authority nominee to become a member of that body corporate.

items.title: The Local Government Act 1988 (Defined Activities) (Exemption) (England) Order 1989
items.description: This Order allows the Common Council of the City of London to carry out the cleaning of the Central Criminal Court (the “Old Bailey”), without having to put that activity out to tender under the provisions of the Local Government Act 1988, so long as it remains the courthouse for the Central Criminal Court.

items.title: The Value Added Tax (Accounting and Records) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations, which come into force on 1st January 1990, make provision for the keeping of records and accounts by taxable persons under the Value Added Tax Act 1983.

items.title: The Gaming Act (Variation of Monetary Limits) (Scotland) (No.2) Order 1989
items.description: This Order, which extends to Scotland only, increases the maximum amount which may be charged for playing a game once and the maximum amounts which may be offered as prizes in amusements by means of machines provided at certain fairs, licensed club premises and other commercial entertainments under section 34 of the Gaming Act 1968.

items.title: The Public Health (Notification of Infectious Diseases) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations amend the Public Health (Notification of Infectious Diseases) (Scotland) Regulations 1988 by adding Lyme Disease and Toxoplasmosis to the list of infectious diseases which are notifiable in terms of the 1988 Regulations.

items.title: The Plant Health (Great Britain) (Amendment) (No. 3) Order 1989
items.description: This Order requires that raw horseradish and radish imported from the Netherlands shall be free of foliage (article 2(1)).

items.title: The Customs Duty (Personal Reliefs) (Amendment) Order 1989
items.description: With effect from 1st January 1990, this Order increases the allowance for goods other than alcohol, tobacco etc. obtained by travellers, duty and tax-paid in the EEC, from £250 to £265.

items.title: The Excise Duties (Small Non-Commercial Consignments) Relief (Amendment) Regulations 1989
items.description: Under the Excise Duties (Small Non-Commercial Consignments) Relief Regulations 1986 certain small consignments of a non-commercial character may be admitted into the United Kingdom without payment of excise duty. The present Regulations increase the maximum sterling value of such a consignment to £75 when imported from another Member State. It implements, for intra-community consignments, the requirements of Article 1(a) of Council Directive 74/651/EEC (OJ No. L354, 30.12.74, p.57) which was added by Council Directive 78/1034/EEC (OJ No. L366, 28.12.78, p.33). That Article provides for the purposes of the Directives, that the equivalent of the European Currency Unit in national currency is fixed at the rates obtaining on 1st October each year to have effect from 1st January the following year.

items.title: The Gaming Machine Licence Duty (Variation of Monetary Limits and Exemptions) Order 1989
items.description: This Order increases the maximum amount (in money or money’s worth) which a gaming machine may pay out for a single game if it is to qualify as a small-prize machine.

items.title: The Value Added Tax (Bad Debt Relief) (Amendment) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations, which come into force on 1st January 1990 amend the Value Added Tax (Bad Debt Relief) Regulations 1986.

items.title: The Value Added Tax (General) (Amendment) (No. 3) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations, which come into force on 1st January 1990 further amend the Value Added Tax (General) Regulations 1985.

items.title: The Civil Aviation (Route Charges for Navigation Services) (Amendment) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations amend the Civil Aviation (Route Charges for Navigation Services) Regulations 1989 as follows–

items.title: The Supply of Beer (Loan Ties, Licensed Premises and Wholesale Prices) Order 1989
items.description: This Order provides that where a brewer makes a loan or gives other financial assistance in return for a tie to his products, the recipient of the loan or assistance must be able to repay it or make whatever other payment may be due upon giving not more than three months' notice, and the tie must then cease. If interest is due at a lower rate during an initial period of up to one year and the loan is repaid during that period, the subsequent higher rate may be charged.

items.title: The Value Added Tax (Do-It-Yourself Builders) (Refund of Tax) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations relate to the refund by the Commissioners of Customs and Excise of value added tax chargeable on certain goods supplied to, or imported by, a person constructing, otherwise than in the course or furtherance of a business carried on by him, a building the construction of which, if done by a contractor, would be eligible for zero-rating under Group 8 of Schedule 5 to the Value Added Tax Act 1983 (as amended).

items.title: The Non-Domestic Rating (Collection and Enforcement) (Central Lists) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations, which are made under Part III of the Local Government Finance Act 1988, make provision for the collection and enforcement of non-domastic rates arising in relation to those whose names appear on a central rating list by virtue of a designation under regulations under section 53 of that Act, and related matters.

items.title: The Non-Domestic Rating (Unoccupied Property) Regulations 1989
items.description: Under Part III of the Local Government Finance Act 1988 rates are payable on unoccupied hereditaments if they fall within a class prescribed by regulations made by the Secretary of State and the other conditions set out in section 45 apply.

items.title: The Motor Vehicles (Type Approval) (Amendment) (No.2) Regulations 1989
The Central Rating Lists Regulations 1989
items.description: Under section 53(1) of the Local Government Finance Act 1988 the Secretary of State may, with a view to securing the central rating en bloc of certain hereditaments, by Regulations designate a person and prescribe in relation to him one or more descriptions of non-domestic hereditaments.
