Such Regulations may require that the central rating lists (which are by virtue of section 140 compiled separately for England and for Wales) shall show in relation to that person hereditaments of the prescribed description or descriptions, and contain such information about hereditaments shown in it as the Secretary of State may prescribe by Regulations under section 53(2) and (5). Supplementary provision may be made under section 143(1).

items.title: The A47 Leicester—Great Yarmouth Trunk Road (Easton-Postwick), A11 London—Norwich Trunk Road (Cringleford—Mile End Road), A140 Ipswich—Norwich Trunk Road (South of Norwich Road—Daniels Road) (Norwich Southern Bypass) Detrunking Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The A47 Leicester—Great Yarmouth Trunk Road (Easton-Postwick), A11 London—Norwich Trunk Road (Cringleford—Mile End Road), A140 Ipswich—Norwich Trunk Road (South of Norwich Road—Daniels Road) (Norwich Southern Bypass) Detrunking Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The A47 Leicester-Great Yarmouth Trunk Road (Norwich Southern Bypass and Slip Roads) (Southern and Eastern Section) Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The A47 Leicester-Great Yarmouth Trunk Road (Norwich Southern Bypass and Slip Roads) (Southern and Eastern Section) Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The A47 Leicester-Great Yarmouth Trunk Road (Norwich Southern Bypass) (River Yare Bridge) Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The A47 Leicester-Great Yarmouth Trunk Road (Norwich Southern Bypass) (River Yare Bridge) Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Occupational Pensions (Revaluation) Order 1989
items.description: This Order is made, as required by section 52A of the Social Security Pensions Act 1975, in the revaluation year beginning on 1st January 1989. The revaluation percentages specified are relevant to the revaluation of benefits under occupational pension schemes, as required by section 52B of, and Schedule 1A to, that Act.

items.title: The Charging Authorities (Population for Precepts) (Wales) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations apply only to Wales.

items.title: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Contamination of Feeding Stuff) (Wales) (No. 4) Order 1989
items.description: This Order consolidates with amendments the Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Contamination of Feeding Stuff) (Wales) (No. 3) Order 1989, as amended. It contains emergency prohibitions restricting various activities in order to prevent human consumption of food rendered unsuitable for that purpose in consequence of the escape of lead into imported animal feeding stuff.

items.title: The Finance Act 1985 (Serious Misdeclaration and Interest on Tax) (Appointed Days) Order 1989
items.description: This Order brings into force sections 14 and 18 of the Finance Act 1985. Both sections will apply to all prescribed accounting periods which begin on, or after, 1st April 1990.

items.title: The Finance Act 1989 (Recovery of Overpaid Tax and Administration) (Appointed Days) Order 1989
items.description: This Order brings into force sections 24 and 25 of the Finance Act 1989.

items.title: The Value Added Tax (Finance, Health and Welfare) Order 1989
items.description: This Order amends Groups 5 and 7 of Schedule 6 to the Value Added Tax Act 1983. It exempts from VAT the making of arrangements for or the underwriting of transactions in securities, the management of authorised unit trust schemes (including single property schemes) and the provision of private ambulance services. It implements the requirements of Article 1(1) of Council Directive 89/465/EEC (OJ No.L 226, 3.8.89, p.21).

items.title: The Value Added Tax (Small Non-Commercial Consignments) Relief (Amendment) Order 1989
items.description: Under the Value Added Tax (Small Non-Commercial Consignments) Relief Order 1986, certain small consignments of a non-commercial character may be admitted into the United Kingdom without payment of value added tax. The present Order increases to £75 the maximum sterling value of such consignments when imported from another Member State. It implements the requirements of Articles 1 and 1a of Directive 74/651/EEC (as amended by Directives 78/1034/EEC and 88/663/EEC). The new limit results from the combined effect of an increase in the relevant European Currency Unit limit and the fixing in national currency of the equivalent of the European Currency Unit at the rate obtaining on the first working day of October each year to have effect from 1st January of the following year.

items.title: The Community Charges (Miscellaneous Provisions) (No. 2) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations, which are made under Parts I and II of the Local Government Finance Act 1988, deal with miscellaneous matters concerning community charges.

items.title: The Agricultural or Forestry Tractors and Tractor Components (Type Approval) (Amendment) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations, which apply throughout the United Kingdom and come into force on 31st December 1989, amend the Agricultural or Forestry Tractors and Tractor Components (Type Approval) Regulations 1988 (the principal Regulations). They implement Commission Directive 88/465/EEC (OJ No. L228, 17.8.88, p.31) and Council Directive 89/173/EEC (OJ No. L67, 10.3.89, p.1) and make certain other amendments to the principal Regulations.

items.title: The Apple and Pear Development Council (Dissolution) Order 1989
items.description: This Order provides for the winding up and dissolution of the Apple and Pear Development Council (article 3), the preparation of final accounts of the Council (article 4) and the imposition and recovery of charges to meet the Council’s liabilities and the expenses of the winding up (article 5). It further provides for the application of surplus moneys and property of the Council for specified purposes (article 6) and the revocation of the Apple and Pear Development Council Order 1986.

items.title: The Apple and Pear Research Council Order 1989
items.description: This Order establishes a development Council for the apple and pear growing industry in England and Wales to be known as the Apple and Pear Research Council.

items.title: The Water Act 1989 (Commencement No.4) Order 1989
items.description: Article 2 of this Order brings into force on 1st April 1990 subsections (2)(c) and (3) to (7) of section 98 of the Water Act 1989 (relating to statutory water companies).

items.title: The Lancashire County Council (Improvement of Eanam/Higher Eanam, Blackburn – New Eanam Canal Bridge) Scheme 1987 Confirmation Instrument 1989
The Rate Limitation (Councils in England) (Prescribed Maximum) (Rates) Order 1989
items.description: This Order prescribes maximum rates for the authorities listed in the Schedule to the Order for the financial year beginning on 1st April 1989. In each case the maximum is shown against the name of the authority.

items.title: The Smoke Control Areas (Exempted Fireplaces) Order 1988
The Surface Waters (Dangerous Substances) (Classification) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations prescribe the system of classifying the quality of inland waters, coastal waters and relevant territorial waters (as defined in section 103(1)(c) of the Water Act 1989) according to the presence in them of concentrations of the dangerous substances listed in the Schedules to the Regulations.

items.title: The Preservatives in Food (Amendment) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations, which come into force on 1st January 1990, amend the Preservatives in Food Regulations 1989 by extending the date by one year to 31st December 1990 of the availability of the defence to proceedings in respect of the sale or importation of food having in it or on it ethylene oxide where the presence of that substance is due to its use for pathogen reduction (regulation 11(3)). This extension only applies to dried herbs and spices and dried herbs intended exclusively for marketing without further processing as medicinal products, in accordance with the provisions of Council Directive 79/117/EEC, as amended by Council Directive 86/355/EEC and Council Directive 89/365/EEC.

items.title: The All-Terrain Motor Vehicles (Safety) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations prohibit the supply of any regulated vehicle which has three wheels.A regulated vehicle is one which is propelled by an internal combustion engine and is designed or constructed primarily for use in leisure activities on all types of land surface or all types of land surface other than highways or roads to which the public has access except for vehicles for disabled persons and any two-wheeled motor cycle with a side-car.

items.title: The Severn-Trent Water Authority (Lee Brockhurst Boreholes) Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The Severn-Trent Water Authority (Lee Brockhurst Boreholes) Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Pension Scheme Surpluses (Valuation) (Amendment) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations, which come into force on 28th December 1989, amend the Pension Scheme Surpluses (Valuation) Regulations 1987 (“the Principal Regulations”).

items.title: The Borough of Tonbridge and Malling (Electoral Arrangements) Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The Borough of Tonbridge and Malling (Electoral Arrangements) Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The County Councils (Library Authority Expenses) (Wales) Order 1989
items.description: Section 70 of the Local Government Finance Act 1988 (“the 1988 Act”) provides that, subject to certain exceptions, the expenses of a county council are its general expenses. One of the exceptions concerns the expenses of a Welsh county council in exercising its functions as library authority in its library area, where that area consists of part only of its administrative area (section 70(2)(c)). In those circumstances such expenses are a Welsh county council’s special expenses and relate only to that council’s library area. Insofar as special expenses are incurred or defrayed on or after 1st April 1990, a county council must secure that such of those expenses as are to be met by precepts are borne only by those charging authorities (district councils in Wales) to whose area the expenses concerned relate (section 69(4)).

items.title: The Transport Act 1985 (Extension of Eligibility for Travel Concessions) (Amendment) Order 1989
items.description: This Order amends the Transport Act 1985 (Extension of Eligibility for Travel Concessions) Order 1986 so that a new class of persons is eligible to receive travel concessions under travel concession schemes in England (except London), Wales and Scotland. The class is persons who in the opinion of the authority or authorities responsible for administering such schemes are persons who if they applied to the Secretary of State for the grant of a driving licence would be likely to be refused such a grant pursuant to section 92 of the Road Traffic Act 1988.

items.title: The Protection of Wrecks (Designation No.2) Order 1989
items.description: This Order designates as a restricted area for the purposes of the Protection of Wrecks Act 1973 an area off the island of Lundy, North Devon, round the site of what is thought to be the wreck of a vessel which is of historical and archaeological importance.

items.title: The Protection of Wrecks (Designation No. 3) Order 1989
items.description: This Order designates as a restricted area for the purposes of the Protection of Wrecks Act 1973 an area in the Goodwin Sands round the site of what is thought to be the wreck of a vessel of historical and archaeological importance. No part of the restricted area lies above high water mark of ordinary spring tides.

items.title: The Horses (Landing from Northern Ireland and the Republic of Ireland) (Revocation) Order 1989
items.description: This Order revokes the Horses (Landing from Northern Ireland and the Republic of Ireland) Order 1954 which required (with certain exceptions) horses imported into Great Britain from Northern Ireland or the Republic of Ireland to be landed at certain ports approved by the Minister of Agriculture, Fisheries and Food and to be examined on landing by a veterinary inspector (article 2).

items.title: The Animals (Scientific Procedures) Act (Fees) Order 1989
items.description: This Order, which does not extend to Northern Ireland, prescribes the fees payable by the holders of certificates under section 6 of the Animals (Scientific Procedures) Act 1986 (scientific procedure establishments) and under section 7 of the Act (breeding and supplying establishments).

items.title: The Non-Domestic Rating (Miscellaneous Provisions) (No. 2) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations make further provision about valuation and other matters in connection with non-domestic rating under Part III of the Local Government Finance Act 1988, which takes effect on 1st April 1990.

items.title: The Non-Domestic Ratepayers (Consultation) Regulations 1989
items.description: Section 134 of the Local Government Finance Act 1988 requires charging authorities and certain precepting authorities to consult persons or bodies appearing to them to be representative of persons subject to non-domestic rates under sections 43 and 45 of the Act as regards hereditaments situated in their areas. The consultations must be made as to each financial year and must be about the authorities' proposals for expenditure (including capital expenditure) in that financial year.

items.title: The Charging Authorities (Notification of Precept Population) (Wales) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations apply only to Wales.

items.title: The Animals (Scientific Procedures) Act (Commencement No. 2) Order 1989
items.description: This Order, which does not extend to Northern Ireland, brings into force on 1st January 1990, those provisions of the Animals (Scientific Procedures) Act 1986 which are not already in force.

items.title: The Inshore Fishing (Prohibition of Fishing and Fishing Methods) (Scotland) Order 1989
items.description: This Order amends the existing regulatory regime governing fishing in Scottish inshore waters for sea fish other than salmon and migratory trout.

items.title: The Teaching Council (Scotland) Election Scheme 1989 Approval Order 1989
items.description: (This Note is not part of the Order)

items.title: The Housing Revenue Account General Fund Contribution Limits (Scotland) Order 1989
items.description: This Order limits the amount of contribution from their general fund to the housing revenue account which local authorities may include in their estimates for the year 1990-91.

items.title: The Employment Act 1989 (Amendments and Revocations) Order 1989
items.description: This Order amends and revokes various instruments. The amendments set out in Part 1 of the Schedule to this Order are made so that the provisions in that Part which are framed by reference to a specified age expressed as a number of years are instead framed by reference to school-leaving age.

items.title: The Offices, Shops and Railway Premises Act 1963 (Commencement No. 3) Order 1989
items.description: This Order brings into force on 16th January 1990, in relation to premises which are in a covered market place, the repeal of section 37 of the Shops Act 1950 (c. 28) (seats for female shop workers) provided for by section 91(4) of, and Schedule 2 to, the Offices, Shops and Railway Premises Act 1963 (c. 41). The repeal of section 37 came into force for all other purposes on 1st August 1964 by virtue of article 1 of, and Part III of Schedule 1 to, the Offices, Shops and Railway Premises Act 1963 (Commencement No. 1) Order 1964 (S.I. 1964/191 (C.3)).

items.title: The Suppression of Terrorism Act 1978 (Application of Provisions) (Republic of Ireland) Order 1989
items.description: This Order, which is made in consequence of the accession of the Republic of Ireland to the European Convention on the Suppression of Terrorism, applies section 1(3)(c) and (d)(ii) of the Suppression of Terrorism Act 1978 (c. 26) respectively to warrants of arrest issued in the Republic for backing in the United Kingdom and to criminal proceedings instituted in the Republic for the purposes of which evidence is sought to be obtained in the United Kingdom.

items.title: The Reservoirs Act 1975 (Application Fees) (Amendment) Regulations 1989
items.description: The Reservoirs Act 1975 (Supervising Engineers Panel) (Applications and Fees) Regulations 1984, the Reservoirs Act 1975 (All Reservoirs Panel) (Applications and Fees) Regulations 1985 and the Reservoirs Act 1975 (Non-Impounding and Service Reservoirs Panels) (Applications and Fees) Regulations 1985 prescribed the fees which are to accompany applications by civil engineers to become members of the panels constituted for the purposes of the Reservoirs Act 1975. Those fees have been increased by the Reservoirs Act 1975 (Application Fees) (Amendment) Regulations 1986 and the Reservoirs Act 1975 (Application Fees) (Amendment) Regulations 1988.

items.title: The A5/A49 Trunk Roads (Shrewsbury Bypass and Improvements) (Temporary Bridge over the River Severn at Uffington, Shrewsbury) Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The A5/A49 Trunk Roads (Shrewsbury Bypass and Improvements) (Temporary Bridge over the River Severn at Uffington, Shrewsbury) Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The (AS) London-Holyhead Trunk Road and Slip Roads and the (A49) Newport-Shrewsbury-Whitchurch-Warrington Trunk Road (AS/A49 Shrewsbury By-Pass and Improvements) Order 1985 (Emstrey Amendment) Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The (AS) London-Holyhead Trunk Road and Slip Roads and the (A49) Newport-Shrewsbury-Whitchurch-Warrington Trunk Road (AS/A49 Shrewsbury By-Pass and Improvements) Order 1985 (Emstrey Amendment) Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The (A5) London–Holyhead Trunk Road and Slip Roads and the (A49) Newport–Shrewsbury–Whitchurch– Warrington Trunk Road (A5/A49 Shrewsbury By-Pass and Improvements) Order 1985 (Sutton Hall Amendment) Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The (A5) London–Holyhead Trunk Road and Slip Roads and the (A49) Newport–Shrewsbury–Whitchurch– Warrington Trunk Road (A5/A49 Shrewsbury By-Pass and Improvements) Order 1985 (Sutton Hall Amendment) Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Medicines (Veterinary Drugs) (Pharmacy and Merchants' List) (No. 2) Order 1989
items.description: This Order revokes the Medicines (Veterinary Drugs) (Pharmacy and Merchants' List) Order 1989.

items.title: The Medicines (Veterinary Drugs) (Prescription Only) Order 1989
items.description: This Order replaces the Medicines (Veterinary Drugs) (Prescription Only) Order 1985.

items.title: The Medicines (Medicated Animal Feeding Stuffs) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations replace the Medicines (Medicated Animal Feeding Stuffs) Regulations 1988.

items.title: The Caseins and Caseinates (Amendment) Regulations 1989
items.description: This version of the The Caseins and Caseinates (Amendment) Regulations 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Medicines (Exemption from Licences) (Wholesale Dealing) Order 1989
items.description: This Order revokes and re-enacts the provisions of the Medicines (Exemption from Licences) (Wholesale Dealing) Orders 1972, 1977 and 1983. The Order continues to exempt from restrictions imposed by section 8(3) of the Medicines Act 1968 as to wholesale dealing in medicinal products (except in accordance with a licence granted under that Act) the sale or offer for sale by way of wholesale dealing of a medicinal product by the product licence holder or by the person who assembled that medicinal product to the order of the licence holder, where the product has not left the premises of a licensed manufacturer or assembler throughout the period until the sale of that medicinal product.

items.title: The Medicines (Exemption from Licences) (Special and Transitional Cases) (Amendment) Order 1989
items.description: This Order amends the Medicines (Exemption from Licences) (Special and Transitional Cases) Order 1971 and the Medicines (Exemption from Licences) (Special Cases and Miscellaneous Provisions) Order 1972 by extending the definition of “medicinal product” to include “intermediate feed”, as defined in article 1(2) in the former Order and to exclude it in the latter.

items.title: The Medicines (Animal Feeding Stuffs) (Enforcement) (Amendment) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations amend the Medicines (Animal Feeding Stuffs) (Enforcement) Regulations 1985 by extending the meaning of animal feeding stuff to include intermediate medicated feeding stuffs. The effect of this amendment is that these Regulations will now apply to intermediate feed, as defined in regulation 1(2).

items.title: The Medicines (Exemptions from Licences) (Intermediate Medicated Feeding Stuffs) Order 1989
items.description: This order confers exemptions from the restrictions imposed by sections 7 and 8 of the Medicines Act 1968 as to dealings in and manufacture of medicinal products except in accordance with a licence under that Act. The exemptions apply in relation to those medicated feeding stuffs which are to be treated as medicinal products by virtue of the Medicines (Intermediate Medicated Feeding Stuffs) Order 1989 and the exemptions are subject to the condition or limitation specified in this Order.

items.title: The Bovine Spongiform Encephalopathy (No. 2) Amendment Order 1989
items.description: This Order amends the Bovine Spongiform Encephalopathy (No. 2) Order 1988 by deleting from that Order a provision whereby the prohibition on the sale, supply and use of certain feedingstuffs for feeding to ruminating animals imposed by that Order will have ceased to have effect on 1st January 1990 (article 2).

items.title: The Export of Goods (Control) (Amendment No. 6) Order 1989
items.description: This Order further amends the Export of Goods (Control) Order 1987.

items.title: The Home Purchase Assistance (Recognised Lending Institutions) (No. 3) Order 1989
items.description: This Order adds two additional bodies to the list in section 447(1) of the Housing Act 1985. (Other bodies have been added to this list by previous Orders.) Bodies which are so listed are lending institutions recognised for the purposes of section 445 (assistance for first-time buyers) of the Act and may receive advances from the Secretary of State under that section to enable them to provide assistance to first-time purchasers of house property (the “Homeloan” Scheme).

items.title: The Housing (Right to Buy) (Priority of Charges) (No. 3) Order 1989
items.description: Under the Housing Act 1985 (“the 1985 Act”) liability to repay discount following the exercise of the right to buy or the right to be granted a shared ownership lease is secured by a charge on the dwelling-house. Such a charge does not have priority over certain charges securing advances by bodies which are approved lending institutions for the purposes of section 156 of that Act.

items.title: The Mortgage Indemnities (Recognised Bodies) (No. 3) Order 1989
items.description: This Order specifies two additional bodies as recognised bodies for the purpose of sections 442 and 443 of the Housing Act 1985. (Other bodies have been specified by previous Orders.)

items.title: The Non-Domestic Rating (Stud Farms) Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The Non-Domestic Rating (Stud Farms) Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The A3 Trunk Road (Roehampton Vale, Wandsworth) (Speed Limits) Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The A3 Trunk Road (Roehampton Vale, Wandsworth) (Speed Limits) Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Education (Publication of Schemes for Financing Schools) Regulations 1989
items.description: Sections 33 and 34 of the Education Reform Act 1988 require local education authorities to prepare and submit to the Secretary of State for his approval schemes for financing county and voluntary schools maintained by them. Section 42(1) of that Act requires such schemes, on their entry into force and on such subsequent occasions as may be prescribed by regulations, to be published in the manner prescribed by regulations.

items.title: The Collection Fund (England) Regulations 1989
items.description: Under Part VI of the Local Government Finance Act 1988 charging authorities are required to maintain collection funds. Into those funds must be paid receipts from community charges, from non-domestic rates and from revenue support grant. From those funds charging authorities must meet precepts from other authorities and their own demands, calculated under section 95 of the Act, in respect of their estimated expenses.

items.title: The Consumer Credit (Exempt Agreements) (Amendment) (No.2) Order 1989
items.description: This Order further amends the Consumer Credit (Exempt Agreements) Order 1989, insofar as it provides exemption for the purposes of the Consumer Credit Act 1974 of certain consumer credit agreements where the creditor is a body specified in Schedule 1 to that Order, by adding the name of one body to the list in Part III of that Schedule and by deleting the name of one body from the list in Part IV of that Schedule.

items.title: The Income Tax (Reduced and Composite Rate) Order 1989
items.description: By virtue of the Income Tax (Building Societies) Regulations 1986 (S.I. 1986/482, amended by S.I. 1987/844, 1988/1011 and 1989/36) a building society is required to pay to the Inland Revenue in respect of payments of dividends and interest an amount representing income tax on certain sums calculated in part at the basic rate and in part at “the reduced rate”.

items.title: The Income Support (Transitional) Amendment No. 2 Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Income Support (Transitional) Regulations 1987 by providing for a person’s transitional addition not to be reduced by any increase in his applicable amount attributable to the temporary presence in the claimant’s household of a child or young person who is in the care of a local authority (regulation 2).

items.title: The Channel Tunnel Act (Competition) Order 1989
items.description: This Order makes provision in connection with the approval by the Secretary of State by order under section 29 of the Restrictive Trade Practices Act 1976 (as amended by section 33 of the Channel Tunnel Act 1987) of the channel tunnel agreement providing for the procurement of very high speed trains for operation through the tunnel system.

items.title: The Oral Snuff (Safety) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations prohibit the supply of oral snuff, including offering or agreeing to supply it and exposing or possessing it for supply. Oral snuff is defined in regulation 1(2) as goods which consist wholly or mainly of tobacco in fine cut, ground or particulate form or in any combination of those forms and which are for oral use other than smoking.

items.title: The A34 Winchester-Preston Trunk Road (North of Oxford to Monkspath) De-Trunking Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The A34 Winchester-Preston Trunk Road (North of Oxford to Monkspath) De-Trunking Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The A41 London–Birmingham Trunk Road (Bicester to South of Knowle) De-Trunking Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The A41 London–Birmingham Trunk Road (Bicester to South of Knowle) De-Trunking Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Lothian and Borders Regions and Midlothian and Ettrick and Lauderdale Districts (Brothershiels) Boundaries Amendment Order 1989
items.description: This Order transfers an area of approximately 2216 acres at Brothershiels from Midlothian District in Lothian Region to Ettrick and Lauderdale District in Borders Region and makes consequential changes to electoral arrangements.

items.title: The A43 Oxford—Market Deeping Trunk Road (Weston-on-the-Green to South of Baynard’s Green) De-Trunking Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The A43 Oxford—Market Deeping Trunk Road (Weston-on-the-Green to South of Baynard’s Green) De-Trunking Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The A423 North of Oxford–South of Coventry Trunk Road (Kidlington to East Adderbury) De-Trunking Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The A423 North of Oxford–South of Coventry Trunk Road (Kidlington to East Adderbury) De-Trunking Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Essex and Suffolk (County Boundaries) Order 1989
items.description: This Order transfers areas between the counties of Essex and Suffolk. Consequential changes to districts, parishes and local government electoral areas are made.

items.title: The Value Added Tax (General) (Amendment) (No.4) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations, which come into force on 1st April 1990, amend regulation 32(2) of the Value Added Tax (General) Regulations 1985 (“the principal Regulations”), and add a new Part VA to the principal Regulations. These provisions implement the requirements of Title XI, and in particular Article 20, of Council Directive No. 77/388/EEC (OJ No. L145, 17.5.77, pp 15–16).

items.title: The Furniture and Furnishings (Fire) (Safety) (Amendment) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations amend the Furniture and Furnishings (Fire) (Safety) Regulations 1988 (“the 1988 Regulations”) and the Rag Flock and Other Filling Materials Regulations 1981 (“the 1981 Regulations”).

items.title: The Public Service Vehicles (Conditions of Fitness, Equipment, Use and Certification) (Amendment) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations amend the Public Service Vehicles (Conditions of Fitness, Equipment, Use and Certification) Regulations 1981.

items.title: The County Court (Amendment) Rules 1989
items.description: This version of the The County Court (Amendment) Rules 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Road Vehicles (Construction and Use) (Amendment) (No. 4) Regulations 1989
items.description: These regulations further amend the Road Vehicles (Construction and Use) Regulations 1986.

items.title: The Valuation (Stud Farms) (Scotland) Order 1989
items.description: Section 7B of the Valuation and Rating (Scotland) Act 1956 (c. 60), as inserted by paragraph 4 of Schedule 6 to the Local Government and Housing Act 1989 (c. 42), makes provision for the reduction of the rateable value of lands and heritages which comprise or include buildings used for the breeding and/or rearing of horses and occupied with agricultural land or agricultural buildings. The rateable value of those lands and heritages is to be ascertained by making a deduction from what would otherwise be their rateable value. The amount of the deduction is either such amount as is specified by the Secretary of State by order or the amount which would otherwise be the rateable value of those buildings, whichever is the smaller.

items.title: The Community Water Charges (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations amend the Community Water Charges (Scotland) Regulations 1988 (S.I. 1988/1538) (“the principal Regulations”).

items.title: The Collection Fund (Wales) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations apply only to Wales. Under Part VI of the Local Government Finance Act 1988 (“the Act”) charging authorities are required to maintain collection funds into which must be paid receipts from community charges. From its collection fund a charging authority must meet precepts from other authorities and its own demand, calculated under section 95 of the Act, in respect of its estimated expenses.

items.title: The Commonwealth Development Corporation (Additional Enterprises) (Variation) Order 1989
items.description: This Order adds enterprises for merchanting, wholesaling and stockholding, for the provision or improvement of factories, and industrial, commercial and retail premises, and for the promotion of tourism to the classes of enterprises to which the powers of the Corporation, under the Commonwealth Development Corporation Acts 1978 to 1986, relate. It varies article 2 of the Commonwealth Development Corporation (Additional Enterprises) Order 1987.

items.title: The Public Telecommunication System Designation (West Country Cable Limited) Order 1989
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to West Country Cable Limited on 5th December 1989 a licence under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in Annex A to that licence, in all or part of the Districts of Northavon, Woodspring, Wandsdyke, Kingswood, Mendip and West Wiltshire and the Cities of Bath and Bristol. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 18th December 1989. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently, by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, West Country Cable limited will be a public telecommunications operator when the Order comes into force.

items.title: The Teachers' Pay and Conditions Act 1987 (Continuation) Order 1989
items.description: Section 6 of the Teachers' Pay and Conditions Act 1987 provides that that Act shall expire on 31st March 1990 unless continued in force by an order made by the Secretary of State. This Order continues that Act in force for a further year.

items.title: The Health and Safety (Fees) (Amendment) Regulations 1989
items.description: This version of the The Health and Safety (Fees) (Amendment) Regulations 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Marriage Fees (Scotland) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations set out, with effect from 1st April 1990, the fees payable under sections 3(1) and 19(2) of the Marriage (Scotland) Act 1977 (fees for the preliminaries to marriage and for the solemnisation of a civil marriage respectively). The fee under section 3(1) is increased from £5.75 to £6.50, and the fee under section 19(2) is increased from £13 to £15. The Marriage Fees (Scotland) Regulations 1988 are revoked.

items.title: The Health and Medicines Act 1988 (Superannuation) (Savings for Retired Practitioners) (Scotland) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations make savings in connection with the coming into force on 1st April 1990 of section 9 of the Health and Medicines Act 1988 ( “the Act”). That section revokes regulation 75 of the National Health Service (Superannuation) (Scotland) Regulations 1980 under which further employment as a medical or dental practitioner within the National Health Service, between the ages of 65 and 70 years, is disregarded for the purpose of determining whether there should be a reduction in a pension payable under those Regulations. Regulation 2 of these Regulations, however, enables those practitioners who, in the month preceding the coming into force of section 9 of the Act, were taking advantage of regulation 75 to continue to do so as if that provision had not been revoked.

items.title: The Taunton–Fraddon Trunk Road A39 (Fairy Cross to Horns Cross Improvement) Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The Taunton–Fraddon Trunk Road A39 (Fairy Cross to Horns Cross Improvement) Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Registration of Births, Deaths and Marriages (Fees) (Scotland) Order 1989
items.description: This Order sets out, with effect from 1st April 1990, the fee payable for a certificate of birth, death or marriage issued for special purposes under certain statutory provisions. The fee is increased as at that date from £1.50 to £3.00. As a consequence, the Registration of Births, Deaths and Marriages (Fees) (Scotland) Order 1982 is revoked.

items.title: The Registration of Births, Deaths, Marriages and Divorces (Fees) (Scotland) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations prescribe, with effect from 1st April 1990, the fees payable to the Registrar General and district registrars for services provided under the Registration of Births, Deaths and Marriages (Scotland) Act 1965.

items.title: The Dartford–Thurrock Crossing Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations make provision for the regulation by the crossing operator of the use of the Dartford–Thurrock Crossing, presently consisting of the Dartford Tunnels, by vehicles exceeding the dimensions or weights prescribed in the Regulations. A charge in addition to any toll is payable to the crossing operator in respect of vehicles above a specified width or length using the tunnels.

items.title: The Export of Goods (Control) Order 1989
items.description: This Order revokes and replaces the Export of Goods (Control) Order 1987 and the subsequent amendment thereto. The changes (apart from minor or drafting changes) it effects are as follows:—

items.title: The Sutton District Water Company (Constitution and Regulation) Order 1989
items.description: Section 101(2) of the Water Act 1989 provides for the replacement of provisions relating to the constitution and regulation of a statutory water company and contained in its local Acts and Orders by provisions contained in a memorandum and articles of association which have been approved by special resolution of the members of the company. The replacement provisions have effect only if they have been approved by the Secretary of State and on such dates as he determines.

items.title: The Authorities for London Post-Graduate Teaching Hospitals Amendment Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations amend directions in regulation 6 of the London Post-Graduate Teaching Hospitals Regulations 1982 concerning functions to be exercised by the relevant special health authorities on behalf of the Secretary of State. The amendments reflect changes made by the Health and Medicines Act 1988 to the National Health ServiceAct 1977 in connection with dental services for school children and charges for private patients and overseas visitors.

items.title: The Financial Services (Designated Countries and Territories) (Overseas Insurance Companies) Order 1989
items.description: This Order designates the Commonwealth of Pennsylvania (i.e. Pennsylvania, USA) for the purposes of section 130 of the Financial Services Act 1986. The Order removes from the prohibition imposed by subsection (1) of that section on the promotion of certain contracts of insurance, rights under which constitute an investment for the purposes of that Act, the promotion of such contracts with an insurance company authorised to effect or carry out such contracts in the Commonwealth of Pennsylvania.

items.title: The A12 London – Great Yarmouth Trunk Road (Gorleston Relief Road and Slip Roads) Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The A12 London – Great Yarmouth Trunk Road (Gorleston Relief Road and Slip Roads) Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Milk and Dairies (Semi-skimmed and Skimmed Milk) (Heat Treatment and Labelling) (Amendment) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations apply to England and Wales only and come into force on 1st April 1990. They amend the Milk and Dairies (Semi-skimmed and Skimmed Milk) (Heat Treatment and Labelling) Regulations 1988.

items.title: The Milk (Special Designation) Regulations 1989
items.description: (This note is not part of the Relations)

items.title: The Foreign Fields (Specification) Order 1989
items.description: The Order specifies the Gullfaks oilfield, in that part of the continental shelf under the North Sea which is subject to Norwegian jurisdiction, as a foreign field for the purposes of the Oil Taxation Act 1983. Specification as a foreign field for the purposes of the Act has two potential effects. First, the field in question becomes a “user field” for the purposes of section 9 of the Act. A “tariff receipts allowance” is then available to participators in a United Kingdom field who are chargeable to petroleum revenue tax in respect of tariffs received by them for the use of field assets by licensees in the user field. Secondly, the licensees in a foreign field specified for the purposes of the Act are, by virtue of section 12, themselves brought within the charge to petroleum revenue tax in respect of consideration received by them which is attributable to the use by participators in United Kingdom fields of the foreign field assets.

items.title: The Valuation Roll and Valuation Notice (Scotland) Order 1989
items.description: This Order prescribes the form of the valuation roll which comes into force on or after 1st April 1990 (article 2), and the form of valuation notice to be issued by the assessor in respect of entries in such a roll (article 3 and Schedule).

items.title: The Valuation Timetable (Scotland) Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The Valuation Timetable (Scotland) Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Private Medical Insurance (Tax Relief) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations, made under sections 54(4) and 57 of the Finance Act 1989 (“the 1989 Act”), specify the cases in which, and the conditions subject to which, tax relief for payments of premiums under contracts of private medical insurance is to be given by deduction from such payments of amounts equal to income tax at the basic rate on them (“relief at source”). They provide the machinery by which the payees of premiums from which amounts have been deducted can recover those amounts from the Board by making claims and may be required by the persons paying such premiums to provide certificates of the premiums paid and amounts deducted from them.The Regulations also provide for the inspection of records kept by insurers under contracts of private medical insurance, for the provision of information by insurers and their managing agents, for the approval and withdrawal of approval of insurers by the Board and for associated rights of appeal, for the conditions for certification by the Board of standard forms of contracts for private medical insurance, and standard variations from those forms, and for the procedure for certification of contracts and standard forms and the exercise of rights of appeal against the Board’s refusal to certify or revocation of certification.

items.title: The Social Fund Cold Weather Payments (General) Amendment Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Social Fund Cold Weather Payments (General) Regulations 1988 by increasing the capital limit for persons aged 60 or over from £500 to £1000 and providing for the capital of a claimant’s partner to be taken into account for the purposes of determining the amountof a claimant’s capital (regulation 2). They also revise in part the description of areas covered by the national climatological message stations (regulation 3).

items.title: The Private Medical Insurance (Disentitlement to Tax Relief and Approved Benefits) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations are in three distinct parts. Part I contains introductory provisions which are common to Parts II and III. Part II is made under powers contained in section 54(7) and (8) of the Finance Act 1989 (“the 1989 Act”) and provides that in certain circumstances an individual making a premium payment under a contract of private medical insurance is to have his entitlement to tax relief on that payment removed, what those circumstances are, for the circumstances in which tax for which relief has been given at source may be recovered from the person to whom the payment was made and for the adaptation of section 30 of the Taxes Management Act 1970 in order to secure that recovery by way of assessment.

items.title: The Housing (Right to Buy) (Prescribed Forms) (Amendment) Regulations 1989
items.description: The right to buy is provided for in Part V of the Housing Act 1985. These Regulations make minor amendments to the form of the notice claiming to exercise the right to buy (Schedule 1 to the Housing (Right to Buy) (Prescribed Forms) Regulations 1986).

items.title: The Supply of Beer (Tied Estate) Order 1989
items.description: This Order provides that brewers, and groups of companies including brewers, which own more than two thousand licensed premises have until 31st October 1992 to dispose of either their brewery business or the excess of licensed premises or to release their ties on half the excess. (A “group” for this purpose is defined as companies related by holdings of or interests in fifteen per cent or more of voting rights.) Any person who finds himself in the position of owning a prohibited interest by the acquisition of shares after 1st May 1992 has six months to get out of that position.

items.title: The European Communities (Designation) ( No. 2) Order 1989 (revoked)
items.description: This is a further Order in the series of Orders designating the Ministers who, and departments which, may exercise powers to make regulations conferred by section 2(2) of the European Communities Act 1972 and specifying the matters in relation to which these powers may be exercised.

items.title: The Exempt Charities Order 1989
items.description: This Order declares Goldsmiths' College to be an exempt charity within the meaning of the Charities Act 1960. An exempt charity is not required to be registered with the Charity Commissioners and the Commissioners cannot exercise any of their powers under the Act of 1960 in relation to an exempt charity except at the charity’s request.

items.title: The Air Navigation (Overseas Territories) Order 1989
items.description: This Order supersedes the Air Navigation (Overseas Territories) Order 1977 (S.I. 1977/422), as amended (S.I. 1977/820, and S.I. 1978/1520).

items.title: The Brunei (Appeals) Order 1989
items.description: This Order confers jurisdiction on the Judicial Committee of the Privy Council in respect of appeals from the Supreme Court of Brunei Darussalam. Section 2(3) of the Brunei (Appeals) Act 1989 revokes the Sarawak, North Borneo and Brunei (Appeal to Privy Council) Order in Council 1951 (S.I. 1951/1949) and the Brunei (Appeal to Privy Council) Order in Council 1963 (S.I. 1963/1491) as from the date of the coming into force of this Order.

items.title: The Cayman Islands (Territorial Sea) Order 1989
items.description: This Order extends the boundaries of the Colony of the Cayman Islands so as to include, as territorial sea, the sea within twelve nautical miles of the baselines of the Cayman Islands, together with its seabed and subsoil, and makes other provisions in this connection. In particular, it defines the baseline from which the breadth of the territorial sea is measured as generally the low-water line, except where there are fringing reefs or bays.

items.title: The Continental Shelf (Designation of Areas) Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The Continental Shelf (Designation of Areas) Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Falkland Islands Courts (Overseas Jurisdiction) Order 1989
items.description: This Order confers jurisdiction on the courts of the Falkland Islands to deal with certain civil and criminal proceedings in respect of matters arising under the law of the British Antarctic Territory and South Georgia and the South Sandwich Islands.

items.title: The Home Loss Payments Order 1989
items.description: This Order, which applies to England and Wales, increases the amounts payable for home loss payments under the Land Compensation Act 1973. A person is entitled to a home loss payment when he is displaced from a dwelling by compulsory purchase or in other circumstances specified in section 29 of the 1973 Act and the payment is made by the acquiring authority or as otherwise specified in that section. Section 30 of the 1973 Act provides for the amount of a home loss payment to be calculated by a specified multiplier of the rateable value of the dwelling, subject to specified maximum and minimum amounts.

items.title: The Housing (Right to Buy Delay Procedure) (Prescribed Forms) Regulations 1989
items.description: Section 124 of the Housing Act 1988 adds sections 153A and 153B to Part V of the Housing Act 1985 (the right to buy). These sections make provision for the consequences of delay by a landlord in dealing with a tenant’s application to exercise the right to buy. These Regulations prescribe the form of notices and particulars for inclusion in notices to be served on or by a landlord under these sections.

items.title: The Merchant Shipping Act 1979 (Overseas Territories) Order 1989
items.description: This Order extends to the territories specified in Schedule 1, subject to the modifications specified in Schedules 2 and 3, sections 21 and 22 of the Merchant Shipping Act 1979 and the Merchant Shipping (Distress Signals and Prevention of Collisions) Regulations 1989. It revokes the Statutory Insruments specified in article 4 to the extent therein stated.

items.title: The Montserrat Constitution Order 1989
items.description: This Order confers a new Constitution on Montserrat. In particular it provides for a Governor appointed by Her Majesty, an Executive Council and a Legislative Council. Provision is also made regarding the fundamental rights and freedoms of the individual.

items.title: The Broadcasting Act 1981 (Isle of Man) Order 1989
items.description: This Order extends to the Isle of Man, with the adaptations and modifications specified in the Schedule, certain provisions of the Broadcasting Act 1981 (as amended), together with associated provisions of the Cable and Broadcasting Act 1984.The Orders in Council which extended provisions consolidated in the Act of 1981, or enactments repealed by such provisions, are revoked.

items.title: The Fishery Limits Act 1976 (Guernsey) Order 1989 (revoked)
items.description: This Order extends sections 2(2) to (7), 8 and 9 of and Schedules 3 and 4 to the Fishery Limits Act 1976 to the Bailiwick of Guernsey with the exceptions, adaptations and modifications specified in the Schedule to the Order.

items.title: The Insolvency Act 1986 (Guernsey) Order 1989
items.description: This Order extends certain provisions of section 426 of the Insolvency Act 1986 (co-operation between courts) to the Bailiwick of Guernsey with the modifications specified in the Schedule to the Order.

items.title: The Abolition of Domestic Rates (Domestic and Part Residential Subjects) (Scotland) Regulations 1989
items.description: Under Part I of the Abolition of Domestic Rates Etc. (Scotland) Act 1987 (“the Act”), as amended by the Local Government Finance Act 1988, rates cease to be leviable in respect of domestic subjects with effect from 1st April 1989. Domestic subjects are defined in section 2(3) of the Act, as amended.

items.title: The Merchant Shipping (Distress Signals and Prevention of Collisions) (Guernsey) Order 1989
items.description: This Order extends to the Bailiwick of Guernsey, and applies to ships registered there and to masters and seamen employed in those ships, the Merchant Shipping (Distress Signals and Prevention of Collisions) Regulations 1989 with the modifications specified in the Schedule to the Order.

items.title: The Sea Fish (Conservation) (Channel Islands) (Amendment) Order 1989
items.description: This Order amends the Sea Fish (Conservation) (Channel Islands) Order 1981, to the extent that it relates to the Bailiwick of Guernsey, so as to extend there the amendments made to the Sea Fish (Conservation) Act 1967 by the Fisheries Act 1981 and to make other minor amendments to that Order.

items.title: The Sea Fisheries (Channel Islands) (Amendment) Order 1989
items.description: This Order amends the Sea Fisheries (Channel Islands) Order 1973, to the extent that it relates to the Bailiwick of Guernsey, so as to extend there the amendments made to the Sea Fisheries Act 1968 by the Fishery Limits Act 1976 and the Fisheries Act 1981 and to make other minor amendments to tha Order.

items.title: The King George’s Fund for Sailors (Amendment of Charter) Order 1989
items.description: This Order amends the Charter of the King George’s Fund for Sailors pursuant to a Scheme made by the Charity Commission on 9th October 1989 under section 18 of the Charities Act 1960.

items.title: The Copyright (Application to Other Countries) (No. 2) (Amendment) Order 1989
items.description: This Order amends the Copyright (Application to Other Countries) (No. 2) Order 1989 to include India in the list of countries which enjoy full protection for sound recordings.

items.title: The Ministerial and other Salaries Order 1989
items.description: This Order increases salaries payable under the Ministerial and other Salaries Act 1975 to Ministers, to salaried Members of the Opposition, to the Speaker of the House of Commons and to other paid office holders. The amounts specified in this Order are the maximum salaries payable. The actual salaries in payment may therefore be below these levels.

items.title: The Friendly Societies (Modification of the Corporation Tax Acts) Regulations 1989
items.description: Section 463 of the Taxes Act 1988 provides that the Corporation Tax Acts are to apply to the non-tax exempt life or endowment business carried on by registered friendly societies in the same way as they apply to mutual life assurance business carried on by insurance companies but gives the Treasury power to provide by regulations that those Acts as so applied are to have effect subject to modifications and exceptions prescribed in the Regulations.

items.title: The Education (Higher Education Corporations) (No. 7) Order 1989
items.description: This Order makes provision for the establishment of a body corporate to conduct Gloucestershire College of Arts and Technology (Higher Education). That body which, by virtue of paragraph 1(2) of Schedule 7 to the Education Reform Act 1988 will have the name of Gloucestershire College of Arts and Technology (Higher Education), is established on 15th January 1990 to prepare itself for the conduct of that institution from 1st April 1990 which is appointed as the transfer date for the purposes of section 126 of that Act. The order also specifies Gloucestershire County Council as the local authority able to nominate one local authority nominee to become a member of that body corporate.

items.title: The Borough of Colchester (Electoral Arrangements) Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The Borough of Colchester (Electoral Arrangements) Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The City of Glasgow and Eastwood Districts (Muirend Road/Netherlee Road) Boundaries Amendment Order 1989
items.description: This Order transfers 2 areas extending in total to approximately 20 acres from the City of Glasgow District to Eastwood District and an area of approximately 20 acres from Eastwood District to the City of Glasgow District and makes consequential changes to electoral arrangements.

items.title: The Sex Discrimination Act 1975 (Exemption of Police Federation Constitutional and Electoral Arrangements) Order 1989
items.description: This Order preserves the effect of the provisions of the Police Federation Regulations 1969 listed in article 2 by disapplying section 1(1) of the Employment Act 1989 (overriding of statutory requirements which conflict with certain provisions of the Sex Discrimination Act 1975). The provisions lsted relate to the constitutional and electoral arrangements for branch boards, certain liaison committees, central conferences, the conferences arrangements committee, central committees and women’s regional conferences of the Police Federation for England and Wales. The Order also renders lawfulacts of discrimination required under those provisions (article 3).

items.title: The Customs Duties (ECSC) (Quota and Other Reliefs) Order 1989
items.description: This Order, which comes into force on 1st January 1990, provides for reliefs from customs duty (in so far as it is charged) on certain iron and steel products originating in the developing countries named in Schedule 2 to the Order in accordance with a Decision of the representatives of the governments of the Member States of the European Coal and Steel Community meeting in Council of 19th December 1989. The reliefs are provided under the Community’s Generalised Tariff Preference Scheme for Developing Countries.

items.title: The River Hamble Harbour Revision Order 1989
items.description: This Order alters the constitution of the Management Committee (a sub-committee of the Hampshire County Council) established by article 12 of the River Hamble Harbour Revision Order 1969, as that article was substituted, with amendments, by article 3 of the River Hamble Harbour Revision Order 1982. This Order would afford a right to the River Hamble Boatyard and Marina Operators' Association to appoint a member.

items.title: The Credit Unions (Increase in Limits of Shareholding, of Deposits by persons too young to be members and of Loans) Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The Credit Unions (Increase in Limits of Shareholding, of Deposits by persons too young to be members and of Loans) Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Community Charges (Notification of Deaths) Regulations 1989
items.description: This version of the The Community Charges (Notification of Deaths) Regulations 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Disabled Persons (Services, Consultation and Representation) Act 1986 (Commencement No. 5) Order 1989
items.description: This Order brings into force in England and Wales on 18th December 1989 section 11 of the Disabled Persons (Services, Consultation and Representation) Act 1986, which imposes a duty on the Secretary of State to make annual reports to Parliament containing information relating to persons suffering from mental illness or mental handicap.(This note is not part of the Order)

items.title: The County Court (Amendment No. 4) Rules 1989
items.description: This version of the The County Court (Amendment No. 4) Rules 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Rules of the Supreme Court (Amendment No. 4) 1989
items.description: This version of the The Rules of the Supreme Court (Amendment No. 4) 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Portland Harbour Fishery Order 1989
items.description: This Order confers on Portland Oystermen Limited (“the Company”) the right of several fishery for oysters, scallops and mussels in a specified area of Portland Harbour for a period of 10 years.

items.title: The Petty Sessional Divisions (North Yorkshire) Order 1989
items.description: This Order gives effect to a draft order submitted by the magistrates' courts committee for the County of North Yorkshire and provides for the abolition of the petty sessional divisions of Allertonshire, Birdforth, Bulmer East, Bulmer West, Gilling East, Hallikeld, Hang East, Hang West, Malton, Richmond and Gilling West, Ryedale and Stokesley, and for the areas comprised therein to form new divisions of Easingwold, Northallerton, Richmond and Ryedale.

items.title: The City of Glasgow and Strathkelvin Districts (Colston Road/Auchinairn Road) Boundaries Amendment Order 1989
items.description: This Order transfers an area of approximately 8 acres from the City of Glasgow District to Strathkelvin District and areas extending to approximately 43 acres from Strathkelvin District to the City of Glasgow District, and makes consequential changes to electoral arrangements.

items.title: The Veterinary Surgeons and Veterinary Practitioners (Registration) (Amendment) Regulations Order of Council 1989
items.description: The Regulations approved by this Order further amend the Veterinary Surgeons and Veterinary Practitioners Registration Regulations 1967 by increasing the fees payable for the registration and annual retention of names on the registers of the Royal College of Veterinary Surgeons. The fees were last fixed in 1985, and the average level of increase is 60 per cent.

items.title: The Merger Reference (Blue Circle, Yale and Myson) (Revocation) Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The Merger Reference (Blue Circle, Yale and Myson) (Revocation) Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: Sports Grounds and Sporting Events (Designation) (Scotland) Amendment Order 1989
items.description: This Order amends the Sports Grounds and Sporting Events (Designation) (Scotland) Order 1985 so as to substitute McDiarmid Park, Perth for Muirton Park, Perth as a sports ground designated for the purposes of Part V of the Criminal Justice (Scotland) Act 1980.

items.title: The Safety of Sports Grounds (Designation) (Scotland) Order 1989
items.description: This Order designates McDiarmid Park, Perth as a stadium which requires a safety certificate under the Safety of Sports Grounds Act 1975.

items.title: The Non-Domestic Rating Contributions (England) Regulations 1989
items.description: Under Part II of Schedule 8 to the Local Government Finance Act 1988 (c. 41), charging authorities are required to pay amounts (called non-domestic rating contributions) to the Secretary of State. Payments in respect of a provisional amount of the contributions are made during the financial year, final calculations and payments being made after the year ends. These Regulations contain rules for the calculation of those contributions.

items.title: The Non-Domestic Rates and Community Charges (Timetable) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations prescribe the date, in relation to the financial year 1990-91 and each subsequent financial year, before which a local authority is required to determine their standard community charge multiplier or multipliers. The date prescribed is 29th January in the financial year preceding each of these years.

items.title: The Standard Community Charge (Scotland) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations prescribe certain matters relating to the standard community charge under section 10 of the Abolition of Domestic Rates Etc. (Scotland) Act 1987, as amended by paragraph 19 of Schedule 12 to the Local Government Finance Act 1988 and by section 142 of the Local Government and Housing Act 1989.

items.title: The Excise Duties (Hydrocarbon Oil) (Travelling Showmen) Relief Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations, subject to certain conditions, relieve travelling showmen from the requirement to repay rebate which has been allowed on hydrocarbon oil. These Regulations replace a relief which was formerly allowed by the Commissionersby way of extra-statutory concession (published in HM Custo ms and Excise PublicNotice 748).

items.title: The Warble Fly (England and Wales) (Amendment) Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The Warble Fly (England and Wales) (Amendment) Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Non-Domestic Rating Contributions (Wales) Regulations 1989
items.description: Under Part II of Schedule 8 to the Local Government Finance Act 1988(c. 41), charging authorities are required to pay amounts (called non-domestic rating contributions) to the Secretary of State. Payments in respect of a provisional amount of the contributions are made during the financial year, final calculations and payments being made after the year ends. These Regulations contain rules for the calculation of those contributions for Welsh charging authorities.

items.title: The Medicines (Intermediate Medicated Feeding Stuffs) Order 1989
items.description: This Order specifies a class of intermediate medicated feeding stuff which is to be treated as a medicinal product for the purposes of the Medicines Act 1968. This class is any substance for use wholly or mainly as an ingredient in the preparation of a substance which is to be fed to animals for a medicinal purpose or for purposes that include that purpose.

items.title: The Undersized Crabs (Variation) Order 1989
items.description: This Order varies the Undersized Crabs Order 1986 which prescribes minimum sizes for the landing, sale and carriage of edible crabs (Cancer pagurus).

items.title: The National Savings Bank (Investment Deposits) (Limits) (Amendment) Order 1989
items.description: This Order, which further amends the National Savings Bank (Investment Deposits) (Limits) Order 1977, reduces the limit on the aggregate amount which can be accepted by the Director of Savings from any person by way of an investment deposit in the National Savings Bank from £100,000 to £25,000.

items.title: The Local Government and Housing Act 1989 (Commencement No. 3) Order 1989
items.description: Article 3 of this Order brings into force on the 16th January 1990 Part IV of the Local Government and Housing Act 1989 which relates to the revenue accounts and capital finance of local authorities (subject to the transitional provisions in Schedule 1) and the following provisions of that Act—

items.title: The Education Support Grants (Amendment) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Education Support Grants Regulations 1984 by substituting a new regulation 4 (rate of grant) and a new Schedule listing the purposes for or in connection with which grants are payable.

items.title: The Mackerel (Specified Sea Areas) (Prohibition of Fishing) Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The Mackerel (Specified Sea Areas) (Prohibition of Fishing) Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Electricity Act 1989 (Nominated Companies) (Scotland) Order 1989
items.description: This Order nominates Scottish Hydro-Electric plc, Scottish Power plc and Scottish Nuclear Limited as the companies to which the property, rights and liabilities of the Scottish Electricity Boards may be transferred in accordance with schemes made under section 67 of the Electricity Act 1989. The first two of these companies are designated as the Scottish electricity companies and the third as the Scottish nuclear company.

items.title: The Anglerfish (Specified Sea Areas) (Prohibition of Fishing) Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The Anglerfish (Specified Sea Areas) (Prohibition of Fishing) Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Misuse of Drugs (Licence Fees) (Amendment) Regulations 1989
items.description: Section 30 of the Misuse of Drugs Act 1971 provides that a licence issued by the Secretary of State for the purposes of that Act or of Regulations made under it may be issued subject to the condition of payment of a fee. The Misuse of Drugs (Licence Fees) Regulations 1986, as amended, prescribe the fee payable in relation to a licence to produce, supply, offer to supply or possess controlled drugs; the present Regulations further amend those Regulations so as to increase the fees there prescribed by approximately 3.5 per cent.

items.title: The Brunei (Appeals) Act 1989 (Commencement) Order 1989
items.description: This Order brings the Brunei (Appeals) Act 1989 into force on 1st February 1990.

items.title: The Town and Country Planning (Liverpool and Wirral Urban Development Area) Special Development Order 1989
items.description: This Order is a special development order applying to land within the Liverpool and Wirral urban development area. The functions of an urban development corporation for that area are performed by the Merseyside Development Corporation by virtue of the Liverpool and Wirral Urban Development Area Order 1988. Under section 148(1) of the Local Government, Planning and Land Act 1980 the Secretary of State, after consultation with the appropriate local planning authority and any other local authority which appears to him to be concerned, may approve proposals submitted to him by an urban development corporation for the development of land within its area. Section 148(2) of that Act provides that a special development order may grant permission for any development of land in accordance with proposals so approved. This Order grants permission for the carrying out of any development in accordance with such proposals, within the Liverpool and Wirral urban development area, in the same circumstances and subject to the same limitations and conditions as it is permitted in the Merseyside urban development area. Copies of schemes submitted by the Merseyside Development Corporation and approved by the Secretary of State will be available for inspection by the public at the offices of the development corporation.

items.title: The A30 Trunk Road (Eastern Green to Market Place, Penzance) (Detrunking) Order 1989
items.description: This version of the The A30 Trunk Road (Eastern Green to Market Place, Penzance) (Detrunking) Order 1989 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Town and Country Planning (Liverpool and Wirral Urban Development Area) Special Development Order 1989
The Export of Goods (Control) (Amendment No.2) Order 1989
items.description: This Order further amends the Export of Goods (Control) Order 1987. It controls the export of certain transceivers to India and Pakistan.

items.title: The Non-Domestic Rates (Scotland) Regulations 1989
items.description: These Regulations prescribe the non-domestic rate for the financial year 1990-91 for each local authority, listed in the Schedule (regulation 3 and the Schedule).

items.title: The Limits on Rent Increases (Housing Associations) (Scotland) Order 1989
items.description: Section 58 of the Rent (Scotland) Act 1984 provides for the phasing of the progression to a registered rent for certain housing association rents. The phasing is dependent upon the rent limits for each stage provided for under section 58(2). Section 58(7) provides that the Secretary of State may make an order varying or repealing any of the rent limit provisions in subsection (2).

items.title: The Limits on Rent Increases (Scotland) Order 1989
items.description: Section 33(1) of the Rent (Scotland) Act 1984 provides that increases in registered rents are to be limited to amounts specified by order made by the Secretary of State. Subsection (1)(a) provides for specification of the maximum amount by which the total rent payable during a specified period may be increased. Subsection (1)(b) provides for restriction of the total additional rental income which may be recovered by the landlord during a specified period.

items.title: The Heathrow Airport–London Noise Insulation Grants Scheme 1989
items.description: The Airports Policy White Paper (Cmnd 9542), published in June 1985, committed the Government to reviewing the Heathrow Airport–London Noise Insulation Grants Scheme 1980 which, as varied, applied to dwellings the construction of which was completed before 1st April 1980 and to insulation works carried out to such dwellings before 1st April 1985. That review has now been completed. The findings indicate that some small extensions are needed to the boundaries of the previous Scheme. This Scheme implements the findings of the review so as to meet the continuing need for domestic sound insulation in the vicinity of Heathrow Airport. The conditions of eligibility and the specifications for insulation works are in other respects the same as in the previous Scheme. The maximum amount of grant payable has been adjusted to take account of increased costs since 1980.

items.title: The Merseyside Residuary Body (Winding Up) Order 1989
items.description: This Order provides for the winding up of the Merseyside Residuary Body in pursuance of a scheme submitted by that Body to the Secretary of State under section 67 of the Local Government Act 1985. Articles 2 to 10 have effect on 31st January 1990 (“the transfer date”).

items.title: The British Gas plc (Rateable Values) Order 1989
items.description: Under paragraph 3(2) of Schedule 6 to the Local Government Finance Act 1988, the Secretary of State may by order provide in the case of non-domestic hereditaments to be shown in the central rating lists for England and Wales that the normal rules of valuation for rating contained in paragraphs 2 to 2B of that Schedule shall not apply, and instead their rateable value shall be such as is specified, or determined in accordance with rules set out, in the order.

items.title: The British Waterways Board (Rateable Values) Order 1989
items.description: Under paragraph 3(2) of Schedule 6 to the Local Government Finance Act 1988, the Secretary of State may by order provide in the case of non-domestic hereditaments to be shown in the central rating lists for England and Wales that the normal rules of valuation for rating contained in paragraphs 2 to 2B of that Schedule shall not apply, and instead their rateable value shall be such as is specified, or determined in accordance with rules set out, in the order.

items.title: The Docks and Harbours (Rateable Values) Order 1989
items.description: Where the Secretary of State so provides by order under paragraph 3(1) of Schedule 6 to the Local Government Finance Act 1988, hereditaments of any class are not to be valued for non-domestic rating on and after 1st April 1990 on the basis set out in paragraphs 2 to 2B of that Schedule (that is to say, by reference to the rent a hypothetical tenant would pay for the hereditament on an annual basis), but on the basis of rules set out in the order.

items.title: The Electricity Generators (Rateable Values) Order 1989
items.description: By virtue of section 3(1) of Schedule 6 to the Local Government Finance Act 1988, where the Secretary of State so provides by order, hereditaments of a description to be prescribed in the order are not to be valued for non-domestic rating on and after 1st April 1990 on the basis set out in paragraphs 2 to 2B of that Schedule (that is to say, by reference to the rent a hypothetical tenant would pay for the hereditament on an annual basis), but on the basis of prescribed rules.

items.title: The Electricity Supply Industry (Rateable Values) Order 1989
items.description: Under paragraph 3(2) of Schedule 6 to the Local Government Finance Act 1988, the Secretary of State may by order provide in the case of non-domestic hereditaments to be shown in the central rating lists for England and Wales that the normal rules of valuation for rating contained in paragraphs 2 o 2B of that Schedule shall not apply, and instead their rateable value shall be such as is specified, or determined in accordance with rules set out, in the order.

items.title: The Non-Domestic Rating (Transitional Period) (Appropriate Fraction) Order 1989
items.description: The Local Government Finance Act 1988 introduces with effect from 1st April 1990 a new business rate, which replaces the general rate insofar as concerns business property. Schedule 7A to the Act provides for the phasing of the gains or losses occasioned by this and by the revaluation of business property for 1990.
